He also became the oldest player in NBA history to record at least 25 points in ten straight games, doing so at 37 years old.{{cite magazine |last=Smith |first=Brook |date=January 8, 2022 |title=Lakers: This LeBron James Stat Proves He is Beating Father Time |url=https://www.si.com/nba/lakers/news/lakers-this-lebron-james-stat-proves-he-is-beating-father-time-bas2121 in fan votes with his 18th All-Star selection, tying Bryant and just one behind Abdul-Jabbar;{{cite magazine |last=Eulau |first=Eric |date=January 21, 2022 |url=https://www.si.com/nba/lakers/news/lakers-lebron-james-officially-leads-all-players-in-all-star-voting-ee21 |title=Lakers: LeBron James Officially Leads All Players in All-Star Voting 2022|url=https://usatoday.com/story/sports/nba/lakers/2022/04/08/lebron-james-miss-lakers-last-two-games-scoring-title/9513769002/|title=Lakers' LeBron James to miss final two games with ankle injury, loses out on scoring title|newspaper=USA Today|access-date=April 10, 2022|archive-date=April 9, 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220409092535/https://www.usatoday.com/story/sports/nba/lakers/2022/04/08/lebron-james-miss-lakers-last-two-games-scoring-title/9513769002/|url-status=live}} aged 37, James would have broken Jordan's record of oldest scoring leader at 34.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Walsh|first=Erin|date=April 8, 2022|url=https://www.cbssports.com/nba/news/lebron-james-ends-bid-for-historic-scoring-title-lakers-rule-him-out-for-rest-of-season-to-rehab-ankle-injury/|title=LeBron James ends bid for historic scoring title; Lakers rule him out for rest of season to rehab ankle injury|website=CBS Sports|access-date=April 10, 2022|archive-date=April 10, 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220410040607/https://www.cbssports.com/nba/news/lebron-james-ends-bid-for-historic-scoring-title-lakers-rule-him-out-for-rest-of-season-to-rehab-ankle-injury/|url-status=live}} For ''[[The Athletic]]'', Bill Oram wrote that James' ankle injury felt like "the moment that might signal the end of the Lakers season".{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Oram|first=Bill|date=March 28, 2022|url=https://theathletic.com/3213293/2022/03/28/oram-lebron-james-injury-in-loss-to-pelicans-could-be-the-biggest-lakers-collapse-of-all/|title=Oram: LeBron James' injury in loss to Pelicans could be the biggest Lakers collapse of all|website=The Athletic|access-date=April 10, 2022|archive-date=April 1, 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220401131358/https://theathletic.com/3213293/2022/03/28/oram-lebron-james-injury-in-loss-to-pelicans-could-be-the-biggest-lakers-collapse-of-all/|url-status=live}}{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Adams|first=Luke|date=March 28, 2022|url=https://www.hoopsrumors.com/2022/03/lebron-james-ankle-injury-feels-horrible.html|title=LeBron James: Ankle Injury Feels 'Horrible'|website=Hoops Rumors|access-date=April 10, 2022|archive-date=April 3, 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220403202454/https://www.hoopsrumors.com/2022/03/lebron-james-ankle-injury-feels-horrible.html|url-status=live}} On April 5, the Lakers were eliminated from both playoff and play-in contention for the first time since [[2018–19 Los Angeles Lakers season|2019]] (James' first season with the Lakers) after a 121–110 loss to the [[2021–22 Phoenix Suns season|Phoenix Suns]].{{#invoke:cite|web|last=McMenamin|first=Dave|date=April 6, 2022|url=https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/33680640/los-angeles-lakers-eliminated-playoff-contention-loss-phoenix-suns-win-san-antonio-spurs|title=Los Angeles Lakers eliminated from playoff contention after 7th straight loss|website=ESPN.com|access-date=April 6, 2022|archive-date=April 6, 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220406051504/https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/33680640/los-angeles-lakers-eliminated-playoff-contention-loss-phoenix-suns-win-san-antonio-spurs|url-status=live}} It marked the fourth time in James' career that he missed the playoffs.{{cite magazine |last=Coleman |first=Madeline |date=April 6, 2022 |url=https://www.si.com/nba/2022/04/06/lakers-eliminated-from-playoff-contention-suns-loss |title=Lakers Eliminated From Playoff Contention With Loss to Suns |magazine=Sports Illustrated |access-date=April 10, 2022}} James was ruled out the rest of the season due to soreness in his left ankle.{{#invoke:cite|web|date=April 8, 2022 |title=Lakers' LeBron James to miss rest of season |url=https://theathletic.com/news/lakers-lebron-james-out-ankle-injury/k6ezPp8Gd4kx/ |access-date=April 10, 2022 |website=The Athletic}} On May 24, James was named to the All-NBA Third Team for the second time; it was his 18th consecutive All-NBA Team selection, extending the record for most selections in NBA history.{{#invoke:cite|web|url=https://www.nba.com/news/antetokounmpo-jokic-doncic-2021-22-kia-all-nba-first-team|title=Giannis Antetokounmpo, Nikola Jokic, Luka Doncic lead 2021–22 Kia All-NBA 1st Team|website=NBA.com|date=May 24, 2022|access-date=May 24, 2022}} ==== 2022–2023: All-time scoring record ==== On August 18, 2022, James signed a two-year, $97.1 million contract extension with the Lakers, which had a player option in the second year.{{#invoke:cite|web|date=August 18, 2022 |title=Lakers Sign LeBron James to Contract Extension |url=https://www.nba.com/lakers/news/lakers-sign-lebron-james-to-contract-extension-220818 |access-date=August 20, 2022 |website=NBA.com |language=en}}{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Wojnarowski |first=Adrian |date=August 17, 2022 |title=LeBron lands 2-year, $97.1M deal with Lakers |url=https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/34413561/lebron-james-los-angeles-lakers-agree-2-year-971-million-extension-includes-3rd-year-player-option |access-date=August 20, 2022 |website=ESPN.com |language=en}} The contract extension made him the highest-paid athlete in NBA history at $529 million, surpassing Kevin Durant in all-time earnings.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Winters |first=Mike |title=Already a billionaire, LeBron James' 2-year contract extension makes him the NBA's all-time highest-paid player |url=https://www.cnbc.com/2022/08/18/lebron-james-becomes-highest-paid-player-in-nba-history.html |access-date=August 20, 2022 |website=CNBC |date=August 18, 2022 |language=en}} On October 20, James made his 2,144th three-pointer in a game against the Los Angeles Clippers, surpassing Paul Pierce for tenth in all-time three-pointers made.{{#invoke:cite|web|url=https://clutchpoints.com/lakers-news-lebron-james-eclipses-paul-pierce-for-nba-history-to-pile-more-on-goat-resume/|title=LeBron James eclipses Paul Pierce for NBA history to pile more on GOAT resume|website=ClutchPoints|last=Villas|first=Rexwell|date=October 20, 2022|access-date=October 20, 2022}} On October 28, James posted 28 points, seven rebounds, five assists, and four steals in an 111–102 loss to the Minnesota Timberwolves. He recorded his 1,135th career 20-point game, passing Karl Malone for the most such games in NBA history.{{#invoke:cite|web|title=Gobert, Wolves extend Lakers' winless start with 111–102 win |url=https://africa.espn.com/nba/recap/_/gameId/401468231 |date=October 28, 2022|access-date=October 29, 2022|website=ESPN.com |language=en}} On December 2, in a 133–129 victory over the [[Milwaukee Bucks]], James recorded 28 points and 11 assists. He recorded his 10,142nd assist, surpassing Johnson for sixth place on the NBA all-time career assists list.{{#invoke:cite|web|url=https://clutchpoints.com/lakers-news-lebron-james-surpasses-magic-johnson-with-latest-all-time-nba-milestone|title=Lakers star LeBron James surpasses Magic Johnson with latest all-time NBA milestone|website=ClutchPoints|last=Songco|first=Paolo|date=December 2, 2022|access-date=December 2, 2022}} On December 13, James scored 33 points in a 122–118 overtime loss to the Celtics, surpassing [[Wilt Chamberlain]] for the second-most 30-point games in NBA history. James recorded 516 30-point games in 1,386 career appearances.{{#invoke:cite|web|title=LeBron James passes Wilt Chamberlain for 2nd-most 30-point games in NBA history |url=https://www.nba.com/news/lebron-james-passes-wilt-chamberlain-for-2nd-most-30-point-games-in-nba-history |access-date=December 14, 2022 |website=NBA.com |language=en}} On December 28, he played in his 1,393rd game, breaking a tie with [[Tim Duncan]] for 10th-most in NBA history.{{#invoke:cite|web|title=Heat get past Lakers 112–98, climb over .500 at 18–17|url=https://www.espn.com/nba/recap/_/gameId/401468673 |date=December 28, 2022|access-date=December 30, 2022|website=ESPN.com |language=en}} The next game, on his 38th birthday, James had a then season-high 47 points on 18-for-27 shooting from the field, along with 10 rebounds and nine assists in a 130–121 come-from-behind victory over the [[Atlanta Hawks]].{{#invoke:cite|web|title=Lakers laud 'amazing' LeBron James after 47-point birthday outing|url=https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/35351037/lebron-james-season-high-47-38th-birthday-lakers-beat-hawks |date=December 30, 2022|access-date=December 31, 2022|website=ESPN.com |language=en}} On January 9, 2023, James received his 66th [[NBA Player of the Month and Week|Player of the Week]] award, doubling the career total of runner-up Bryant.{{#invoke:cite|web|title=LeBron James, Donovan Mitchell named NBA Players of the Week |url=https://www.nba.com/news/nba-players-week-lebron-james-donovan-mitchell |access-date=January 9, 2023|website=nba.com}} Four days later, he became the second player in NBA history to reach 38,000 points.{{#invoke:cite|web|title=LeBron James becomes second player in NBA history to score 38,000 career points as Lakers star eyes record |url=https://www.cbssports.com/nba/news/lebron-james-becomes-second-player-in-nba-history-to-score-38000-career-points-as-lakers-star-eyes-record/ |access-date=January 16, 2023 |website=NBA.com |date=January 16, 2023 |language=en}} On January 16, James had a season high 48 points, eight rebounds and nine assists in a 140–132 victory over the [[Houston Rockets]]. It was his 100th career game with 40 or more points – including the postseason.{{#invoke:cite|web|title=LeBron drops 48, Lakers beat Rockets 140–132 to snap skid|url=https://www.espn.com/nba/recap/_/gameId/401468819 |date=January 16, 2023|access-date=January 18, 2023|website=ESPN.com |language=en}} On January 23, James scored 46 points, including a career-high nine 3-pointers, in a 133–115 loss to the Los Angeles Clippers, becoming the first player in NBA history to score at least 40 points in a game against all NBA franchises.{{#invoke:cite|web|date=January 24, 2023 |title=LeBron scores 46 points with 9 3s, but Clippers rout Lakers |url=https://www.espn.com/nba/recap?gameId=401468874 |access-date=January 25, 2023 |website=ESPN.com |language=en}}{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Camras |first=Noah |title=Lakers News: LeBron James Makes Scoring History Tuesday Against Clippers |url=https://www.si.com/nba/lakers/news/lakers-news-lebron-james-makes-scoring-history-tuesday-against-clippers-nc2000 |access-date=January 25, 2023 |website=All Lakers {{!}} News, Rumors, Videos, Schedule, Roster, Salaries And More |date=January 25, 2023 |language=en}} On January 19, James was named as a starter at the [[2023 NBA All-Star Game]], tying Abdul-Jabbar's record for the most All-Star selections (19). It was also James' 19th consecutive All-Star appearance, surpassing Bryant's previous mark.{{#invoke:cite|web|url=https://clutchpoints.com/lakers-news-lebron-james-surpasses-kobe-bryant-in-history-amid-nba-all-star-starter-not|title=LeBron James surpasses Kobe Bryant in history amid NBA All-Star starter nod|website=ClutchPoints|last=Yapkowitz|first=David|date=January 26, 2023|access-date=January 26, 2023}} On January 31, James put up a triple-double with 28 points, 10 rebounds, and 11 assists in a 129–123 overtime victory over the New York Knicks, becoming the first player in NBA history to put up a triple-double in his 20th season. He also surpassed [[Mark Jackson]] and Steve Nash for fourth on the NBA all-time career assists list.{{#invoke:cite|web|url=https://clutchpoints.com/lakers-news-lebron-james-epic-performance-vs-knicks-reaches-heights-never-seen-nba-history|title=LeBron James' epic performance for Lakers vs. Knicks reaches heights never seen in NBA history|website=ClutchPoints|last=Guinhawa|first=Angelo|date=January 31, 2023|access-date=January 23, 2023}}{{#invoke:cite|web|url=https://clutchpoints.com/lakers-news-lebron-james-drops-steve-nash-from-top-4-of-assist-list-amid-scoring-chase|title=Lakers star LeBron James drops Steve Nash from Top 4 of assist list amid scoring chase|website=ClutchPoints|last=Guinhawa|first=Angelo|date=January 31, 2023|access-date=January 31, 2023}} On February 7, 2023, James passed Abdul-Jabbar as the [[List of National Basketball Association career scoring leaders|all-time leading scorer]] in NBA history.{{cite web |url=https://clutchpoints.com/lakers-news-lebron-james-passes-fellow-lakers-legend-kareem-abdul-jabbar-for-most-points-in-nba-history |title=LeBron James passes fellow Lakers legend Kareem Abdul-Jabbar for most points in NBA history |website=ClutchPoints |last=Corvo |first=Michael |date=February 7, 2023 |access-date=February 7, 2023}} Abdul-Jabbar had previously set the record on April 5, 1984, eight months before James was born.{{#invoke:cite|web|title=Top Moments: Kareem Abdul-Jabbar sets career scoring record |url=https://www.nba.com/news/history-top-moments-kareem-record |access-date=February 26, 2023 |website=NBA.com |language=en}} After James broke the record, the NBA stopped the game with 10.9 seconds left in the third quarter for an on-court ceremony. During the ceremony, he received the game ball from Abdul-Jabbar.{{#invoke:cite|web|title=Watch: Kareem passes basketball to LeBron after breaking record |url=https://www.nbcsports.com/chicago/bulls/watch-kareem-abdul-jabbar-passes-basketball-lebron-james-after-breaking-record |access-date=February 21, 2023 |website=RSN |date=February 8, 2023 |language=en}} James also gave a speech and then embraced Abdul-Jabbar, as well as his family.{{#invoke:cite|web|url=https://www.npr.org/2023/02/07/1152839238/lebron-james-nba-scoring-record-kareem-abdul-jabbar|title=LeBron James breaks the NBA career scoring record, passing Kareem Abdul-Jabbar|work=NPR|date=February 7, 2023|accessdate=February 7, 2023}}{{#invoke:cite|web| url=https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/35611846/lakers-lebron-james-passes-kareem-abdul-jabbar-become-nba-all-scoring-leader| title=LeBron James breaks Kareem Abdul-Jabbar's NBA scoring record| work=ESPN| date=February 7, 2023| accessdate=February 8, 2023}} James missed the following three games due to left ankle soreness.{{#invoke:cite|web|first=Dave |last=McMenamin|title=Sources: LeBron James feared out several weeks with foot injury |date=February 27, 2023|work=ESPN|url=https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/35749430/sources-lebron-james-feared-several-weeks-foot-injury|access-date=February 28, 2023}}{{#invoke:cite|web|first=Broderick|last=Turner|title=LeBron James is out tonight at Portland, expected back Wednesday|date=February 12, 2023|work=Los Angeles Times|url=https://www.latimes.com/sports/lakers/story/2023-02-12/lebron-james-questionable-lakers-at-trail-blazers|access-date=February 28, 2023}} On February 19, Team LeBron lost the 2023 NBA All-Star Game 184–175 to Team Giannis, marking the first time that James lost an All-Star game as a captain.{{Efn|James and [[Giannis Antetokounmpo]] were captains for the [[2023 NBA All-Star Game]].{{cite web |title=2023 All-Star Draft & All-Star Game: Format, explainer and FAQ's |url=https://www.nba.com/news/2023-all-star-draft-all-star-game-explainer |website=NBA.com |access-date=February 3, 2023 |date=February 6, 2023}}}} Prior to the game, he had been undefeated (5–0) as a captain, and since the format was changed in 2018.{{#invoke:cite|web|title=2023 NBA All-Star Game score: Jayson Tatum breaks scoring record, wins MVP; LeBron James' streak ends |url=https://www.cbssports.com/nba/news/2023-nba-all-star-game-score-jayson-tatum-breaks-scoring-record-wins-mvp-lebron-james-streak-ends/live/ |access-date=February 21, 2023 |website=www.cbssports.com |date=February 20, 2023 }} During the game, James suffered a right hand contusion and was ruled out for the entire 2nd half.{{#invoke:cite|news|url=https://www.si.com/fannation/nba/fastbreak/injuries/los-angeles-lakers-lebron-james-hand-injury-in-2023-nba-all-star-game-video|title=VIDEO: LeBron James Appeared To Get Injured During NBA All-Star Game On This Play|first=Brett|last=Siegel|newspaper=Fastbreak on Fannation |date=February 19, 2023|access-date=April 12, 2023}} On February 26, James sustained a right foot injury during a game against the [[Dallas Mavericks]]. Despite the injury, he was able to finish the game and helped the Lakers pull off the biggest comeback victory of the season at the time after trailing by 27 points late in the first half. He scored 26 points on 10-for-23 shooting, eight rebounds, and three assists in 37 minutes.{{#invoke:cite|web|last1=McMenamin |first1=Dave |title=LeBron James to monitor right foot after injury against Mavs |url=https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/35743907/lebron-james-monitor-right-foot-injury-mavs |website=[[ESPN]] |date=February 27, 2023 |access-date=March 26, 2023}} However, following an examination by team medical staff on March 2, James was diagnosed with a tendon injury in his right foot and would be reevaluated in three weeks.{{#invoke:cite|web|last1=McMenamin |first1=Dave |title=LeBron James to have foot tendon injury reevaluated in 3 weeks |url=https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/35769531/lebron-james-foot-tendon-injury-reevaluated-3-weeks |website=[[ESPN]] |date=March 2, 2023 |access-date=March 26, 2023}} After missing the next 13 games, James made his return on March 26 against the [[Chicago Bulls]]. He came off the bench for just the second time in his career; the other being in 2007.{{#invoke:cite|web|last1=Greenawalt |first1=Tyler |title=LeBron James to return today vs. Bulls after 13-game absence |date=March 26, 2023 |url=https://sports.yahoo.com/lebron-james-to-return-today-vs-bulls-after-13-game-absence-185657919.html |publisher=Yahoo Sports |access-date=March 26, 2023}}{{#invoke:cite|web|title=Lakers' LeBron James: Will come off the bench Sunday |url=https://www.cbssports.com/fantasy/basketball/news/lakers-lebron-james-will-come-off-the-bench-sunday/ |work=CBS Sports |date=March 26, 2023 |access-date=March 26, 2023}} On April 2, James put up a triple-double with 18 points, 10 rebounds, and 11 assists in a 134–109 victory over the [[Houston Rockets]]. He also tied [[Jason Kidd]] for fourth place on the all-time career triple-doubles list.{{#invoke:cite|web|url=https://clutchpoints.com/lakers-news-lebron-james-notches-historic-milestone-vs-rockets|title=Lakers star LeBron James notches another historic milestone in Rockets demolition job|website=ClutchPoints|last=Songco|first=Paolo|date=April 2, 2023|access-date=April 2, 2023}} Two days later, James put up 37 points, five rebounds, six assists, a steal, a block, and a game-winning layup in a 135–133 overtime victory over the [[Utah Jazz]].{{#invoke:cite|web|url=https://clutchpoints.com/lakers-news-lebron-james-ties-kobe-bryant-with-epic-clutch-gene-stat-thatll-leave-haters-in-the-wind|title=Lakers star LeBron James ties Kobe Bryant with epic clutch gene stat that'll leave haters in the wind|website=ClutchPoints|last=Guinhawa|first=Angelo|date=April 4, 2023|access-date=April 4, 2023}} On April 9, in the final game of the [[2022–23 NBA season|2022–23 season]], James posted 36 points, six rebounds, and six assists to lead the Lakers to a 128–117 victory over the Jazz, earning the seventh seed in the Western Conference play-in tournament.{{#invoke:cite|web|title=Lakers hold off Jazz 128–117, earn play-in date with Wolves |url=https://www.espn.com/nba/recap/_/gameId/401469383 |website=ESPN |access-date=April 10, 2023 |date=April 9, 2023}} James finished the regular season with averages of 28.9 points per game, 8.3 rebounds per game, 6.8 assists per game, becoming the oldest player in NBA history to average at least 25 points, five rebounds, and five assists in a season.{{#invoke:cite|web|url=https://clutchpoints.com/lakers-news-lebron-james-completes-historic-feat-never-seen-before-as-year-20-comes-to-an-end|title=LeBron James completes historic feat never seen before as year 20 comes to an end|website=ClutchPoints|last=Yapkowitz|first=David|date=May 23, 2023|access-date=May 23, 2023}} In Game 4 of the Lakers' first-round series against the [[Memphis Grizzlies]], James had 22 points, a playoff career-high 20 rebounds, and seven assists in a 117–111 overtime victory, marking his first 20–20 game of his career.{{#invoke:cite|web|title=LeBron leads Lakers past Grizzlies 117–111 in OT for 3–1 lead |url=https://www.espn.com/nba/recap/_/gameId/401541184 |website=ESPN |access-date=April 25, 2023 |date=April 24, 2023}} He became the first Lakers player with a 20–20 game in the postseason since [[Shaquille O'Neal]] in Game 4 of the [[2004 NBA Finals]] and also the oldest player in league history with at least 20 points and 20 rebounds in a playoff game.{{#invoke:cite|web|title=Lakers' LeBron James on 1st Career 20–20 Game: 'That's Pretty Cool, I Guess' |url=https://bleacherreport.com/articles/10073708-lakers-lebron-james-on-1st-career-20-20-game-thats-pretty-cool-i-guess |website=Bleacher Report |last=Wells |first=Adam |access-date=April 27, 2023 |date=April 25, 2023}} In Game 6 of the Western Conference semifinals, James put up 30 points, nine rebounds, nine assists, two steals, and a block in a 122–101 victory over the defending champion [[Golden State Warriors]] to lead the Lakers to the 2023 Western Conference finals, their second Conference finals appearance in five seasons. He also won his 41st playoff series, surpassing [[Derek Fisher]] for the most all-time playoff series wins in NBA history.{{#invoke:cite|web|url=https://clutchpoints.com/lakers-news-lebron-anthony-davis-share-emotional-moment-warriors-win|title=WATCH: Lakers stars LeBron James, Anthony Davis share emotional moment after beating Warriors|website=ClutchPoints|last=Songco|first=Paolo|date=May 12, 2023|access-date=May 12, 2023}}{{#invoke:cite|web|url=https://clutchpoints.com/lakers-news-lebron-james-passes-la-icon-for-playoff-record-and-it-isnt-kobe-bryant-or-kareem-abdul-jabbar|title=LeBron James passes Lakers icon for playoff record, and it isn't Kobe Bryant or Kareem Abdul-Jabbar|website=ClutchPoints|last=Guinhawa|first=Angelo|date=May 12, 2023|access-date=May 12, 2023}} In the Western Conference finals, the Lakers were swept by the [[2023 NBA Finals|eventual NBA champion]] [[Denver Nuggets]]; James set his personal playoff record with 31 points in the first half.{{#invoke:cite|web|url=https://www.espn.com/nba/recap/_/gameId/401547684|title=Jokic leads Denver Nuggets past LeBron's Lakers 113–111, into their first NBA Finals|website=ESPN.com.|date=May 22, 2023|accessdate=May 24, 2023}} ====2023–2024: NBA Cup MVP and 40K points==== [[File:LeBron James 23.jpg|thumb|James in 2023]] Entering the [[2023–24 NBA season|2023–24 season]], James was the oldest player in the league, following the retirements of [[Udonis Haslem]] and Andre Iguodala.{{Cite web |last=Reynolds |first=Tim |date=October 20, 2023 |url=https://www.nba.com/news/lebron-james-numbers-season-21 |title=Inside the numbers: LeBron James is now the NBA's oldest player |agency=Associated Press |website=NBA.com |access-date=October 21, 2023}} James switched back to jersey No. 23 from No. 6 in honor of [[Bill Russell]], who had died the previous year and whose No. 6 jersey had been retired league-wide.{{Cite web |date=2023-07-16 |title=LeBron to go back to No. 23 in honor of Russell |url=https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/38016718/lebron-james-change-jersey-number-back-23-honor-bill-russell |access-date=2025-06-09 |website=ESPN.com |language=en}} In the season opener against the defending champion Nuggets, James put up 21 points, eight rebounds and five assists in a 119–107 loss.{{Cite web |last=Quinn |first=Sam |date=October 25, 2023 |title=Lakers vs. Nuggets score, takeaways: Nikola Jokic's triple-double leads Denver past LeBron, L.A. in opener |url=https://www.cbssports.com/nba/news/lakers-vs-nuggets-score-takeaways-nikola-jokics-triple-double-leads-denver-past-lebron-l-a-in-opener/live/ |access-date=October 25, 2023 |website=CBSSports.com |language=en}} On November 1, James put up 35 points in a 130–125 overtime victory over the Los Angeles Clippers. His 35 points were his 81st 30-point game since turning 35, surpassing [[Karl Malone]] for the most 30-point games by any player since turning 35 in NBA history.{{Cite web |url=https://clutchpoints.com/lakers-news-lebron-james-breaks-karl-malone-old-man-feat-wild-ot-win-clippers |title=Lakers: LeBron James breaks Karl Malone's old-man feat in wild OT win vs. Clippers |website=ClutchPoints |last=Villas |first=Rexwell |date=November 1, 2023 |access-date=November 1, 2023}} On November 15, James logged 28 points, 10 rebounds and 11 assists in a 125–110 loss to the Sacramento Kings, becoming the second-oldest player to have recorded a triple-double. He also moved up to the fifth and eighth places (passing Jason Kidd and [[Jason Terry]], respectively) in the all-time triple-doubles and three-pointers lists, respectively.{{Cite web |last=Helin |first=Kurt |url=https://www.nbcsports.com/nba/news/watch-lebron-pass-jason-kidd-for-fifth-all-time-in-triple-doubles |title=Watch LeBron pass Jason Kidd for fifth all-time in triple-doubles |publisher=[[NBC Sports]] |date=November 16, 2023 |access-date=May 18, 2024}}{{Cite web |url=https://www.foxsports.com/stories/nba/kings-overcome-lebrons-tripledouble-hold-on-in-4th-quarter-for-125110-win-over-lakers |title=Kings overcome LeBron's triple-double, hold on in 4th quarter for 125–110 win over Lakers |website=Fox Sports |date=November 16, 2023 |access-date=November 16, 2023}}{{Cite magazine |last=Stinar |first=Ben |url=https://www.si.com/fannation/nba/fastbreak/news/breaking-lebron-james-made-nba-history-in-kings-lakers-game |title=LeBron James Made Exciting NBA History In Kings-Lakers Game |magazine=Sports Illustrated |date=November 15, 2023 |access-date=November 16, 2023}} Four days later, James scored a season-high 37 points in a narrow 105–104 win over the Houston Rockets. He also tied [[Vince Carter]] for seventh on the all-time three-pointers made list with 2,290, and moved past [[Clyde Drexler]] for eighth on the all-time steals list at 2,208.{{Cite web |last=Reedy |first=Joe |url=https://apnews.com/article/rockets-lakers-e95e43d759cb81d3de96bf131b9345e6 |title=James scores season-high 37, hits go-ahead free throw as Lakers hold off Rockets 105–104 |work=Associated Press News |date=November 20, 2023 |access-date=November 20, 2023}} In November, James became the first player in NBA history to reach 5,000 career turnovers.{{cite web | last=Quinn | first=Sam | title=WATCH: LeBron James becomes first player in NBA history to reach 5,000 turnovers | website=CBSSports.com | date=2023-11-11 | url=https://www.cbssports.com/nba/news/lebron-james-becomes-first-player-in-nba-history-to-reach-5000-turnovers/ | access-date=2025-06-21}} On November 21, in a 131–99 blowout victory over the [[Utah Jazz]], he became the first player in league history to reach 39,000 career points.{{Cite web |url=https://www.nba.com/news/lebron-james-39000-career-points |title=LeBron James becomes 1st player in NBA history to reach 39,000 career points |website=NBA.com |date=November 21, 2023 |access-date=November 22, 2023}} Six days later, in a 138–94 blowout loss to the Philadelphia 76ers, James surpassed Abdul-Jabbar's record of 66,300 minutes to become the player with the most minutes played in NBA games during the regular season and playoffs.{{Cite web |last=Rosa |first=Francisco |title=Lakers' LeBron James Passes Kareem Abdul-Jabbar to Set NBA Minutes Record |url=https://bleacherreport.com/articles/10098909-lakers-lebron-james-passes-kareem-abdul-jabbar-to-set-nba-minutes-record |access-date=November 28, 2023 |website=Bleacher Report |language=en}} The Lakers' 44-point loss was also the worst loss of James' career.{{Cite web |url=https://www.cbssports.com/nba/news/lebron-james-suffers-worst-defeat-in-nba-career-as-embiid-76ers-rout-lakers-by-44-points/ |title=LeBron James suffers worst defeat in NBA career as Embiid, 76ers rout Lakers by 44 points |agency=Associated Press |publisher=CBS News |date=November 28, 2023 |access-date=November 28, 2023}} On December 9, James and the Lakers won the [[2023 NBA In-Season Tournament|inaugural NBA In-Season Tournament]] with a 123–109 victory over the Pacers, with James receiving the Tournament MVP award and being selected to the All-Tournament team.{{Cite web |last=Bontemps |first=Tim |date=December 9, 2023 |title=Lakers win NBA Cup; LeBron James earns in-season tournament MVP |url=https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/39080368/lakers-win-nba-cup-lebron-james-earns-season-tournament-mvp |access-date=November 28, 2023 |website=ESPN |language=en}} On January 25, 2024, James was named an All-Star starter for the [[2024 NBA All-Star Game]], marking his 20th NBA All-Star selection, surpassing Abdul-Jabbar for the most All-Star selections in NBA history.{{cite web |url=https://clutchpoints.com/nba-all-star-game-2024-starters-lebron-james-giannis-antetokounmpo |title=2024 NBA All-Star Game starters: LeBron James earns league-record 20th appearance |website=ClutchPoints |last=Siegel |first=Brett |date=January 25, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240127045124/https://clutchpoints.com/nba-all-star-game-2024-starters-lebron-james-giannis-antetokounmpo |archive-date=January 27, 2024 |url-status=live |access-date=January 25, 2024}} Two days later, James put up a triple-double with 36 points, a career-high 20 rebounds, and 12 assists, including a pair of game-winning free throws, in a narrow 145–144 double overtime victory over the Golden State Warriors,{{Cite web |url=https://www.reuters.com/sports/basketball/lebron-james-triple-double-lifts-lakers-over-warriors-2ot-2024-01-28/ |title=LeBron James' triple-double lifts Lakers over Warriors in 2OT |work=[[Reuters]] |date=January 28, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240128111641/https://www.reuters.com/sports/basketball/lebron-james-triple-double-lifts-lakers-over-warriors-2ot-2024-01-28/ |archive-date=January 28, 2024 |url-status=live |access-date=January 28, 2024}} becoming the first player in Lakers franchise history since Abdul-Jabbar in 1976 to put up at least 30 points, 20 rebounds, and 10 assists in a game.{{cite web |url=https://clutchpoints.com/lakers-news-lebron-james-la-2ot-classic-vs-warriors-has-fans-calling-it-game-of-the-year |title=LeBron James, Lakers' 2OT classic vs. Warriors has fans calling it game of the year |website=ClutchPoints |last=Guinhawa |first=Angelo |date=January 27, 2024 |access-date=January 27, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240128111832/https://clutchpoints.com/lakers-news-lebron-james-la-2ot-classic-vs-warriors-has-fans-calling-it-game-of-the-year |archive-date=January 28, 2024 |url-status=live}} On February 28, James spearheaded a 21-point fourth-quarter comeback by the Lakers to beat the Los Angeles Clippers 116–112, marking the largest fourth-quarter comeback of James' career and the Lakers' biggest comeback since 2003.{{Cite web |last=Botkin |first=Brad |url=https://www.cbssports.com/nba/news/lebron-james-single-handedly-outscores-clippers-in-fourth-quarter-as-lakers-come-back-from-21-point-deficit/ |title=LeBron James single-handedly outscores Clippers in fourth quarter as Lakers come back from 21-point deficit |work=[[CBS Sports]] |date=March 1, 2024 |access-date=March 4, 2024}} He outscored the Clippers by himself 19–16 in the final quarter, going 5-for-8 from three-point range and dishing out four assists.{{cite web |last1=McMenamin |first1=Dave |title=LeBron James powers biggest 4th-quarter comeback of career |url=https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/39621969/lebron-james-powers-biggest-4th-quarter-comeback-career |website=ESPN.com |date=February 29, 2024 |access-date=February 29, 2024}} Three days later, James became the first player in NBA history to reach 40,000 career points.{{Cite web |last=Woike |first=Dan |url=https://www.latimes.com/sports/lakers/story/2024-03-02/lebron-james-40000-points-milestone-lakers-nuggets |title=LeBron James becomes first NBA player to score 40,000 points in Lakers' loss to Nuggets |work=[[Los Angeles Times]] |date=March 2, 2024 |access-date=November 7, 2024}} On March 16, he scored a season-high 40 points on 15-of-23 shooting with eight rebounds and nine assists in a 128–121 loss to the Warriors.{{cite web |last1=Woike |first1=Dan |title=Anthony Davis injury overshadows LeBron James' 40 points in loss to Warriors |url=https://www.latimes.com/sports/lakers/story/2024-03-16/anthony-davis-lebron-james-lakers-warriors-recap |website=[[Los Angeles Times]] |access-date=March 16, 2024}} On March 31, James put up 40 points on a career-high nine three-pointers made along with seven rebounds and five assists in a 116–104 victory over the Brooklyn Nets. He also surpassed Jordan for the most 30-point games (across the regular season and playoffs) in NBA history with 672.{{cite web |url=https://clutchpoints.com/lakers-news-lebron-james-brooklyn-nets-3-point-shooting-los-angeles |title='Masterful' LeBron James' leads Lakers over Nets with career-best 3-point shooting night |website=ClutchPoints |last=Corvo |first=Michael |date=March 31, 2024 |access-date=March 31, 2024}}{{cite web |url=https://clutchpoints.com/lakers-news-lebron-james-breaks-tie-with-michael-jordan-for-insane-nba-30-point-record |title=Lakers' LeBron James breaks tie with Michael Jordan for insane NBA 30-point record |website=ClutchPoints |last=Silverman |first=Steve |date=March 31, 2024 |access-date=March 31, 2024}} On April 2, James passed [[Oscar Schmidt]] to become [[List of basketball players with most career points|the world's all-time scoring leader in basketball history]].{{cite web |url=https://sports.yahoo.com/lebron-james-surpassed-oscar-schmidt-075718103.html |title=LeBron James surpassed Oscar Schmidt to become world's all-time scoring leader |website=sports.yahoo.com |date=April 5, 2024 |access-date=October 27, 2024}} In the season finale on April 14 against the Pelicans, James scored 28 points, brought down 11 rebounds, and his 17 assists pushed his career total over the 11k mark.{{cite web |title=LeBron James' triple-double lifts Lakers over Pelicans and into a play-in rematch with New Orleans |url=https://www.espn.com/nba/recap/_/gameId/401585823 |website=ESPN.com |date=April 14, 2024 |access-date=April 30, 2024}} James finished his 21st season in the NBA averaging 25.7 points, 8.3 assists and 7.3 rebounds a game. He also shot 54% from the field and a career-high 41.0% from 3-point range, making him the first player to average over 25 points and eight assists while shooting at least 50% overall and 40% from beyond the arc for an entire season.{{cite web |last1=Marvi |first1=Robert |title=Some amazing facts about LeBron James' 2023–24 regular season stats |url=https://lebronwire.usatoday.com/2024/04/15/some-amazing-facts-about-lebron-james-2023-24-regular-season-stats/ |website=lebronwire.usatoday.com |date=April 15, 2024 |access-date=May 3, 2024}} On the road against the Pelicans in the play-in game, James had 23 points, nine rebounds, and nine assists to win 110–106 and clinch his 17th career playoff appearance.{{cite web |title=LeBron James and the Lakers secure a playoff berth with 110–106 win over the Pelicans |url=https://www.cbsnews.com/losangeles/news/lebron-james-and-the-lakers-secure-a-playoff-berth-with-110-106-win-over-the-pelicans-2/ |website=cbsnews.com |date=April 16, 2024 |access-date=May 3, 2024}} After the Lakers fell into a 3–0 series deficit in the first round against the Denver Nuggets, James scored 14 of his 30 points in the fourth quarter in Game 4 as the Lakers won 119–108 to stave off elimination.{{Cite web |last=Beacham |first=Greg |date=April 28, 2024 |title=LeBron scores 30, and the Lakers avoid 1st-round elimination with a 119–108 win over champion Denver |url=https://apnews.com/article/lakers-nuggets-score-nba-playoffs-0f5deb96aa3bef29b6b6fa996f9bff5b |access-date=September 7, 2024 |website=AP News |language=en}} In the process, James recorded his 19th career 30-point game when facing elimination and improved to 15–12 in 27 career elimination games.{{cite web |title=Starting 5, April 28: Lakers stay alive, Thunder & Celtics take control |url=https://www.nba.com/news/starting-5-april-28-lakers-stay-alive-thunder-celtics-take-control |website=NBA.com |access-date=May 3, 2024}} The Lakers lost the series in five games, despite James' 30-point and 11-rebound outing in a 108–106 closeout loss in Game 5.{{Cite web |url=https://www.cbssports.com/nba/news/murray-scores-32-and-hits-game-winner-in-nuggets-108-106-series-clinching-victory-over-lakers/ |title=Murray scores 32 and hits game-winner in Nuggets' 108–106 series-clinching victory over Lakers |work=[[CBS Sports]] |agency=[[Associated Press]] |date=April 30, 2024 |access-date=November 7, 2024}} ====2024–2025: First father-son duo==== On July 6, 2024, James re-signed with the Lakers on a two-year, $104 million contract which carried a [[Trade (sports)#No-trade clause|no-trade clause]] and had a player option in the second year.{{cite web |title=Los Angeles Lakers Re-Sign Lebron James |url=https://www.nba.com/lakers/news/los-angeles-lakers-re-sign-lebron-james-70624 |website=NBA.com |date=July 6, 2024 |access-date=July 14, 2024}} Two weeks prior, James' son [[Bronny James|Bronny]] had been drafted 55th overall by the Lakers. This marked the first [[List of second-generation NBA players|father-son duo]] in NBA history.{{Cite web |date=June 27, 2024 |title=From Luke and Bill Walton to LeBron and Bronny James, these are the top NBA father-son duos |url=https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/38955259/nba-top-father-son-duos-james-walton-thompson-curry-sabonis |access-date=July 15, 2024 |website=ESPN.com |language=en}} On October 22, in a game against the [[Minnesota Timberwolves]], James and Bronny became the first father-son duo to appear in an NBA game together in NBA history.{{cite web |url=https://clutchpoints.com/lakers-news-lebron-james-bronny-james-nba-history-timberwolves |title=Lakers' LeBron James, Bronny James make NBA history vs. Timberwolves |last=Finnegan |first=Troy |website=ClutchPoints |date=October 22, 2024 |access-date=October 22, 2024}} During his [[2024–25 NBA season|2024–25 season]] debut, he tied Carter for the record for the [[List of NBA seasons played leaders|most NBA seasons played]].{{cite news |title=Most seasons in the NBA: How LeBron James will make history with 2024 Lakers debut |date=October 22, 2024 |website=The Sporting News |url=https://www.sportingnews.com/us/nba/news/most-seasons-lebron-james-nba-history/3f8df894de47295cb93a240d |access-date=October 22, 2024}} On November 13, he logged a triple-double of 35 points, 12 rebounds, and 14 assists, in a 128–123 victory against the [[Memphis Grizzlies]]. At age 39 years and 319 days, he broke his own record previously set in November 2019 for the oldest player to record a triple-double in three consecutive games.{{Cite web |last=Quinn |first=Sam |url=https://www.cbssports.com/nba/news/lebron-james-records-third-consecutive-triple-double-breaks-his-own-record-as-lakers-beat-grizzlies/ |title=LeBron James records third consecutive triple-double, breaks his own record as Lakers beat Grizzlies |work=[[CBS Sports]] |date=November 14, 2024 |access-date=November 14, 2024}} Two days later, he recorded his fourth consecutive triple-double of the season with 15 points, 16 rebounds, and 12 assists, in a 120–115 victory against the [[San Antonio Spurs]], breaking his personal record for the most consecutive triple-doubles made. He also tied the Lakers franchise record for the most consecutive triple-doubles, joining [[Magic Johnson]] (1981, 1987) and [[Russell Westbrook]] (2021).{{Cite web |last=McMenamin |first=Dave |url=https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/42418548/lebron-james-posts-4th-straight-triple-double-lakers-win |title=LeBron James posts 4th straight triple-double in Lakers' win |publisher=[[ESPN]] |date=November 16, 2024 |access-date=November 16, 2024}} On December 1, he reached 15,000 career field goals in a 105–104 victory against the [[Utah Jazz]], joining Abdul-Jabbar as the only players to reach said milestone.{{Cite web |url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/sports/nba/top-stories/lebron-james-makes-nba-history-as-second-player-to-reach-15000-field-goals/articleshow/115892196.cms |title=LeBron James Makes NBA History As Second Player To Reach 15,000 Field Goals |work=[[The Times of India]] |date=December 2, 2024 |access-date=December 3, 2024}} On December 19 against the Sacramento Kings, he passed Abdul-Jabbar for the most career regular season minutes played.{{Cite web |url=https://www.nba.com/news/lebron-james-breaks-nba-record-career-minutes |title=Lakers star LeBron James breaks NBA record for career minutes |date=December 19, 2024 |website=NBA.com}} On January 3, 2025, James put up 30 points and eight assists in a 119–102 win over the [[Atlanta Hawks]]. He surpassed Jordan (562) for the most 30-point games in NBA history. James also passed [[Dirk Nowitzki]] (1,522) for the fourth-most games played in the regular season in NBA history.{{cite web|url=https://clutchpoints.com/lakers-news-lebron-james-passes-michael-jordan-for-most-30-point-games-in-nba-history|title=Lakers' LeBron James passes Michael Jordan for most 30-point games in NBA history|website=ClutchPoints|last=Pagaduan|first=Jedd|date=January 4, 2025|access-date=January 4, 2025}}{{Cite web |url=https://www.nba.com/news/lebron-james-lakers-passes-dirk-games-played-jordan-30-point-games |title=LeBron James passes Michael Jordan in 30-point games, Dirk Nowitzki in games played |date=January 4, 2024 |website=NBA.com}} On January 21, James posted a triple-double with 21 points, 10 rebounds and 13 assists in a 111–88 win over the [[Washington Wizards]]. He became the second NBA player to post a triple-double at age 40 or older. [[Karl Malone]] is the only other NBA player to record a triple-double in his 40s. James also passed [[Scottie Pippen]] for 7th place on the all-time steals list.{{Cite web |url=https://www.nba.com/news/lebron-james-posts-triple-double-at-40 |title=LeBron James joins Karl Malone as only players to record triple-double at age 40 |date=January 22, 2024 |website=NBA.com}} On February 1, James registered a triple-double of 33 points, 11 rebounds and 12 assists in a 128–112 win over the [[New York Knicks]]. He also became the seventh player to reach 2,500 career three-pointers.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nba.com/news/lebron-james-seventh-nba-player-2500-career-3-pointers|title=Lakers star LeBron James becomes seventh NBA player to reach 2,500 3-pointers|publisher=[[National Basketball Association]]|agency=[[Associated Press]]|date=February 1, 2025|access-date=February 2, 2025}} On February 6, James recorded season-highs 42 points and 17 rebounds along with eight assists in a 120–112 win over the [[Golden State Warriors]]. He joined Jordan (43 pts) as the only players to record 40+ points in a game at 40 years or older. At 40 years and 38 days, James became the oldest player to score 40+ points in a game. He is also the youngest, at 19 years, 88 days old. He became the first player older than 30 to record 30+ pts, 15+ reb, 5+ ast and 5+ triples in a game.{{Cite web |url=https://www.nba.com/news/starting-5-feb-7-2025-2 |title=Starting 5, Feb. 7: LeBron, Steph & a (new) all-time duel, Ant can't stop |date=February 7, 2025 |website=NBA.com}} James was selected for his 21st NBA All-Star Game, but missed the [[2025 NBA All-Star Game|2025 contest]] due to foot and ankle soreness, ending his streak of consecutive All-Star starts at 20.{{cite news|first=Dan|last=Woike|title=LeBron James won't play in NBA All-Star Game, ending his 20-year streak|date=February 16, 2025|newspaper=Los Angeles Times|url=https://www.latimes.com/sports/lakers/story/2025-02-16/lakers-star-lebron-james-misses-21st-all-star-game|access-date=February 17, 2025}} On March 4, 2025, James surpassed 50,000 career points across the regular season and playoffs during a 136–115 win against the [[New Orleans Pelicans]].{{cite web|title=Lakers star LeBron James surpasses 50,000 combined points in regular season and playoffs|url=https://www.nba.com/news/lakers-lebron-james-surpasses-50000-total-points|website=NBA.com|date=March 4, 2025|access-date=March 5, 2025}} On March 8, James injured his groin in the second half of a 111–101 loss to the [[Boston Celtics]], sidelining him for multiple weeks.{{cite news |title=Report: LeBron James expected to miss time after suffering groin injury vs. Celtics |url=https://www.nba.com/news/lebron-james-groin-injury |access-date=March 15, 2025 |work=NBA.com |date=March 9, 2025}} After a seven-game absence, James returned on March 22 in a 146–115 loss to the [[Chicago Bulls]].{{cite news |last1=Beacham |first1=Greg |title=LeBron James returns from 7-game absence as Bulls dismantle Lakers |url=https://www.nba.com/news/lebron-james-starts-in-return-from-his-7-game-injury-absence-when-lakers-face-bulls |access-date=March 23, 2025 |work=NBA.com |date=March 22, 2025}} On March 26, James scored 13 points and a game-winning, buzzer-beating tip-in in a 120–119 victory against the [[Indiana Pacers]].{{Cite magazine|last=Phillips|first=Ryan|url=https://www.si.com/nba/lebron-james-buzzer-beater-tip-in-vs-pacers|title=LeBron James Saves Brutal Statistical Night With Buzzer Beater Tip-In vs. Pacers|magazine=[[Sports Illustrated]]|date=March 26, 2025|access-date=March 27, 2025}} It marked James' eight career game-winning buzzer-beater and his first as a Laker.{{cite news |last1=Packham |first1=Drew |title=Horry Scale: LeBron James saves Lakers in Indy with tip-in |url=https://www.nba.com/news/horry-scale-lebron-james-pacers-tip-in |access-date=March 27, 2025 |work=NBA.com |date=March 26, 2025}} On April 9, he played his 1,561st regular season game, passing Kareem Abdul-Jabbar for second on the league's [[List of NBA career games played leaders|games played list]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.espn.com/nba/recap?gameId=401705725|title=Luka Doncic scores 45 points in Dallas return as Lakers clinch playoff spot with 112-97 win|publisher=[[ESPN]]|agency=[[Associated Press]]|date=April 9, 2025|access-date=April 10, 2025}} On April 27, he logged his 144th career playoff double-double with 27 points and 12 rebounds along with eight assists, three steals and three blocks, in a 116–113 loss to the [[Minnesota Timberwolves]] in Game 4 of the first round. He passed [[Wilt Chamberlain]] for third in the career playoff double-doubles list.{{Cite web|url=https://www.espn.ph/nba/recap/_/gameId/401768057|title=Timberwolves push Lakers to edge of elimination with 116-113 comeback win behind Edwards' 43 points|publisher=[[ESPN]]|agency=[[Associated Press]]|date=April 27, 2025|access-date=May 3, 2025}} The Lakers lost Game 5 and were eliminated in the first round for the second straight year.{{Cite web|last=Woike|first=Dan|url=https://www.latimes.com/sports/lakers/story/2025-04-30/los-angeles-lakers-minnesota-timberwolves-nba-playoff-game-5|title=Lakers' season comes to a disappointing end with first-round loss to Timberwolves|work=[[Los Angeles Times]]|date=April 30, 2025|access-date=May 3, 2025}} In the game, James sprained the [[medial collateral ligament]] in his left knee and focused on being ready by training camp.{{cite news|title=LeBron James says knee 'good,' targeting 100% by training camp|date=June 17, 2025|work=ESPN|url=http://espn.com/nba/story/_/id/45528391/lakers-lebron-james-says-injured-knee-feeling-good-targeting-close-100-percent-possible-training-camp|access-date=June 19, 2025}} ====2025–2026: Delayed start amid injury==== On October 9, 2025, the Lakers announced that James would be sidelined for the start of the [[2025–26 NBA season|2025–26 season]] due to [[sciatica]] on his right side.{{Cite web|last=McMenamin|first=Dave|url=https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/46544930/lakers-star-lebron-james-sidelined-3-4-weeks-sciatica|title=LeBron James set to miss start of Lakers' season with sciatica|publisher=[[ESPN]]|date=October 9, 2025|access-date=October 23, 2025}} He missed the Lakers' first game of the season, a 119–109 loss to the [[Golden State Warriors]], marking the first time in his career he missed a season opener.{{Cite web|url=https://www.espn.com/nba/recap?gameId=401809244|title=Luka scores 43, but Butler's Warriors top Lakers 119-109 in a LeBron-less season opener|publisher=[[ESPN]]|agency=[[Associated Press]]|date=October 22, 2025|access-date=October 23, 2025}} ==National team career== [[File:LeBron James vs Yao Ming - Olympics 2008.jpg|thumb|James attempting a shot over China's [[Yao Ming]] at the [[2008 Summer Olympics]]]] As a 19-year-old rookie, James made his debut for the [[United States men's national basketball team|United States national team]] at the [[2004 United States men's Olympic basketball team|2004 Olympics]] in Athens, Greece.{{#invoke:cite|web|title=LeBron James Bio|url=http://www.usabasketball.com/bios/james_lebron.html|publisher=USA Basketball|access-date=February 26, 2013|archive-date=July 10, 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130710085235/http://www.usabasketball.com/bios/james_lebron.html|url-status=dead}} He spent the Games mostly on the bench,{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Thamel|first=Pete|author-link=Pete Thamel |title=After Sitting in 2004, Ready to Stand and Deliver|date=July 28, 2008|newspaper=[[The New York Times]] |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2008/07/28/sports/olympics/28hoops.html|access-date=February 26, 2013}} averaging 14.6 minutes per game with 5.8 points, and 2.6 rebounds per game in eight games. Team USA finished the competition with a bronze medal, becoming the first U.S. basketball team to return home without a gold medal since adding active NBA players to their lineup.{{#invoke:cite|news|agency=Associated Press|title=U.S.: 3-for-24 from 3-point range|url=https://www.espn.com/olympics/summer04/basketball/news/story?id=1859825|website=ESPN|date=August 15, 2004 |access-date=December 22, 2021}} James felt that his limited playing time was a "lowlight" and believed he was not given "a fair opportunity to play". At the [[2006 FIBA World Championship]] in Japan, James took on a greater role for Team USA, averaging 13.9 points, 4.8 rebounds, and 4.1 assists per game as co-captain.{{#invoke:cite|web|url=http://www.usabasketball.com/seniormen/2006/06_mwc_cumulative_stats.html|title=2006 USA Men's World Championship Team (8–1)|publisher=USA Basketball|access-date=May 1, 2007|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070513145308/http://www.usabasketball.com/seniormen/2006/06_mwc_cumulative_stats.html|archive-date=May 13, 2007}}{{#invoke:cite|web|website=NBA.com|url=http://www.nba.com/nuggets/news/anthony_usa_captain_081706.html|title=Carmelo Anthony Named One of Three USA World Championship Team Captains|access-date=May 7, 2007|date=August 17, 2006}} The team finished the tournament with an 8–1 record, winning another bronze medal. James' behavior was again questioned, this time by teammate [[Bruce Bowen]], who confronted James during tryouts regarding his treatment of staff members.{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Wojnarowski|first=Adrian|author-link=Adrian Wojnarowski|title=Season of LeBron ends with Olympic gold medal|date=August 12, 2012|publisher=Yahoo! Sports|url=https://sports.yahoo.com/news/nba--season-of-lebron-ends-with-olympic-gold-medal.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120815052130/http://sports.yahoo.com/news/nba--season-of-lebron-ends-with-olympic-gold-medal.html|archive-date=August 15, 2012|url-status=live}}{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Wojnarowski|first=Adrian|author-link=Adrian Wojnarowski|title=Team USA's declaration of independence|date=September 3, 2007|publisher=Yahoo! Sports|url=https://sports.yahoo.com/nba/news?slug=aw-teamusa090307|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305224654/https://sports.yahoo.com/nba/news?slug=aw-teamusa090307|archive-date=March 5, 2016|url-status=live}} Before naming James to the [[2008 United States men's Olympic basketball team|2008 Olympic team]], Team USA managing director [[Jerry Colangelo]] and coach [[Mike Krzyzewski]] gave James an ultimatum to improve his attitude, and he heeded their advice.{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Helin|first=Kurt|title=USA Basketball president lauds LeBron James' maturity|date=August 14, 2012|publisher=NBC Sports|url=https://nba.nbcsports.com/2012/08/14/usa-basketball-president-lauds-lebron-james-maturity/|access-date=December 22, 2021}} At the [[FIBA Americas Championship 2007]], he averaged 18.1 points, 3.6 rebounds, and 4.7 assists per game, including a 31-point performance against Argentina in the championship game, the most ever by an American in an Olympic qualifier.{{#invoke:cite|news|website=ESPN|url=https://www.espn.com/olympics/olybb/news/story?id=3002435|title=James' record outing highlights Team USA's dominance in title game|date=September 2, 2007|access-date=December 22, 2021|agency=Associated Press}} Team USA went 10–0, winning the gold medal and qualifying for the [[2008 Olympics]] in Beijing, China. James credited the team's attitude and experience for their improvement, saying: "I don't think we understood what it meant to put on a USA uniform and all the people that we were representing in 2004. We definitely know that now."{{#invoke:cite|web|title=LeBron: Now I Understand What It Means to Be an Olympian|url=https://www.tsn.ca/nhl/story/?id=245620|access-date=February 26, 2013|date=August 8, 2008|publisher=The Sports Network|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130728022140/http://www.tsn.ca/nhl/story/?id=245620|archive-date=July 28, 2013|url-status=dead}} At the Olympics, Team USA went unbeaten, winning their first gold medal since [[2000 Olympics|2000]].{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Morgan|first=Chris|url=https://www.pastemagazine.com/olympics/the-olympics/that-one-time-they-lost-on-the-team-usa-mens-baske/|title=That One Time They Lost: On the Team USA Men's Basketball Disaster in Athens|magazine=Paste|date=August 17, 2016|access-date=December 22, 2021}} In the final game, James' statistics were – 14 points, six rebounds, and three assists against Spain.{{#invoke:cite|web|url=https://www.basketball-reference.com/olympics/games/2008-08-24-USA-ESP/|title=United States vs. Spain, August 24, 2008 – Final Round|access-date=February 20, 2018|website=Basketball Reference|publisher=Sports Reference}} [[File:Mansoor Ahmed photos of Team USA basketball at London 2012 Olympics.jpg|thumb|James attempting a dunk at the [[2012 Summer Olympics]]]] James did not play at the [[2010 United States men's FIBA World Championship team|2010 FIBA World Championship]] but rejoined Team USA for the [[2012 United States men's Olympic basketball team|2012 Olympics]] in London, England.{{#invoke:cite|news|agency=Reuters|title=LeBron, Kobe return to U.S. team for London Games|url=https://www.chicagotribune.com/ct-xpm-2012-07-07-sns-rt-us-teambre867000-20120707-story.html|newspaper=Chicago Tribune|access-date=December 22, 2021|date=July 7, 2012}} He became the leader of the team, with Bryant, who would soon be 34, stepping back.{{#invoke:cite|news|title=Column: Going out in style – 'K Boys' golden again|date=August 12, 2012|website=ESPN|agency=Associated Press|url=http://www.espn.com/espn/wire?section=oly&id=8262281|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714233540/http://sports.espn.go.com/espn/wire?section=oly&id=8262281|archive-date=July 14, 2014|url-status=live}} James facilitated the offense from the post and perimeter, called the defensive sets, and provided scoring when needed.{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Freeman|first=Eric|title=The Team USA Reputations Index: How the gold medal changes our opinions of these players|date=August 13, 2012|publisher=Yahoo! Sports|url=https://sports.yahoo.com/blogs/nba-ball-dont-lie/team-usa-reputations-index-gold-medal-changes-opinions-140024134--nba.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120815201907/http://sports.yahoo.com/blogs/nba-ball-dont-lie/team-usa-reputations-index-gold-medal-changes-opinions-140024134--nba.html|archive-date=August 15, 2012|url-status=live}} During a game against [[Australia men's national basketball team|Australia]], he recorded the first triple-double in U.S. Olympic basketball history with 11 points, 14 rebounds, and 12 assists.{{efn|Assists were recorded as an official Olympic statistic starting in 1976.{{#invoke:cite|web|title=LeBron James Tallies First Triple-Double in U.S. Olympic History To Power USA To Semifinals With 119–86 Win Against Australia|date=August 8, 2012|publisher=USA Basketball|url=http://www.usabasketball.com/mens/national/12_moly_game_06.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120810210413/http://www.usabasketball.com/mens/national/12_moly_game_06.html|archive-date=August 10, 2012|url-status=dead}}}} Team USA went on to win their second straight gold medal, again defeating Spain in the final game.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Stein|first=Marc|title=Team USA as good as gold again|url=http://www.espn.com/olympics/summer/2012/basketball/story/_/id/8262102/2012-olympics-team-usa-good-gold-again|publisher=[[ESPN]]|access-date=October 14, 2018|date=August 12, 2012}} James contributed 19 points in the victory, becoming the all-time leading scorer in U.S. men's basketball history. He also joined Michael Jordan as the only players to win an NBA MVP award, NBA championship, NBA Finals MVP, and Olympic gold medal in the same year.{{#invoke:cite|web|url=http://hangtime.blogs.nba.com/2012/08/12/u-s-surges-past-spain-late-claims-second-straight-olympic-gold-medal/|title=U.S. Surges Past Spain Late, Claims Second Straight Olympic Gold Medal|first=Sekou|last=Smith|date=August 12, 2012|access-date=August 13, 2012|website=NBA.com|archive-date=November 26, 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151126163535/http://hangtime.blogs.nba.com/2012/08/12/u-s-surges-past-spain-late-claims-second-straight-olympic-gold-medal/|url-status=dead}} Afterwards, Krzyzewski said: "[James] is the best player, he is the best leader and he is as smart as anybody playing the game right now."{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Deveney|first=Sean|title=Olympics 2012: The golden era of LeBron James|date=August 12, 2012|magazine=Sporting News|url=http://aol.sportingnews.com/olympics/story/2012-08-12/olympics-2012-lebron-james-team-usa-spain-carmelo-anthony-london-games-us-gold-m|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120812231144/http://aol.sportingnews.com/olympics/story/2012-08-12/olympics-2012-lebron-james-team-usa-spain-carmelo-anthony-london-games-us-gold-m|archive-date=August 12, 2012|url-status=dead}} After a 12-year gap, James rejoined the national team on the [[2024 United States men's Olympic basketball team|2024 Olympic squad]] in Paris as Team Captain at 39 years old.{{Cite web |date=July 30, 2024 |title=LeBron James Has 'Captain LeMerica' Nickname Added to Basketball-Reference Profile |website=[[Bleacher Report]] |url=https://www.bleacherreport.com/articles/10130181-lebron-james-has-captain-lemerica-nickname-added-to-basketball-reference-profile}} Voted by the entire 592-strong U.S. delegation, James was named as one of the two Team USA [[Standard-bearer|flag-bearers]] for the [[2024 Summer Olympics opening ceremony|opening ceremony]] of the games, becoming the first male basketball player to be so honored.{{Cite web |date=July 22, 2024 |title=NBA superstar James named USA's Olympic flagbearer |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/articles/c250z5rgwj3o |access-date=July 22, 2024 |website=BBC}}{{Cite web |date=July 22, 2024 |title=LeBron James to Serve as Flag Bearer for Team USA at Paris 2024 Opening Ceremony |url=https://www.usab.com/news/2024/07/lebron-james-to-serve-as-flag-bearer-for-team-usa-at-paris-2024-opening-ceremony |access-date=July 22, 2024 |website=USA Basketball}} James led the team in rebounds and assists. He recorded his second Olympic triple double in a comeback win in the semi-final against [[2024 Serbia men's Olympic basketball team|Serbia]] with 16 points, 12 rebounds and 10 assists.{{Cite web |title=Steph Curry, LeBron James fuel USA's comeback win over Serbia |url=https://www.nba.com/news/usa-serbia-paris-olympics |access-date=August 9, 2024 |website=NBA.com |language=en}} James became the first player in Olympic history with two triple doubles.{{Cite web |date=August 8, 2024 |title=USA fight back from −17 to defeat Serbia in Semi-Finals |url=https://www.fiba.basketball/en/events/mens-olympic-basketball-tournament-paris-2024/news/usa-fight-back-from-17-to-defeat-serbia-in-semi-finals |access-date=August 10, 2024 |website=www.fiba.basketball |language=en}} Team USA went on to win their fifth straight gold medal, defeating France 98–87 in the final game,{{Cite web |date=August 10, 2024 |title=Basketball: USA win men's gold, beating France 98–87 – Paris 2024 |url=https://olympics.com/en/paris-2024/live-updates/5c19d7a0-e32c-44e6-80fd-a2733b71bede |access-date=August 10, 2024 |website=olympics.com |language=en}} with James recording 14 points, 6 rebounds and 10 assists. At the conclusion of the tournament with averages of 14.2 points, 6.8 rebounds and 8.5 assists, James was named the [[FIBA Men's Olympics MVP]] and was selected to the [[FIBA Men's Olympics All-Star Five]].{{cite web |url=https://www.fiba.basketball/en/events/mens-olympic-basketball-tournament-paris-2024/news/lebron-james-mvp-of-the-paris-2024-headlines-the-all-star-five |website=FIBA |access-date=August 11, 2024 |title=LeBron James MVP of the Paris 2024 headlines the All-Star Five |date=August 10, 2024}} James became the third male basketball player to win three gold medals at the Olympics. ==Player profile== Standing {{convert|6|ft|9|in|m|2}} tall and weighing {{convert|250|lbs|kg|0}},{{#invoke:cite|web|url=https://www.nba.com/stats/player/2544/career|url-status=dead|title=LeBron James Career Stats Page|website=NBA.com|date=November 14, 2006|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150207094746/http://www.nba.com/playerfile/lebron_james/career_stats.html|archive-date=February 7, 2015|access-date=December 22, 2021}} James has played the majority of his career at the [[small forward]] and power forward positions,{{#invoke:cite|web|url=https://www.basketball-reference.com/players/j/jamesle01.html|title=LeBron James Stats|website=Basketball Reference|publisher=Sports Reference|date=June 12, 2004|access-date=April 25, 2024}} but he has also been deployed at the other positions when necessary.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Simmons|first=Bill|title=LeBron Makes LeLeap|url=http://grantland.com/features/lebron-makes-leleap/|website=Grantland|date=June 22, 2012|access-date=June 22, 2012}} His playing style, which is athletic and versatile,{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Goldsberry|first=Kirk|title=The Evolution of King James|url=http://grantland.com/features/how-lebron-james-transformed-game-become-highly-efficient-scoring-machine/|website=Grantland|date=March 29, 2013|access-date=December 22, 2021}} has drawn comparisons to [[Basketball Hall of Fame]] players [[Oscar Robertson]]; [[Magic Johnson]] and [[Michael Jordan]].{{#invoke:cite|news|last=DuPree|first=David|url=http://usatoday30.usatoday.com/sports/basketball/playoffs/2006-04-20-james-cover_x.htm|title=King James' next conquest|work=USA Today|date=April 21, 2006|access-date=August 21, 2006}}{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Davis|first=Nate|title=Jalen Rose, Warren Sapp scoff at notion of LeBron in NFL|url=http://content.usatoday.com/communities/thehuddle/post/2011/11/jalen-rose-warren-sapp-scoff-at-notion-of-lebron-james-playing-in-nfl/|newspaper=USA Today|access-date=June 23, 2012|date=November 17, 2011}}{{cite magazine |date=August 5, 2014 |title=Sports Illustrated's Fittest 50 |url=https://www.si.com/edge/photos/2014/08/05/sports-illustrated-fittest-50-athletes-sports |magazine=Sports Illustrated |access-date=August 10, 2014}} Through the 2024–25 season, James' career averages are 27.0 points, 7.5 rebounds, 7.4 assists, 1.5 steals, and 0.7 blocks per game. Throughout the 2010s, he was usually ranked as the best player in the NBA each season by ESPN and ''Sports Illustrated''.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Windhorst|first=Brian|title=LeBron James is still the undefeated champ of #NBArank|url=http://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/20703687/cleveland-cavaliers-lebron-james-remains-undefeated-champ-nbarank|publisher=[[ESPN]]|access-date=September 15, 2018|date=September 15, 2017}}{{#invoke:cite|web|title=SI's Top 100 NBA Players of 2019: How Long Will LeBron James Remain on Top|url=https://www.si.com/nba/video/2018/09/13/top-100-nba-players-how-long-will-lebron-james-remain-top|access-date=September 15, 2018|date=September 13, 2018|magazine=Sports Illustrated}} ===Offense=== [[File:Lebronred.jpg|thumb|left|upright=0.7|James drives to the basket in March 2008. A deft finisher, he led the NBA in scoring and shooting percentage at the rim in 2013.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Goldsberry|first=Kirk|title=The 2012–13 CourtVision Awards: The Best Shooters|url=http://www.grantland.com/blog/the-triangle/post/_/id/68456/the-2012-13-courtvision-awards-the-best-shooters|website=Grantland|date=July 12, 2013|access-date=November 24, 2013}}]] As an 18-year-old rookie, James led the Cavaliers in scoring.{{#invoke:cite|web|title=2003–04 Cleveland Cavaliers Roster and Stats|url=https://www.basketball-reference.com/teams/CLE/2004.html|website=Basketball Reference|publisher=Sports Reference}} He holds numerous "youngest to" distinctions,{{#invoke:cite|news|title=James receives 78 of 118 first-place votes|url=https://www.espn.com/nba/news/story?id=1786166|website=ESPN|access-date=December 22, 2021|date=April 21, 2004|agency=Associated Press}}{{#invoke:cite|web|publisher=USA Basketball|url=http://www.usabasketball.com/biosmen/lebron_james_bio.html|title=USA Basketball: LeBron James Bio|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070514045140/http://www.usabasketball.com/biosmen/lebron_james_bio.html|archive-date=May 14, 2007}} including being the youngest player to score 30,000 career points.{{#invoke:cite|web|title=LeBron James' path to 30,000 career regular-season points|url=http://www.espn.com/espn/feature/story/_/id/22134348/lebron-james-path-30000-career-regular-season-points|publisher=[[ESPN]]|access-date=September 15, 2018|date=January 2018}} During his first stint in Cleveland, James was primarily used as an on-ball [[point forward]], and although his shooting tendencies were perimeter-oriented, he established himself as one of the best [[Slasher (basketball)|slashers]] and finishers in basketball.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Kim|first=Allen|date=October 8, 2010|title=NBA Power Rankings: Dwyane Wade and the Top 20 Slashers in the NBA|url=https://bleacherreport.com/articles/485933-nba-power-rankings-dwyane-wade-and-the-top-20-slashers-in-the-nba|website=Bleacher Report|access-date=May 19, 2021}}{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Burton|first=Roy|date=March 30, 2012|title=Power Ranking the NBA's Most Reckless Slashers|url=https://bleacherreport.com/articles/1125089-power-ranking-the-nbas-most-reckless-slashers|website=Bleacher Report|access-date=May 19, 2021}} His combination of speed, quickness, and size often created matchup problems for opposing teams because James was capable of blowing by larger defenders and overpowering smaller ones.{{cite magazine |last=Mahoney |first=Rob |date=December 7, 2016 |title=Then And Now: The Evolution Of LeBron James |url=https://www.si.com/nba/2016/12/07/lebron-james-cavaliers-heat-dwyane-wade-chris-bosh-kyrie-irving |magazine=Sports Illustrated |access-date=April 8, 2017}} These qualities became more apparent in transition, where he developed a reputation for grabbing defensive rebounds and then beating the defense downcourt for highlight reel baskets.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Simmons|first=Bill|title=50 Shades of Westbrook|date=March 9, 2017|url=https://theringer.com/the-russell-westbrook-problem-54e59db48117|publisher=The Ringer|access-date=April 8, 2017}} Around this time, James was frequently criticized for not having a reliable [[Jump shot (basketball)|jump shot]] or post game.{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Araton|first=Harvey|title=With Stars Aplenty, Heat Looks for Leader|date=March 8, 2011|page=B11|newspaper=The New York Times|url=https://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/08/sports/basketball/08dribble.html?_r=1&scp=16&sq=miami+heat&st=nyt|archive-url=https://archive.today/20120907181706/http://www.nytimes.com/2011/03/08/sports/basketball/08dribble.html?_r=2&scp=16&sq=miami+heat&st=nyt|archive-date=September 7, 2012|url-status=dead}} Teams would try to exploit these weaknesses by giving him space in the half court and forcing him to settle for three-pointers and long two-pointers, a strategy famously used by Spurs coach [[Gregg Popovich]] in the 2007 Finals, where James converted on only 36 percent of his field goals in four games.{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Zillgitt|first=Jeff|url=https://www.usatoday.com/story/sports/nba/playoffs/2013/06/04/lebron-james-miami-heat-vs-san-antonio-spurs-2007-finals-cleveland-cavaliers/2390097/|title=LeBron James '50 times better' than in 2007 NBA Finals|newspaper=USA Today|access-date=April 8, 2017}} In Miami, Heat coach [[Erik Spoelstra]] changed James' role to a more unconventional one. James spent more time in the post and improved his shot selection and accuracy on jump shots.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Haberstroh|first=Tom|title=Is LeBron James an elite shooter?|date=February 25, 2013|url=http://insider.espn.com/nba/story/_/page/PerDiem-130225/nba-lebron-james-shooting-star|work=ESPN|access-date=June 17, 2013}}{{subscription required}} He also learned how to work as an off-ball cutter in the Heat's "pass-happy" offense.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Lowe|first=Zach|title=The Miami Juggernaut|url=http://grantland.com/features/how-miami-heat-went-historic-winning-streak-came-dominate-league/|website=Grantland|date=March 26, 2013|access-date=December 22, 2021}} Behind these improvements, James' overall scoring efficiency rose to historically great levels.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Levy|first=Ian|title=What Drives LeBron James' Scoring Efficiency?|url=http://hardwoodparoxysm.com/2014/04/29/drives-lebron-james-scoring-efficiency/|publisher=Hardwood Paroxysm|access-date=April 8, 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170409111401/http://hardwoodparoxysm.com/2014/04/29/drives-lebron-james-scoring-efficiency/|archive-date=April 9, 2017|url-status=dead}} During this time, ESPN's Tom Haberstroh called James' free-throw shooting his biggest weakness, describing it as "average".{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Haberstroh|first=Tom|title=Miami Heat: 2013–14 roster (Subscription required)|date=September 18, 2013|url=http://insider.espn.com/nba/preview2013/story/_/page/2013-14-mia-preview/miami-heat-player-profiles|work=ESPN Insider|access-date=December 22, 2021}} Upon returning to the Cavaliers, James began to experience subtle age-related declines in productivity,{{cite magazine |last=Shoals |first=Bethlehem |title=LeBron James Is Underrated and Overlooked |url=https://www.gq.com/story/lebron-james-is-underrated-and-overlooked |magazine=GQ |access-date=April 9, 2016}} posting his lowest scoring averages since his rookie season in 2015 and 2016.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Engelmann|first=Jeremias|title=NBA has co-MVPs this season|date=April 8, 2016|url=https://www.espn.com/nba/insider/story/_/id/15160735/nba-co-mvps-season|work=ESPN Insider|access-date=December 22, 2021}} His shooting also temporarily regressed, and James briefly ranked as the NBA's worst high-volume shooter from outside the paint.{{cite magazine |last=Yuscavage |first=Chris |date=December 29, 2015 |url=https://www.complex.com/sports/2015/12/lebron-james-worst-shooter-nba-this-season |title=LeBron James Is the Worst Shooter in the NBA So Far This Season |magazine=Complex |access-date=January 7, 2022}} Despite these changes, he remained an elite offensive player who beat defenses with body control, strength, and varying attacking speeds. For most of his career, James has controlled the offense as the primary ball handler on his team.{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Windhorst|first=Brian|title=LeBron James takes control of the Lakers|date=November 28, 2018|website=ESPN|url=http://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/25384727/lebron-james-takes-control-lakers|access-date=November 28, 2018}} His playmaking ability is generally considered one of his premier skills, and some analysts rank him among the greatest passers in NBA history.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Lowe|first=Zach|title=The Decision 2.0: OK, What's Next?|url=http://grantland.com/the-triangle/lebron-james-cleveland-cavaliers-aftermath-miami-heat/|website=Grantland|date=July 11, 2014}} By exploiting his size, vision, and the attention James garners from opposing defenses,{{cite magazine |last=Rosenberg |first=Michael |title=Don't hate LeBron, enjoy him; he's most gifted player in NBA history |url=https://www.si.com/more-sports/2012/05/21/lebron |magazine=Sports Illustrated |access-date=December 22, 2021 |date=May 21, 2012}} he creates easy points for his teammates with accurate assists.{{cite magazine |last=Mahoney |first=Rob |title=The Case For: LeBron James as Most Valuable Player |url=https://www.si.com/nba/2013/04/16/lebron-james-mvp-nba-2013 |magazine=Sports Illustrated |access-date=December 22, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130527070601/http://nba.si.com/2013/04/16/lebron-james-mvp-nba-2013/ |archive-date=May 27, 2013}} James executes unconventional passes, including after leaving his feet and through defensive traffic.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Windhorst|first=Brian|title=LeBron James' unprecedented season of sharing has arrived|date=November 29, 2016|url=http://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/18159427/why-lebron-james-cooking-more-dishes|work=ESPN|access-date=January 15, 2017}} His uncanny tendency to find the open man has helped force NBA teams to incorporate some elements of [[Zone defense|zone]] into their schemes to better cover the weak side of the court and prevent James from passing to open shooters. Early in his career, James was criticized for overpassing in pressure situations, in particular for passing instead of shooting in the waning seconds of close games;{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Morre|first=Matt|title=Heat 93, Pacers 91: LeBron is clutch, then not clutch, clutch enough?|date=March 10, 2012|work=CBS Sports|url=https://www.cbssports.com/nba/news/heat-93-pacers-91-lebron-is-clutch-then-not-clutch-clutch-enough/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130407000005/http://www.cbssports.com/nba/blog/eye-on-basketball/17717241/heat-93-pacers-91-lebron-is-clutch-then-not-clutch-clutch-enough|archive-date=April 7, 2013|access-date=December 22, 2021}}{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Jackson|first=Scoop|title=LeBron's stomach-turning stigma|date=March 8, 2012|publisher=[[ESPN]]|url=https://www.espn.com/espn/commentary/story/_/page/jackson-120308/lebron-james-passing-last-second-shots-earns-long-lasting-stigma|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120309191029/http://espn.go.com/espn/commentary/story/_/page/jackson-120308/lebron-james-passing-last-second-shots-earns-long-lasting-stigma|archive-date=March 9, 2012|access-date=December 22, 2021}} however, as his career progressed, James' clutch performance was viewed more favorably.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Paine|first=Neil|title=LeBron May Be The Most Clutch Playoff Shooter Of His Generation|url=http://fivethirtyeight.com/datalab/lebron-may-be-the-most-clutch-playoff-shooter-of-his-generation/|website=FiveThirtyEight|date=May 11, 2015|access-date=May 22, 2015}} ===Defense=== [[File:LeBron James (40626830701).jpg|thumb|James defending against [[Joe Harris (basketball)|Joe Harris]] in February 2018]] At the beginning of his NBA career, James was considered a poor defensive player,{{#invoke:cite|web|title=LeBron against the all-time greats|date=March 2012|url=https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/page/5-on-5-120301/nba-lebron-james-all-time-greats|work=ESPN|access-date=December 22, 2021}} but he improved steadily through the years. In 2009, James became proficient at the chase-down block, which involves coming in from behind the opposition in transition to block the shot.{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Abrams|first=Jonathan|title=On Defense, James Is Closer Than He Appears|url=https://www.nytimes.com/2010/05/06/sports/basketball/06cavs.html?_r=0|newspaper=The New York Times|access-date=June 11, 2013|date=May 5, 2010}} In Miami, he developed into a more versatile defensive player, and the Heat relied on him to guard all five positions.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Haberstroh|first=Tom|title='Point center' LeBron James fourth in DPOY|date=May 2, 2012|url=https://www.espn.com/blog/truehoop/miamiheat/post/_/id/13790/point-center-lebron-james-fourth-in-dpoy|work=ESPN|access-date=December 22, 2021}} From the years 2012–2016, he was ranked among the top three defenders in the NBA by general managers, earning the vote as the best perimeter defender in the league preceding the [[2014–15 NBA season]].{{Cite web |title=NBA GM Survey: 2014–15 edition |url=https://www.nba.com/news/nba-gm-survey-2014-15 |access-date=December 20, 2024 |website=nba.com}} Along with [[Shane Battier]] and Dwyane Wade, Miami used James in an ultra-aggressive defensive scheme,{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Lowe|first=Zach|title=Lights, Cameras, Revolution|url=http://grantland.com/features/the-toronto-raptors-sportvu-cameras-nba-analytical-revolution/|website=Grantland|date=March 19, 2013|access-date=December 22, 2021}} with James cheating off the ball to help out inside or get into rebounding position.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Harper|first=Zach|title=LeBron James' defensive assignment allows versatility for Miami Heat|url=https://www.cbssports.com/nba/news/lebron-james-defensive-assignment-allows-versatility-for-miami-heat/|work=CBS Sports|date=May 23, 2013 |access-date=December 22, 2021}} Beginning in 2014, some analysts reported a regression in his defensive impact, stemming from a lack of effort and expected age-related declines.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Haberstroh|first=Tom|title=Who's the real plus-minus MVP?|date=April 9, 2014|url=http://insider.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/10754836/nba-does-real-plus-minus-say-league-most-valuable-player|work=ESPN Insider|access-date=December 22, 2021|url-access=subscription}}{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Partnow|first=Seth|title=Here's proof LeBron James is exiting the prime of his career|url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/fancy-stats/wp/2016/01/05/heres-proof-lebron-james-is-exiting-the-prime-of-his-career/|newspaper=The Washington Post|access-date=August 7, 2016}} During his second stint in Cleveland, James' defense progressively declined.
