After missed drives on offense, he often dawdled back on defense while complaining to the referees; he provided less help off the ball, and was less aggressive in [[switch (basketball)|switching]].{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Duncan|first=Nate|title=NBA No-Defense Team: Carmelo Anthony, Enes Kanter and Regular Season LeBron James get the big honors|date=June 12, 2018|work=The Athletic|url=https://theathletic.com/383038/2018/06/12/nba-no-defense-team-carmelo-anthony-enes-kanter-and-regular-season-lebron-james-get-the-big-honors/|url-access=subscription|access-date=March 5, 2019|quote=His regular-season work has increasingly lagged during his Cleveland years, but even as recently as last season the overall numbers were solid.}} James himself admitted to taking plays off at times, referring to this approach as "chill mode".{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Pelton|first=Kevin|date=December 31, 2014|title=LeBron's 'chill mode' hurting Cavs|url=http://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/12102220/nba-why-lebron-james-chill-mode-hurting-cleveland-cavaliers|access-date=April 8, 2017|publisher=[[ESPN]]}} He eventually developed a reputation for raising his defensive level in the playoffs, which some analysts referred to as "Playoff LeBron".{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Alamar|first=Ben|title=It's defense that makes 'Playoff LeBron' a thing|url=http://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/23027889/playoff-impact-lebron-james-felt-mainly-defense-nba|publisher=[[ESPN]]|access-date=September 15, 2018|date=April 6, 2018}} ==Legacy== [[File:Lebron wizards 2017 (cropped).jpg|thumb|left|upright=0.81|James with the Cavaliers in 2017. NBA analyst [[Brian Windhorst]], who spent his career covering James, recounted: "No one has ever had as much hype as James has had to live up to, and James has delivered on every last drop."{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Windhorst|first=Brian|title=All-Time #NBArank: LeBron No. 3|url=http://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/page/nbarank3/all-nbarank-3|publisher=[[ESPN]]|access-date=October 14, 2018|date=February 10, 2016}}]] James left high school as one of the most [[Media circus|hyped]] prospects in NBA history.{{efn|See Ryan Jones,{{rp|142}} [[Jay Bilas]],{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Bilas|first=Jay|title=Did 'LeBron Mania' go too far?|url=http://www.espn.com/columns/bilas_jay/1477784.html|publisher=[[ESPN]]|date=December 17, 2002|access-date=August 6, 2017}} [[Chris Broussard]],{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Broussard|first=Chris|title=James Answers the Hype with Standout Debut|url=https://www.nytimes.com/2003/10/30/sports/basketball-james-answers-hype-with-standout-debut.html|newspaper=The New York Times|date=October 30, 2003|access-date=August 6, 2017}} [[Sam Smith (sportswriter)|Sam Smith]],{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Smith|first=Sam|title=Hype over LeBron doesn't match Alcindor's|url=http://a.espncdn.com/nba/columns/smith_sam/1475855.html|publisher=[[ESPN]]|date=December 12, 2003|access-date=August 6, 2017}} and [[Chad Ford]].{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Ford|first=Chad|title=Top 25 draft prospects since 2000|url=http://www.espn.com/nba/draft2014/insider/story/_/id/11099139/nba-draft-lebron-james-leads-rank-top-25-draft-prospects-last-15-years|publisher=[[ESPN]]|date=June 18, 2014|access-date=August 6, 2017|website=ESPN Insider}}}} Upon entering the NBA, he made an immediate impact and was voted Rookie of the Year in his debut season.{{#invoke:cite|web|title=NBA Rookie of the Year Award Winners|url=http://www.nba.com/history/awards/rookie-of-the-year|website=NBA.com|access-date=October 14, 2018}} As of 2024, James has been named to 21 All-NBA Teams, including 13 times to the First Team, which are both NBA records. His four MVP awards are matched only by Michael Jordan, Kareem Abdul-Jabbar, Wilt Chamberlain, and [[Bill Russell]]; James and Russell are the only players to win four MVP awards in a five-year span.{{#invoke:cite|web|title=LeBron takes 2013 Kia Most Valuable Player award|url=http://www.nba.com/2013/news/05/05/lebron-2013-mvp-award/index.html?ls=iref:nbahpt3b|website=NBA.com|access-date=May 5, 2013|date=May 5, 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130507235207/http://www.nba.com/2013/news/05/05/lebron-2013-mvp-award/index.html?ls=iref:nbahpt3b|archive-date=May 7, 2013|url-status=dead}} James has also won four Finals MVP Awards, which is the second-most all-time,{{#invoke:cite|web|title=NBA Finals MVP Award Winners|url=http://www.nba.com/history/awards/finals-mvp|website=NBA.com|access-date=October 14, 2018}} and earned All-Defensive honors every season from 2009 to 2014. While James has never won the Defensive Player of the Year Award, he has finished second in the voting twice and lists it as one of his main goals.{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Wallace|first=Michael|title=LeBron James wins 4th MVP award|date=May 5, 2013|publisher=[[ESPN]]|url=https://www.espn.com/nba/playoffs/2013/story/_/id/9244504/lebron-james-miami-heat-1-vote-shy-unanimous-mvp-honor|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130505201808/http://espn.go.com/nba/playoffs/2013/story/_/id/9244504/lebron-james-miami-heat-1-vote-shy-unanimous-mvp-honor|archive-date=May 5, 2013|url-status=live}} His teams have appeared in the Finals ten times and won four championships; his 10 Finals appearances are tied for third all-time.{{efn|Tied with Abdul-Jabbar behind [[Bill Russell]] (12) and [[Sam Jones (basketball, born 1933)|Sam Jones]] (11) of the Celtics.{{#invoke:cite|news|title=LeBron James one win away from history: 10th NBA Finals appearance|date=September 26, 2020|website=NBC Sports|agency=Associated Press|url=https://nba.nbcsports.com/2020/09/26/lebron-james-one-win-away-from-history-10th-nba-finals-apperance/|access-date=October 9, 2020}}}} Some analysts have criticized him for not having a better Finals record, while others have countered that James usually performed well but his team was defeated by superior competition.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Paine|first=Neil|title=Where This Year's Cavs Rank Among LeBron's NBA Finals Supporting Casts|url=http://fivethirtyeight.com/datalab/where-this-years-cavs-rank-among-lebrons-nba-finals-supporting-casts/|website=FiveThirtyEight|access-date=August 7, 2016|date=June 1, 2015}}{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Haberstroh|first=Tom|title=LeBron's 2–4 Finals record is one of his greatest achievements|url=http://www.espn.com/nba/insider/story/_/id/15882307/what-lebron-james-losing-nba-finals-record-really-means-nba|publisher=[[ESPN]]|access-date=August 7, 2016|date=June 2, 2016}}{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Lowe|first=Zach|title=The Rematch: An NBA Finals preview|url=http://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/15888230/this-matchup-cavs-warriors-what-saw-2015|publisher=[[ESPN]]|access-date=August 7, 2016|date=June 2, 2016}} On the basis of his career longevity and on-court performances, [[sports publications]] have consistently included James in rankings of the best basketball players in history,{{efn|See ''[[Sports Illustrated]]'' (ranked 5th in 2016),{{#invoke:cite|web|last=McCullum|first=Jack|title=SI's 50 greatest players in NBA history|url=https://www.si.com/nba/2016/02/09/michael-jordan-lebron-james-stephen-curry-nba-greatest|access-date=October 14, 2018|date=February 8, 2016|magazine=Sports Illustrated}} [[ESPN]] (ranked 3rd in 2016 and 2nd in 2020),{{#invoke:cite|web|title=All-Time #NBArank: Counting down the greatest players ever|url=http://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/page/nbarankalltime/greatest-players-ever|publisher=[[ESPN]]|access-date=May 27, 2017|date=March 3, 2016}}{{#invoke:cite|web|title=Ranking the top 74 NBA players of all time: Nos. 10–1|url=https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/29105801/ranking-top-74-nba-players-all-nos-10-1|website=ESPN.com|date=May 13, 2020|access-date=April 3, 2022}} [[CBS Sports]] (ranked 2nd in 2017),{{#invoke:cite|web|title=CBS Sports' 50 greatest NBA players of all time: Where do LeBron, Curry rank?|url=http://www.cbssports.com/nba/news/cbs-sports-50-greatest-nba-players-of-all-time-where-do-lebron-curry-rank/|work=CBS Sports|access-date=February 18, 2017|date=February 17, 2017}} [[Fox Sports]] (ranked 2nd in 2017),{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Lynch|first=Andrew|title=Ranking the 25 greatest players in NBA history|url=http://www.foxsports.com/nba/gallery/ranking-the-25-greatest-players-in-nba-history-100716|work=Fox Sports|access-date=October 14, 2018|date=December 25, 2017}} ''[[Slam (magazine)|Slam]]'' (ranked 2nd in 2018),{{cite magazine |title=Top 100: The Greatest Players of All Time |magazine=Slam |last=Page |first=Dennis S. |date=February 23, 2018}} and ''[[Bleacher Report]]'' (ranked 2nd in 2019).{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Bailey|first=Andy|title=Bleacher Report's All-Time Player Rankings: NBA's Top 50 Revealed|url=https://bleacherreport.com/articles/2854727-bleacher-reports-all-time-player-rankings-nbas-top-50-revealed|website=Bleacher Report|access-date=March 31, 2020|date=September 26, 2019}}}} and he was named the Associated Press Male Athlete of the Decade for the 2010s.{{#invoke:cite|news|title=LeBron James named AP top male athlete of decade|url=https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/28389041/lebron-james-named-ap-top-male-athlete-decade|website=ESPN|access-date=October 31, 2020|date=December 29, 2019|agency=Associated Press}} This has resulted in frequent comparisons to Jordan in debates over the sport's all-time best.{{efn|name=GOAT debate}} In a 2016 interview with ''Sports Illustrated'', James acknowledged that his motivation was surpassing Jordan as the [[GOAT (sports culture)|greatest]].{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Quinn|first=Sam|title=LeBron James' quest to catch Michael Jordan faces an obstacle both utterly unparalleled and strangely familiar|work=CBS Sports|date=March 25, 2020|access-date=April 1, 2020|url=https://www.cbssports.com/nba/news/lebron-james-quest-to-catch-michael-jordan-faces-an-obstacle-both-utterly-unparalleled-and-strangely-familiar/}} In February 2018, ''[[The Ringer (website)|The Ringer]]'' spent an entire week devoted to both players, with [[Bill Simmons]] ultimately concluding that Jordan was still ahead.{{#invoke:cite|web|title=Jordan-LeBron Week|url=https://www.theringer.com/nba/2018/2/20/17029830/lebron-vs-jordan-week|website=The Ringer|access-date=October 14, 2018|date=February 19, 2018}} In polls, James has ranked second behind Jordan.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Manrique|first=Bruno|title=NBA players overwhelmingly vote Michael Jordan as the GOAT over LeBron James, Kobe Bryant|work=ClutchPoints|date=April 8, 2019|access-date=April 6, 2020|url=https://clutchpoints.com/nba-news-players-overwhelmingly-vote-michael-jordan-goat-over-lebron-james-kobe-bryant/}}{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Parrish|first=Gary|title=Candid Coaches: Who's the real GOAT – Michael Jordan or LeBron James?|work=CBS Sports|date=August 31, 2018|access-date=April 1, 2020|url=https://www.cbssports.com/college-basketball/news/candid-coaches-whos-the-real-goat-michael-jordan-or-lebron-james/}}{{#invoke:cite|news|last1=Davis|first1=Scott|last2=Hickey|first2=Walt|last3=Yutong|first3=Yuan|title=Most Americans think Michael Jordan is the 'GOAT' over LeBron James, and it's not even close|website=Business Insider|date=March 18, 2019|access-date=April 1, 2020|url=https://www.businessinsider.com/jordan-lebron-goat-debate-americans-jordan-2019-3}} The results strongly correlate with age, with older voters more commonly choosing Jordan. Davis ''et al.'' of ''[[Business Insider]]'' stated: "The data would suggest that younger, more-engaged NBA fans lean toward James, as he's still playing. Older generations who watched Jordan play and tune in less today lean toward Jordan." Referring to James as the best challenger to Jordan's status as the greatest basketball player of all time, Sam Quinn of CBS Sports stated that "the margin for error where Jordan is involved is overwhelmingly slim" and that "in the rings-obsessed basketball discourse", Jordan having more titles and an "unblemished Finals record holds significant weight". Fans perceived as [[wikt:overenthusiastic|overenthusiastic]] about James have been satirically referred to as ''Bronsexuals'', characterized as having a [[wikt: man crush|man crush]] on Lebron James.{{cite web | last=Shekhar | first=Amulya | title="I think they call you people Bronsexuals?": Jalen Rose trolls fans of LeBron James after Lakers get knocked out by Suns | website=The SportsRush | date=2021-06-06 | url=https://thesportsrush.com/nba-news-i-think-they-call-you-people-bronsexuals-nba-fans-react-to-jalen-rose-trolling-fans-of-lebron-james-after-lakers-get-knocked-out-by-suns/ | access-date=2025-08-14}}{{cite web | title=THE PRESS BOX: These Raging Bronsexuals! | website=The Tribune | date=2018-05-15 | url=https://www.tribune242.com/news/2018/may/15/press-box-these-raging-bronsexuals/ | access-date=2025-08-14}} Furthermore, it is emphasized that amongst that [[fandom]] demographic, a [[bandwagon effect]] and [[biases]] towards Lebron James have obscured clarity in the legacy debate versus Jordan,{{cite web | last=Korpan | first=Andrew | title=Lakers News: Luka Doncic Gives 1-Word Response To Caleb Pressley's 'Bronsexual' Question | website=ClutchPoints | date=2025-04-28 | url=https://clutchpoints.com/nba/los-angeles-lakers/lakers-news-luka-doncic-caleb-pressley-bronsexual | access-date=2025-08-14}}{{cite web | title=MSN | website=MSN | date=2025-08-14 | url=https://www.msn.com/en-us/sports/nba/lakers-news-luka-doncic-gives-1-word-response-to-caleb-pressley-s-bronsexual-question/ar-AA1DMQwV | access-date=2025-08-14}}{{cite web | title=PRESS BOX – Naughty: I got into a debate with a 'Bronsexual' | website=The Tribune | date=2020-07-24 | url=https://www.tribune242.com/news/2020/jul/24/press-box-naughty-i-got-debate-bronsexual/ | access-date=2025-08-14}} such as [[recency bias]].{{cite web | last=Brady | first=Alex | title=Recency Bias and Its Footing in the NBA GOAT Debate | website=The Georgetown Voice | date=2020-10-24 | url=https://georgetownvoice.com/2020/10/24/recency-bias-nba-goat/ | access-date=2025-08-14}} In addition to praising James' on-court accomplishments, analysts have also noted his influence on player empowerment throughout the NBA, which stemmed from his willingness to change teams during free agency. Ben Golliver of ''[[The Washington Post]]'' opined that James' move to the Heat in 2010 "defined a decade of player movement", and that he "fundamentally flipped the power balance between stars and their organizations."{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Golliver|first=Ben|title=LeBron James' 'Decision' defined a decade of player movement|url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/sports/2020/07/06/lebron-james-the-decision-impact/|access-date=October 31, 2020|newspaper=The Washington Post|date=July 6, 2020}}{{subscription required}} James' fellow players have also remarked on his influence, such as Warriors forward [[Draymond Green]], who reflected: "We've taken control of our destiny. And I think a lot of people hate that ... I think the doors that he's opened for athletes and especially basketball players is his biggest accomplishment."{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Kaskey-Blomain|first=Michael|title=Warriors' Draymond Green credits Lakers' LeBron James for helping to empower NBA players|url=https://www.cbssports.com/nba/news/warriors-draymond-green-credits-lakers-lebron-james-for-helping-to-empower-nba-players/|work=CBS Sports|access-date=October 31, 2020|date=September 16, 2019}} Multiple current and former NBA players have suggested James' free agency decisions catalyzed the league-wide trend in "[[Superteams in the NBA|superteams]]" in the 21st century.{{Cite web |last=Rohrbach |first=Ben |date=June 6, 2017 |title=Kevin Durant: LeBron James 'paved the way' for this Warriors super-team |url=https://sports.yahoo.com/kevin-durant-lebron-james-paved-way-warriors-super-team-202638207.html |access-date=July 1, 2024 |website=Yahoo! Sports |language=en-US}}{{Cite web |last=Starjacki |first=Will |date=April 27, 2021 |title=Julius Erving takes a subtle jab at LeBron James for creating superteams |url=https://www.basketballnetwork.net/latest-news/julius-erving-takes-a-subtle-jab-at-lebron-james-for-creating-superteams |access-date=July 1, 2024 |website=Basketball Network – Your daily dose of basketball |language=en}}{{Cite magazine |last=Nelson |first=Cory |date=July 18, 2022 |title=Shaquille O'Neal Blames LeBron James For 'Superteam' Concept |url=https://www.si.com/nba/heat/miami-news/shaquille-oneal-blames-lebron-james-for-superteam-concept |access-date=July 1, 2024 |magazine=Sports Illustrated |language=en}}{{Cite magazine |last=Barrasso |first=Justin |date=June 10, 2021 |title=Charles Barkley: 'I Root Against All Superteams' |url=https://www.si.com/nba/2021/06/10/charles-barkley-nba-playoffs-nets-superteam |access-date=July 1, 2024 |magazine=Sports Illustrated |language=en-us}}{{Cite magazine |last=Axson |first=Scooby |date=June 16, 2017 |title=LeBron James: I didn't start superteams |url=https://www.si.com/nba/2017/06/16/lebron-james-address-superteams-claims |access-date=July 1, 2024 |magazine=Sports Illustrated |language=en-us}} In 2022, James voiced his desire to play into his forties, potentially alongside or against his sons Bronny and [[Bryce James|Bryce]].{{cite magazine |url=https://www.si.com/nba/2022/08/30/lebron-james-says-wants-to-play-alongside-sons-bronny-bryce |title=LeBron James Says He Wants to Play into 40s, Alongside Both Sons |last=McDaniel |first=Mike |magazine=Sports Illustrated |date=August 30, 2022 |access-date=March 17, 2024 |language=en}} This eventually happened when Bronny was selected by the Lakers 55th overall in the [[2024 NBA draft]], making them the first [[List of second-generation NBA players|father-son duo in NBA history]].{{cite web |last1=McMenamin |first1=Dave |title=Lakers pick Bronny James in second round of NBA draft |url=https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/40447437/lakers-pick-bronny-james-second-round-nba-draft |website=ESPN.com |date=June 27, 2024 |access-date=June 27, 2024}} On October 22, 2024, in the season opener against the [[Minnesota Timberwolves]], they shared the court for the first time.{{cite news|title=LeBron and Bronny James make NBA history by playing together in Lakers' 110–103 win over Minnesota|date=October 22, 2024|publisher=ESPN|agency=AP|url=https://www.espn.com/nba/recap/_/gameId/401704628|access-date=October 24, 2024}} ==Personal life== James married his high school sweetheart, Savannah James ({{née|Brinson}}; born August 27, 1986), on September 14, 2013, in San Diego, California.{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Reynolds|first=Tim|date=March 21, 2015|title=Lebron Weds Girlfriend in San Diego|work=The Big Story |agency=[[Associated Press]]|url=http://bigstory.ap.org/article/ap-source-lebron-weds-girlfriend-san-diego|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130918100116/http://bigstory.ap.org/article/ap-source-lebron-weds-girlfriend-san-diego|archive-date=September 18, 2013|access-date=September 18, 2013}} They have two sons, [[Bronny James|Bronny]] and [[Bryce James|Bryce]], and a daughter Zhuri.{{#invoke:cite|web|url=https://people.com/parents/lebron-james-says-3-kids-greatest-things-in-his-life/|title=LeBron James Says His 3 Kids Are the 'Greatest' Things in His Life|last=Slater|first=Georgia|website=People.com|date=July 14, 2021|access-date=August 30, 2022|language=en|archive-url=https://archive.today/20240101162144/https://people.com/parents/lebron-james-says-3-kids-greatest-things-in-his-life/|archive-date=January 1, 2024|url-status=live}} Bronny was a standout high school basketball player, named as a McDonald's All-American in 2023, 20 years after James' selection.{{#invoke:cite|web|title=Roster announced for the 2023 McDonald's All-American Game |url=https://247sports.com/LongFormArticle/McDonalds-All-American-Game-announced-Isaiah-Collier-Aaron-Bradshaw-Justin-Edwards-DJ-Wagner-Bronny-James-Jared-McCain-and-more-203584643/#203584643_2|archive-url=https://archive.today/20240101162355/https://247sports.com/longformarticle/mcdonalds-all-american-game-announced-isaiah-collier-aaron-bradshaw-justin-edwards-dj-wagner-bronny-james-jared-mccain-and-more-203584643/%232093555|archive-date=January 1, 2024|url-status=live|last=Bossi|first=Eric|date=January 24, 2023|website=[[247Sports]]|access-date=January 1, 2024}} James owns a 30,000 square foot mansion in Akron, Ohio that he custom built in 2003 on his property at a cost of $2.1 million.{{Cite web |last=Nation |first=Cavaliers |date=March 24, 2015 |title=An Exclusive Look at LeBron's $9.2 Million Ohio Mansion (Full Gallery Inside) |url=https://cavaliersnation.com/2015/03/24/an-exclusive-look-at-lebrons-9-2-million-ohio-mansion-full-gallery-inside/ |access-date=March 9, 2024 |website=Cavaliers Nation |language=en-US}} During his stint with the Heat, James resided in [[Coconut Grove]], where he bought a $9 million three-story mansion overlooking [[Biscayne Bay]].{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Carter|first=Nicole|title=Miami heat: LeBron James buys $9M mansion in Coconut Grove, Fla|url=http://www.nydailynews.com/life-style/real-estate/miami-heat-lebron-james-buys-9m-mansion-coconut-grove-fla-article-1.470576|newspaper=[[New York Daily News]]|access-date=July 4, 2012|date=December 9, 2010}} In November 2015, James bought a 9,350 square-foot (870 m2) East Coast-style mansion in [[Brentwood, Los Angeles]], for about $21 million.{{#invoke:cite|news|title=LeBron James Snags Brentwood Mansion for $21 Million|magazine=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]]|date=November 10, 2015|url=https://variety.com/2015/dirt/real-estalker/lebron-james-buys-brentwood-manse-1201635639/|last=David|first=Mark|access-date=August 21, 2022}} He purchased another home in Brentwood in December 2017 for $23 million.{{#invoke:cite|web|date=July 12, 2017|title=LeBron James buys a second home in Brentwood for $23 million|url=https://www.latimes.com/business/realestate/hot-property/la-fi-hotprop-lebron-james-brentwood-20171207-story.html|access-date=August 15, 2021|newspaper=[[Los Angeles Times]]|last=Leitereg|first=Neal}} In September 2020, he purchased a third home in Los Angeles, this one in [[Beverly Hills, California|Beverly Hills]] for $36.75 million.{{#invoke:cite|web|date=September 30, 2020|title=LeBron James buys Beverly Hills mansion for $36.75 million|url=https://www.latimes.com/business/real-estate/story/2020-09-30/lebron-james-buys-beverly-hills-mansion-for-36-million |access-date=October 4, 2024|newspaper=[[Los Angeles Times]]|last=Flemming|first=Jack}} James' best friends in the NBA are Carmelo Anthony, [[Chris Paul]], and former Heat teammate Dwyane Wade. The four have been referred to as the [[Banana Boat Team|"banana boat crew"]]. During an excursion to the [[Bahamas]], James rescued Anthony from the water when Anthony was carried away from the boat by the current. During an [[Instagram Live]] session, Anthony later recounted: "He saved my life". When asked about how much danger Anthony was in prior to the rescue, James responded: "I don't really know what to say, to be honest. I'm just happy he's still here, obviously."{{#invoke:cite|web|date=August 18, 2020|title=LeBron recalls Melo ocean rescue ahead of series|url=https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/29683739/lebron-james-recounts-role-carmelo-anthony-ocean-rescue-ahead-lakers-blazers-series|access-date=August 19, 2020|publisher=[[ESPN]]|last=McMenamin|first=Dave}} In September 2023, ''ESPN'' reported that a new set of client names from the [[Biogenesis scandal#Accusations and investigation|Biogenesis scandal]] had been released; included was Ernest "Randy" Mims, a longtime friend and business manager of James, as well as David Alexander, a well-known trainer of prominent athletes who co-owned a cold-pressed juice and smoothie business with Savannah and also served as her personal trainer. The DEA determined that there "was never any indication that LeBron James did anything wrong."{{cite news | last=Fish | first=Mike | title=DEA files show probe of Biogenesis clinic surfaced clients not previously publicly known | date=September 13, 2023 | url=https://www.espn.com/mlb/story/_/id/38089564/tony-bosch-biogenesis-treated-wwe-star-big-show-boxer-shannon-briggs-others | work=espn.com }}{{cite news | last=Blum | first=Eric | title=LeBron James, wife and 2 associates named in federal PED investigation | date=September 13, 2023 | url=https://deadspin.com/lebron-jameds-wife-biogenesis-anthony-bosch-1850834572/ | work=deadspin.com }} ===Health=== James invests heavily in his health, reportedly spending $1.5 million a year to pay for [[personal chef]]s, [[athletic trainer]]s, and [[physical therapies]] for recovery. [[Kyle Korver]] has described James' fitness as being on "another level". However, James' routine is not perfectly clean; he drinks wine every night, [[Long-term effects of alcohol#Cardiovascular system|believing it to be good for his heart]],{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Thompson |first=Jackson |date=December 14, 2021 |title=LeBron James says he drinks wine every day, and he believes it's good for his heart. Here's what the science says. |url=https://www.insider.com/lebron-james-says-he-drinks-wine-every-day-021-12 |access-date=May 16, 2022|website=[[Insider.com]]}} while [[Tristan Thompson]] has stated that James eats desserts with every meal.{{cite magazine |date=May 1, 2021 |title=The Dirty Diet Behind LeBron James' Supernatural Rig |url=https://www.menshealth.com.au/lebron-james-dirty-diet |magazine=[[Men's Health]] |access-date=February 17, 2022 |author=Men's Health Staff}} In a March 2025 interview, James denied spending that much on his health, claiming he did not know where that number came from.{{cite web|url=https://www.news10.com/sports/sports-illustrated/24fcc386/lebron-james-debunks-popular-theory-about-him-spending-1-million-on-his-body|title= LeBron James Debunks Popular Theory About Him Spending $1 Million on His Body|website=news10.com|last=Rasmussen|first=Karl|date=March 26, 2025|access-date=April 19, 2025}} In January 2009, doctors at the [[Cleveland Clinic]] discovered a growth in the right side of James' jaw. [[Biopsy]] results showed that James developed a benign jaw [[tumor]], specifically in his [[parotid gland]],{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Windhorst|first=Brian|url=https://www.cleveland.com/cavs/2009/06/lebron_james_has_surgery_to_re.html|title=LeBron James has surgery at Cleveland Clinic to remove benign growth from jaw|website=[[Cleveland.com]]|date=June 3, 2009|access-date=June 1, 2021}} which required a five-hour surgery to remove on June 2 after the end of the Cavaliers' run in the 2009 playoffs.{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Windhorst|first=Brian|url=https://www.cleveland.com/cavs/2009/10/lebron_james_remembers_his_spr.html|title=LeBron James remembers his spring surgery on jaw tumor: 'It was a nerve-racking experience'|website=[[Cleveland.com]]|date=October 19, 2009|access-date=June 1, 2021}} ===Public image=== James is considered by fellow NBA players, media tabloids, and [[popular culture]] to be the "face of the NBA", often alongside Warriors superstar Stephen Curry.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Vardon|first=Joe|title=LeBron James is the 'face' of the NBA, but is Stephen Curry next?|date=February 15, 2015|url=https://www.cleveland.com/cavs/2015/02/lebron_james_stephen_curry_fac.html|newspaper=[[The Plain Dealer]]|access-date=December 22, 2021}}{{Cite magazine |date=June 2, 2016 |title=Stephen Curry annoyed by 'face of the NBA' debate |url=https://www.si.com/nba/2016/06/02/stephen-curry-lebron-james-face-nba-warriors-cavaliers |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190119121234/https://www.si.com/nba/2016/06/02/stephen-curry-lebron-james-face-nba-warriors-cavaliers |archive-date=January 19, 2019 |access-date=January 17, 2019 |magazine=[[Sports Illustrated]] |author=SI Wire}} His opinions have yielded significant influence on people who make important league decisions; for example, in 2014, James asked commissioner [[Adam Silver]] to increase the duration of the All-Star break, and the request was granted the following season.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Feldman|first=Dan|title=LeBron James reportedly asked Adam Silver for longer All-Star break, fewer back-to-backs|date=January 30, 2015|url=https://nba.nbcsports.com/2015/01/30/lebron-james-reportedly-asked-adam-silver-for-longer-all-star-break-fewer-back-to-backs/|publisher=NBC Sports|access-date=December 22, 2021}} On February 13, 2015, James was elected the first vice president of the [[National Basketball Players Association]] (NBPA).{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Pollakoff|first=Brett|title=Report: LeBron James elected Vice President of NBA Players Association|date=February 13, 2015|url=https://nba.nbcsports.com/2015/02/13/report-lebron-james-elected-vice-resident-of-nba-players-association/|publisher=NBC Sports|access-date=December 22, 2021}} Throughout his career, James has been ranked by ''Forbes'' as one of the world's most influential athletes,{{efn|Such as in 2010{{cite magazine |url=https://www.forbes.com/2010/04/21/lance-armstrong-endorsements-business-sports-influential-athletes.html |title=The Most Influential Athletes |magazine=Forbes |date=April 21, 2010 |access-date=January 11, 2022}} and 2013.{{cite magazine |date=May 6, 2013 |title=Most Influential Athletes 2013 |url=https://www.forbes.com/pictures/eddf45kkml/lebron-james-4/ |magazine=Forbes |access-date=July 27, 2013 |last=Van Riper |first=Tom}}}} and has been listed by ''[[Time (magazine)|Time]]'' as one of the [[Time 100|100 most influential people in the world]].{{cite news |title=LeBron James is one of Time's 100 Most Influential People again |url=https://www.beaconjournal.com/story/news/local/2019/04/17/lebron-james-is-one-time/5415499007/ |access-date=January 1, 2024 |work=Akron Beacon Journal |date=April 17, 2019}} During his first stint with the Cavaliers, James was adored by local fans, and [[Sherwin-Williams]] displayed a giant Nike-produced banner of James on its world headquarters.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Cho|first=Janet|title=Sherwin-Williams replaces LeBron poster with Cleveland skyline|date=October 27, 2010|url=https://www.cleveland.com/business/2010/10/sherwin-williams_replaces_lebr.html|website=[[Cleveland.com]]|access-date=December 1, 2012}} Despite their affection for James, Cleveland fans and critics were frequently annoyed when he sported a Yankees hat when he attended [[Cleveland Indians]] baseball games against the [[New York Yankees]].{{#invoke:cite|web|first=David|last=Briggs|website=MLB.com|date=October 5, 2007|title=LeBron spurns Tribe, sports Yanks cap|url=http://mlb.mlb.com/content/printer_friendly/mlb/y2007/m10/d04/c2250946.jsp|access-date=December 22, 2021}} Following his actions during the 2010 free agency period and ''The Decision'', James was listed as one of the most disliked athletes in the United States.{{Cite magazine |last=Van Riper |first=Tom |date=February 7, 2012 |title=America's Most Disliked Athletes |url=https://www.forbes.com/pictures/eddf45fkhi/lebron-james-2/ |magazine=Forbes |access-date=January 11, 2022}} By 2013, his image had mostly recovered and he was reported by ESPN as the most popular player in the NBA for the second time in his career.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Rovell|first=Darren|title=LeBron James NBA's most popular|date=July 26, 2013|url=https://www.espn.com/nba/truehoop/miamiheat/story/_/id/9512608/poll-shows-lebron-james-passes-kobe-bryant-nba-most-popular-player|publisher=[[ESPN]]|access-date=December 22, 2021}} In 2014, James was named the most popular male athlete in America by the Harris Poll.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Harper|first=Zach|title=LeBron James is finally the most popular male athlete in America|url=https://www.cbssports.com/nba/news/lebron-james-is-finally-the-most-popular-male-athlete-in-america/|work=CBS Sports|date=July 17, 2014 |access-date=December 22, 2021}} He has led the league in jersey sales six times.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Rovell|first=Darren|title=LeBron James tops jersey sale|date=April 17, 2014|url=https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/10795792/lebron-james-miami-heat-tops-nba-jersey-sales|publisher=[[ESPN]]|access-date=April 27, 2014}} {{anchor|voguecover}} {{multiple image | align = right | image1 = Harry R. Hopps, Destroy this mad brute Enlist - U.S. Army, 03216u edit.jpg | width1 = 130 | alt1 = | caption1 = | image2 = Vogue-Cover James-Bundchen.jpg | width2 = 135 | alt2 = | caption2 = | footer = The World War I ''[[Anti-German sentiment|Destroy This Mad Brute]]'' poster and the April 2008 ''[[Vogue (magazine)|Vogue]]'' cover with James and [[Gisele Bündchen]], which critics said referenced the earlier poster{{#invoke:cite|news|date=March 27, 2008|title=Some Call LeBron James' 'Vogue' Cover Offensive|work=NPR |agency=NPR|url=https://www.npr.org/templates/story/story.php?storyId=89151096}} }} [[Sports memorabilia|Memorabilia]] associated with James is highly sought after; two of James' [[rookie card]]s are among the most expensive [[basketball card]]s ever sold at auction, and one of those cards also briefly held the record for the [[List of most expensive sports cards|most expensive modern-day sports card]] when it sold for $1.8 million at auction in July 2020.{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Acquavella|first=Katherine|title=LeBron James rookie card sells for modern-day record $1.8 million at auction|url=https://www.cbssports.com/nba/news/lebron-james-rookie-card-sells-for-modern-day-record-1-8-million-at-auction/|access-date=August 28, 2020|work=[[CBS Sports]]|date=July 20, 2020}} A [[Mike Trout]] rookie card broke the record for a modern-day card the following month.{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Beer|first=Tommy|title=Mike Trout Rookie Card Sells For $3.93 Million, Breaking The All-Time Record|url=https://www.forbes.com/sites/tommybeer/2020/08/23/mike-trout-rookie-card-sells-for-393-million-breaking-the-all-time-record/#5d0f4674238b|access-date=August 28, 2020|magazine=[[Forbes]]|date=August 23, 2020}} All jerseys worn in the [[2020 NBA All-Star Game]] were auctioned by the NBA and NBPA to raise funds for charity; James' jersey sold for $630,000, setting a record for a modern-day sports jersey.{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Mueller|first=Rich|title=LeBron's 2020 All-Star Game Jersey Sets Record Price|url=https://www.sportscollectorsdaily.com/lebrons-2020-all-star-game-jersey-sets-record-price/|access-date=August 28, 2020|work=Sports Collectors Daily|date=March 8, 2020}} In March 2008, James became the first black man, as well as the third man overall after [[Richard Gere]] and [[George Clooney]], to appear on the cover of ''[[Vogue (magazine)|Vogue]]'', when he posed with [[Gisele Bündchen]].{{#invoke:cite|news|date=March 14, 2008 |title=NBA: LeBron becomes third man on Vogue cover |newspaper=[[Houston Chronicle]] |agency=Chronicle News Services |url=http://www.chron.com/sports/rockets/article/NBA-LeBron-becomes-third-man-on-Vogue-cover-1785218.php |access-date=February 26, 2013}} In response, ESPN columnist [[Jemele Hill]] considered the cover offensive and "memorable for the wrong reasons", describing the demeanor of James and his holding Bündchen as a reference to classic imagery of the movie monster [[King Kong]], a dark savage capturing his fair-skinned love interest.{{#invoke:cite|news|url=https://www.espn.com/espn/page2/story?page=hill/080320|date=March 21, 2008|title=LeBron should be more careful with his image|publisher=[[ESPN]]|access-date=December 22, 2021|last=Hill|first=Jemele|author-link=Jemele Hill|quote=But this cover gave you the double-bonus of having LeBron and Gisele strike poses that others in the blogosphere have noted draw a striking resemblance to the racially charged image of King Kong enveloping his very fair-skinned lady love interest.}} In 2025, [[Mattel]] announced that it was adding James to the Barbie roster, "making him the first professional male athlete to be honoured as a [[Ken (doll)|Ken doll]]." According to the BBC, it is "an inch taller than the standard Ken doll."{{Cite web |last=Granville |first=Samantha |date=2025-04-10 |title=LeBron James becomes first male athlete with a Ken doll |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c7877zznd0po |access-date=2025-04-10 |website=[[BBC News]] |language=en-GB}} James was announced to be honorary co-chair of the 2025 [[Met Gala]] in [[Manhattan]],{{cite web|url=https://www.roadandtrack.com/news/a62559589/lewis-hamilton-named-co-chair-of-the-2025-met-gala/|title=Lewis Hamilton Named Co-Chair of the 2025 Met Gala|author=Fred Smith|work=Road & Track |date=October 9, 2024 |publisher=Road&Track|access-date=April 13, 2025}} though he had to skip due to the knee injury he sustained in game 5 of the first round against the Minnesota Timberwolves.{{cite web |url=https://www.si.com/nba/lebron-james-will-miss-2025-met-gala-honorary-chair-knee-injury |title=LeBron James Will Miss 2025 Met Gala As Honorary Chair Due to Knee Injury |author=Madison Williams |date=May 5, 2025 |publisher=Sports Illustrated |access-date=2025-05-05}}{{cite web | url=https://www.latimes.com/entertainment-arts/story/2025-05-05/lebron-james-met-gala-injured-knee | title=LeBron James begs off attending the Met Gala, where he is the honorary co-chairman | website=[[Los Angeles Times]] | date=May 5, 2025 }} ==Activism== ===Philanthropy=== [[File:LeBronJames MichelleObama DwyaneWade.jpg|thumb|right|James, First Lady [[Michelle Obama]], and [[Dwyane Wade]] tape a public service announcement in January 2014.]] James has maintained close ties to his hometown of Akron, Ohio, which is also where his charity foundation, the LeBron James Family Foundation (LJFF), is based.{{#invoke:cite|web|date=May 1, 2012 |title=The LeBron James Family Foundation to Unveil its Brand New Website, LeBronJamesFamilyFoundation.org |url=http://www.nba.com/heat/community/the_lebron_james_family_founda_2012_05_01.html |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220114011900/https://www.nba.com/heat/community/the_lebron_james_family_founda_2012_05_01.html |archive-date=January 14, 2022 |website=[[NBA.com]]}} Since 2005, the foundation has held an annual bike-a-thon to raise money for various causes.{{#invoke:cite|web|date=July 6, 2011 |title=LeBron's bike event stresses education |url=https://www.espn.com/nba/truehoop/miamiheat/news/story?id=6741900 |access-date=December 22, 2021 |publisher=[[ESPN]] |agency=Associated Press}} In 2015, James announced a partnership with the University of Akron to provide scholarships for as many as 2,300 children beginning in 2021.{{#invoke:cite|web|date=August 14, 2015|agency=Associated Press |title=LeBron James, Akron to fund 4-year scholarships for I Promise students |url=https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/13437404/lebron-james-university-akron-fund-4-year-scholarships-promise-students |access-date=December 22, 2021 |publisher=[[ESPN]]}} In 2017, he received the [[J. Walter Kennedy Citizenship Award]] from the NBA for his "outstanding service and dedication to the community."{{#invoke:cite|web|date=May 21, 2017 |title=LeBron James Wins PBWA's 2016–17 J. Walter Kennedy Citizenship Award |url=http://www.nba.com/cavaliers/releases/pbwa-citizenship-award-170521 |access-date=May 21, 2017 |website=[[NBA.com]] |author=clenadams}} In November 2017, the Akron School Board approved the [[I Promise School]], a public elementary school created in a partnership with the LJFF to help struggling elementary school students stay in school.{{#invoke:cite|news|date=November 28, 2017|url=https://www.usatoday.com/story/sports/nba/2017/11/28/board-oks-plan-for-lebron-james-i-promise-school-in-akron/108092894/|title=Board OKs plan for LeBron James' 'I Promise' school in Akron|newspaper=[[USA Today]]|agency=Associated Press|access-date=January 8, 2019}} The school officially opened on July 30, 2018, with James reflecting that it was his most important professional accomplishment.{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Singer |first=Michael |date=November 30, 2017 |title=LeBron James: Opening school is my most important professional accomplishment |newspaper=[[USA Today]] |url=https://www.usatoday.com/story/sports/nba/2017/11/30/lebron-james-opening-school-most-important-professional-accomplishment/909374001/ |access-date=August 8, 2018}} Other noteworthy public and charitable initiatives undertaken by James in Akron include the I Promise Institute at the University of Akron,{{#invoke:cite|web|url=https://www.lebronjamesfamilyfoundation.org/i-promise-institute/|url-status=live|title=I Promise Institute|publisher=LeBron James Family Foundation|date=May 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220114011722/https://www.lebronjamesfamilyfoundation.org/i-promise-institute/|archive-date=January 14, 2022|access-date=February 21, 2022}} I Promise Village (housing complex),{{#invoke:cite|web|url=https://www.lebronjamesfamilyfoundation.org/news/the-i-promise-village|url-status=live|title=I Promise Village|publisher=LeBron James Family Foundation|date=August 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220114011707/https://www.lebronjamesfamilyfoundation.org/news/the-i-promise-village|archive-date=January 14, 2022|access-date=February 21, 2022}} House Three-Thirty (community center/retail plaza),{{#invoke:cite|web|url=https://www.lebronjamesfamilyfoundation.org/news/welcome-to-house-three-thirty|url-status=live|title=Welcome to House Three Thirty|publisher=LeBron James Family Foundation|date=January 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220114011710/https://www.lebronjamesfamilyfoundation.org/news/welcome-to-house-three-thirty|archive-date=January 14, 2022|access-date=February 21, 2022}} and I Promise Health Quarters (medical center). James is also an active supporter of various non-profit organizations, including [[After-School All-Stars]], Boys & Girls Clubs of America, and [[Children's Defense Fund]].{{#invoke:cite|web|title=LeBron James Charity Work, Events and Causes|url=http://www.looktothestars.org/celebrity/lebron-james|website=Look to the Stars|access-date=July 1, 2012}}{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Rueckert|first=Phineas|date=March 21, 2017|title=LeBron James Just Delivered Nikes He Helped Design for Kids With Disabilities|work=Global Citizen |url=https://www.globalcitizen.org/en/content/lebron-james-shoes-children-with-disabilities/|access-date=December 25, 2017}}{{#invoke:cite|news|url=http://www.enstarz.com/articles/127725/20151217/lebron-james-news-cleveland-cavaliers-star-pays-respect-to-a-fan-in-the-middle-of-a-game.htm|title=Inspiring Videos: Cleveland Cavaliers Star LeBron James Pays Respect To A Fan In The Middle Of A Game|last=EnStars|date=December 17, 2015|website=Enstarz|access-date=December 25, 2017}} In 2016, he donated $2.5 million to the [[Smithsonian]] [[National Museum of African American History and Culture]] to support an exhibit on [[Muhammad Ali]].{{#invoke:cite|web|date=November 17, 2016 |title=LeBron James donates $2.5 million to Smithsonian's Muhammad Ali exhibit |url=http://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/18070104/lebron-james-cleveland-cavaliers-donates-25-million-smithsonian-muhammad-ali-exhibit |access-date=November 17, 2016 |publisher=[[ESPN]]}} ===Politics=== {{multiple image | align = right | direction = vertical | image1 = LeBronJamesBush.jpg | image2 = Barack Obama shaking hands with LeBron James, July 2012.jpg | footer = James meets with presidents [[George W. Bush]] in 2008, and [[Barack Obama]] in 2012, respectively. }} Throughout his career, James has taken public stances on controversial issues, and has mentioned a feeling of obligation to effect change using his status.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Wallace|first=Michael|title=LeBron: No place for Sterling|date=April 27, 2014|url=https://www.espn.com/nba/truehoop/miamiheat/story/_/id/10845651/nba-lebron-james-scores-most-telling-point-sterling|publisher=[[ESPN]]|access-date=December 22, 2021}} Those include the [[War in Darfur]],{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Smith|first=Shelley|date=May 16, 2008|title=LeBron speaking out on Darfur|publisher=[[ESPN]]|url=https://www.espn.com/nba/news/story?id=3398947|access-date=January 11, 2021}} the [[killing of Trayvon Martin]],{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Hill|first=Jemele|title=The Heat's hoodies as change agent|date=March 26, 2012|url=https://www.espn.com/espn/commentary/story/_/page/hill-120326/lebron-james-other-athletes-protest-trayvon-martin-shooting-show-change-agent-power-sports|publisher=[[ESPN]]|access-date=December 22, 2021}} the now-former NBA owner [[Donald Sterling]]'s racist comments in 2014, the [[Shooting of Michael Brown|Michael Brown]] verdict,{{#invoke:cite|web|last=McMenamin|first=Dave|title=LeBron James: Bigger issues at play|date=November 25, 2014|url=https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/11938106/lebron-james-others-comment-ferguson-decision|publisher=[[ESPN]]|access-date=December 22, 2021}} the [[death of Eric Garner]],{{#invoke:cite|web|last=McMenamin|first=Dave|title=LeBron, Irving in 'I Can't Breathe' tees|date=December 9, 2014|url=https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/12001456/lebron-james-kyrie-irving-cleveland-cavaliers-kevin-garnett-deron-williams-brooklyn-nets-wear-breathe-shirt-reference-eric-garner|publisher=[[ESPN]]|access-date=December 22, 2021}} the [[U.S. national anthem kneeling protests]],{{#invoke:cite|web|last=McMenamin|first=Dave|title=LeBron James feels that Colin Kaepernick is being blackballed by NFL|url=http://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/21474704/lebron-james-agrees-colin-kaepernick-being-blackballed|publisher=[[ESPN]]|date=November 19, 2017|access-date=November 20, 2017}} the [[shooting of Breonna Taylor]],{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Martin |first=Jill |date=August 19, 2020 |title=Lakers wear red MAGA-like hats but the message asks for justice for Breonna Taylor |publisher=[[CNN]] |url=https://www.cnn.com/2020/08/18/us/lakers-breonna-taylor-hats-spt-trnd/index.html |access-date=August 19, 2020}} the [[death of Ma'Khia Bryant]],{{#invoke:cite|web|url=https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/31306343/lebron-james-explains-why-deleted-tweet-police-shooting-makhia-bryant|title=LeBron James explains why he deleted tweet on police shooting of Ma'Khia Bryant|date=April 21, 2021|last=McMenamin|first=Dave|publisher=[[ESPN]]|access-date=April 22, 2021|archive-date=April 22, 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210422015447/https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/31306343/lebron-james-explains-why-deleted-tweet-police-shooting-makhia-bryant|url-status=live}} [[Kyle Rittenhouse]],{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Axelrod|first=Ben|date=November 11, 2021|title='What tears?' LeBron James mocks Kyle Rittenhouse's courtroom breakdown|url=https://www.wkyc.com/article/sports/nba/lebron-james/lebron-james-mocks-kyle-rittenhouse/95-5e9a3c2f-2e13-478a-b92e-19f7b4abb8ba|access-date=December 25, 2021|website=WKYC}} and the ruling of ''[[Dobbs v. Jackson Women's Health Organization]]'' that overturned ''[[Roe v. Wade]]'' in 2022.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Peterson |first=Anne M. |date=June 24, 2022 |title=American soccer star Rapinoe leads athletes reaction to U.S. Supreme Court's abortion decision |url=https://www.cbc.ca/sports/athletes-react-american-supreme-court-abortion-decision-1.6501291 |access-date=June 25, 2022 |publisher=[[CBC News]] |agency=Associated Press |language=en-US}} On August 27, 2020, James and his Lakers teammates, as well as the Bucks, began boycotting the 2020 NBA playoffs to protest the [[shooting of Jacob Blake]].{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Nesbitt |first=Andy |date=August 27, 2020 |title=The Milwaukee Bucks' boycott should be celebrated forever |url=https://ftw.usatoday.com/2020/08/the-milwaukee-bucks-boycott-should-be-celebrated-forever |access-date=August 29, 2020 |newspaper=[[USA Today]]}} In response, senior White House advisor [[Jared Kushner]] stated that he was planning to reach out to James regarding the boycott. Following a players' committee to discuss the boycott, James and others reached out to former president [[Barack Obama]], who reportedly advised them to continue playing and finish that year's NBA season.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=McMenamin|first=Dave|date=August 29, 2020|title=LeBron, CP3 advised by Obama during stalemate|url=https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/29762633/lebron-james-chris-paul-received-advice-barack-obama-stalemate|access-date=August 29, 2020|publisher=[[ESPN]]}} During the [[2019–2020 Hong Kong protests]], a statement James made about a since-deleted tweet by [[Daryl Morey]],{{#invoke:cite|news|url=https://www.vox.com/2019/10/7/20902700/daryl-morey-tweet-china-nba-hong-kong|title=The raging controversy over the NBA, China, and the Hong Kong protests, explained|last=Yglesias|first=Matthew|date=October 7, 2019|website=[[Vox Media|Vox]]|access-date=October 16, 2019}} in which Morey expressed support for the pro-democracy movement in Hong Kong, became the subject of controversy. James said Morey was "misinformed".{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Medina |first=Mark |date=October 14, 2019 |title=LeBron James says Rockets GM Daryl Morey 'misinformed' on China tweet, then clarifies remark |newspaper=[[USA Today]] |url=https://www.usatoday.com/story/sports/nba/lakers/2019/10/14/lebron-james-addresses-daryl-morey-china-tweet-clarifies-remark/3982048002/ |access-date=October 16, 2019}} His statement drew backlash from protesters in the movement, with many taking to social media to use racist language against him.{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Bieler |first=Des |date=October 15, 2019 |title=Hong Kong protesters burn LeBron James jersey, chant profanity toward Lakers star |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/sports/2019/10/15/hong-kong-protesters-burn-lebron-james-jersey-chant-profanity-toward-lakers-star/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220530014717/https://www.washingtonpost.com/sports/2019/10/15/hong-kong-protesters-burn-lebron-james-jersey-chant-profanity-toward-lakers-star/ |archive-date=May 30, 2022}}{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Leicester |first=John |date=October 15, 2019 |title=LeBron James no longer King James for Hong Kong protesters |url=https://www.sandiegouniontribune.com/news/nation-world/story/2019-10-15/lebron-james-no-longer-king-james-for-hong-kong-protesters |newspaper=[[San Diego Union-Tribune]] |language=en}}{{Cite web |last=Littal |first=Robert |date=2019-10-17 |title=Some Hong Kong Protestors Resort to Calling LeBron a N*gger, a Slave With Low IQ & an Orangutan on Social Media For Not Supporting Morey's Tweet (Racist Posts).. |url=https://blacksportsonline.com/2019/10/some-hong-kong-protestors-resort-to-calling-lebron-a-ngger-a-slave-with-low-iq-an-orangutan-on-social-media-for-not-supporting-moreys-tweet-racist-posts/ |access-date=2025-01-02 |website=BlackSportsOnline |language=en-US}} In February 2022, political commentator [[Bill Maher]] called James hypocritical for not taking a critical stance towards the Chinese government's human rights abuses.{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Haring |first=Bruce |date=February 18, 2022 |title=Bill Maher Attacks John Cena, LeBron James, Tom Cruise, Eileen Gu |url=https://deadline.com/2022/02/bill-maher-real-time-hbo-john-cena-eileen-gu-lebron-james-tom-cruise-china-totalitarian-favors-1234956958/ |website=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |accessdate=February 21, 2022}} James had taken various other stands on issues regarding sports, such as the [[Kaepernick controversy]] and the [[Houston Astros sign stealing scandal]].{{#invoke:cite|web|date=February 18, 2020 |title=LeBron urges Manfred to 'listen to your players' |url=https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/28731746/lebron-james-calls-rob-manfred-handling-astros-scandal-fix-sake-sports |access-date=February 18, 2020 |publisher=[[ESPN]]|agency=Associated Press}}{{#invoke:cite|news|url=https://www.businessinsider.com/lebron-james-standing-national-anthem-nba-impact-2018-5|title=LeBron James' early-season declaration that he would stand for the national anthem reportedly had a big impact around the NBA|last=Davis|first=Scott|date=May 27, 2018|website=[[Business Insider]]|access-date=February 18, 2020}} In October 2023, in response to the [[October 7 attacks|October 7th attacks]], James voiced his support for Israel on Instagram.{{#invoke:cite|web|date=October 12, 2023 |title=LeBron James Voices Support For Israel, Denounces Hamas Attacks |url=https://www.tmz.com/2023/10/12/lebron-james-voices-support-for-israel-denounces-hamas-attacks/ |access-date=October 18, 2023 |publisher=[[TMZ]] |language=en}} His statement drew backlash for its omission of the Palestinians affected by the [[Gaza war|crisis the attacks provoked]], including from Palestinian-American leader and activist [[Omar Suleiman (imam)|Omar Suleiman]].{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Battaglino |first=Mike |date=October 12, 2023 |title=LeBron James gets crucified on social media after publicly expressing thoughts on Israel-Hamas war |url=https://lakersdaily.com/lakers-lebron-james-gets-crucified-on-social-media-after-publicly-expressing-thoughts-on-israel-hamas-war/ |access-date=October 18, 2023 |website=Lakers Daily |language=en-US}}{{Cite tweet |author=Dr. Omar Suleiman |user=omarsuleiman504 |title=As a man who claims to have recently read the autobiography of Malcolm X & produced a film on Muhammad Ali, this is utterly disgraceful. |number=1712279819205873714 |date=October 11, 2023 |access-date=October 18, 2023 |language=en}}{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Iqbal |first=Zainab |date=October 12, 2023 |title=US sports stars ignore Palestinians in pro-Israel posts, face fan backlash |url=https://www.middleeasteye.net/news/israel-palestine-war-us-sports-stars-ignore-palestinians-pro-israel-backlash |access-date=October 18, 2023 |website=Middle East Eye |language=en}} ====Presidential elections==== In June 2008, James donated $20,000 to a committee in support of Barack Obama for the [[2008 U.S. presidential election]].{{#invoke:cite|web|url=http://www.insidehoops.com/blog/?p=1529|title=Lebron donates cash to Obama|website=Inside Hoops|date=July 31, 2008|access-date=May 7, 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110522122453/http://www.insidehoops.com/blog/?p=1529|archive-date=May 22, 2011|url-status=dead}} Later that year, James gathered almost 20,000 people at the then-called [[Quicken Loans Arena]] for a viewing of Obama's 30-minute ''[[American Stories, American Solutions]]'' television advertisement.{{#invoke:cite|web|date=October 30, 2008 |title=Jay-Z, LeBron James get out vote for Obama |url=https://www.today.com/popculture/jay-z-lebron-james-get-out-vote-obama-1C9419780 |access-date=December 1, 2023 |website=Today.com|agency=Associated Press}} The advertisement was shown on a large screen above the stage, where [[Jay-Z]] later held a free concert. In November 2016, James endorsed and campaigned for [[Hillary Clinton]] for the [[2016 U.S. presidential election]].{{#invoke:cite|news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2016/nov/06/hillary-clinton-campaign-voter-turnout-swing-states|title=LeBron James praises 'President Hillary Clinton' at Cleveland rally|last=Siddiqui|first=Sabrina|date=November 6, 2016|work=The Guardian|access-date=March 22, 2019}} [[File:Gavin Newsom signs SB206 on The Shop 02.jpg|thumb|left|James with California governor [[Gavin Newsom]] on ''[[The Shop]]'', when Newsom signed the [[Fair Pay to Play Act]], in 2019.]] Following a [[Racism in the United States|racist]] incident at his Los Angeles home in 2017, James expressed that "being black in America is tough. We got a long way to go for us as a society and for us as African Americans until we feel equal in America."{{#invoke:cite|web|last=Ward-Henninger|first=Colin|title=Cavs' LeBron James responds to racist graffiti on house with powerful statement|url=https://www.cbssports.com/nba/news/cavs-lebron-james-responds-to-racist-graffiti-on-house-with-powerful-statement/|work=CBS Sports|date=May 31, 2017 |access-date=August 8, 2018}} Later on that year, in the aftermath of the [[Unite the Right rally]], James questioned the "[[Make America Great Again]]" slogan and said: "It's sad what's going on in Charlottesville. Is this the direction our country is heading? Make America Great Again huh? Our youth deserve better!!"{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Tsuji|first=Alysha|title=LeBron James saddened by Charlottesville: 'Make America Great Again huh?!'|url=https://ftw.usatoday.com/2017/08/lebron-james-charlottesville-sad-comment-twitter-politics-nba|date=August 13, 2017|newspaper=[[USA Today]]|access-date=August 8, 2018}} James also called President [[Donald Trump]] a "bum" after the president rescinded a White House invitation to Stephen Curry.{{#invoke:cite|news|last1=Bonesteel |first1=Matt |last2=Payne |first2=Marissa |date=September 23, 2017 |title=LeBron James sticks up for Stephen Curry, calls President Trump a 'bum' |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/early-lead/wp/2017/09/23/lebron-james-sticks-up-for-stephen-curry-calls-president-trump-a-bum/ |access-date=August 8, 2018}} During a 2018 interview with CNN journalist [[Don Lemon]], James then accused Trump of attempting to divide the country with sports, suggesting that "sports has never been something that divides people it's always been something that brings someone together." He declared that he would "never sit across from him. I'd sit across from Obama though."{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Kaskey-Blomain|first=Michael|date=July 31, 2018|title=LeBron James says he would never sit across from President Trump|url=https://247sports.com/nba/los-angeles-lakers/Article/LeBron-James-says-he-would-never-sit-across-from-President-Trump-120229451/|website=247 Sports|access-date=June 12, 2021}}{{cite magazine |first=Jack |last=Holmes |access-date=June 12, 2021 |title=LeBron James Neatly Dismissed Trump's Racial Divisiveness in a Must-See Interview |url=https://www.esquire.com/news-politics/a22602866/lebron-james-donald-trump-divisive-wouldn-t-sit-across/ |date=July 31, 2018 |magazine=Esquire}} In response, Trump tweeted: "LeBron James was just interviewed by the dumbest man on television, Don Lemon. He made LeBron look smart, which isn't easy to do."{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Stewart|first=Emily|access-date=June 12, 2021|title=Trump is insulting LeBron James' intelligence – and Don Lemon's – on Twitter|url=https://www.vox.com/2018/8/4/17650982/trump-lebron-james-tweet-don-lemon|date=August 4, 2018|publisher=[[Vox Media|Vox]]}} On August 19, 2020, James announced his intentions to support the [[Joe Biden 2020 presidential campaign]] and that of his running-mate [[Kamala Harris]] in advance of the [[2020 U.S. presidential election]].{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Folley|first=Aris|date=August 19, 2020|title=LeBron James says he plans to campaign for Biden and Harris|url=https://thehill.com/blogs/in-the-know/in-the-know/512770-lebron-james-says-he-plans-to-campaign-for-biden-and-harris|access-date=August 19, 2020|newspaper=[[The Hill (newspaper)|The Hill]]}} James spoke in support of the [[More Than a Vote]] movement and encouraged members of the African-American community to vote,{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Ramsay|first=George|date=August 18, 2020|url=https://edition.cnn.com/2020/08/18/sport/lebron-james-voter-suppression-nba-spt-intl/index.html|title=LeBron James speaks out against voter suppression with 'More Than A Vote' campaign|publisher=[[CNN]]|access-date=August 19, 2020}} saying, "People in our community have been just lied to for so many years. We have people that have had convictions in the past, that've been told they cannot vote because they got a conviction. That is voter suppression."{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Joseph|first=Cameron|date=August 19, 2020|title=LeBron James Is Fighting to Make Sure Felons Know They Can Vote|url=https://www.vice.com/en_us/article/5dz49z/lebron-james-is-fighting-to-make-sure-felons-know-they-can-vote|access-date=August 19, 2020|publisher=[[Vice Media]]}} On October 31, 2024, James announced his support for the [[Kamala Harris 2024 presidential campaign]] in the [[2024 U.S. presidential election]] in a video posted on social media.{{cite web |url=https://www.reuters.com/world/us/lebron-james-endorses-harris-president-says-choice-is-clear-2024-10-31/ |title=LeBron James endorses Harris for president, says 'choice is clear' |last=Singh |first=Kanishka |website=[[Reuters]] |date=October 31, 2024 |access-date=November 1, 2024}} ==Media figure and business interests== According to ''[[Forbes]]'', in May 2023, James became the first active NBA player to become a [[The World's Billionaires|billionaire]],{{#invoke:cite|web|url=https://www.forbes.com/sites/chasewithorn/2022/06/02/lebron-james-is-officially-a-billionaire/?sh=5981d1f1453e|title=LeBron James Is Officially A Billionaire|date=March 23, 2023|last=Peterson-Withorn|first=chase|work=Forbes|access-date=May 3, 2023}} making him one of the [[List of celebrities by net worth|richest celebrities]]. James' biography ''LeBron'' was published on April 11, 2023, by [[Jeff Benedict]]. The biography is based on three years of research and more than 250 interviews.{{Cite book |url=https://www.simonandschuster.com/books/LeBron/Jeff-Benedict/9781982110895 |title=LeBron |date=April 11, 2023 |isbn=978-1-9821-1089-5 |language=en |last1=Benedict |first1=Jeff |publisher=Avid Reader Press / Simon & Schuster}} ===Endorsements=== James has signed numerous endorsement contracts; some of the companies that he has done business with are [[AT&T]],{{#invoke:cite|web|url=https://www.forbes.com/sites/kurtbadenhausen/2020/03/02/att-taps-lebron-james-to-promote-its-new-streaming-service/|title=Lebron James Adds Multiyear AT&T Deal to his Endorsement Stable|last=Badenhausen|first=Kurt|date=March 2, 2020|website=[[Forbes]]|access-date=September 24, 2023}} [[Audemars Piguet]],{{Cite magazine |last=Adams |first=Ariel |date=September 27, 2013 |title=You Can Now Buy The $51,500 LeBron James Limited Edition Watch By Audemars Piguet |url=https://www.forbes.com/sites/arieladams/2013/09/27/you-can-now-buy-the-51500-lebron-james-limited-edition-watch-by-audemars-piguet/#178fa4d05739 |magazine=Forbes |access-date=May 27, 2017}} [[Beats Electronics|Beats by Dre]],{{#invoke:cite|news|last=Connelly |first=Tony |date=May 28, 2017 |title=Beats by Dre signs UFC star Conor McGregor to endorsement deal as it moves deeper into popular culture |work=The Drum |url=http://www.thedrum.com/news/2017/05/28/beats-dre-signs-ufc-star-conor-mcgregor-endorsement-deal-it-moves-deeper-popular |access-date=December 25, 2017}} [[Blaze Pizza]],{{#invoke:cite|web|url=https://www.businessinsider.com/how-blaze-pizza-convinced-lebron-james-to-leave-mcdonalds-2017-7|title=The fastest-growing pizza chain in America reveals how it lured Brands]],{{#invoke:cite|news|url=https://www.reuters.com/article/us-dunkinbrands/dunkin-aims-at-china-with-pork-donuts-lebron-james-idUSTRE8240HX20120305|title=Dunkin' aims at China with pork donuts, |date=March 30, 2017 |url=https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/19036468/lebron-james-partners-intel-appear-final-four-advertisement |access-date=November 5, 2024}} [[Kia]],{{#invoke:cite|web|url=https://kia.co.nz/about-kia/kia-news/lebron-james-announced-as-kias-newest-brand-ambassador/|title=Lebron James announced as Kia's newest Brand Ambassador|date=November |date=May 5, 2013 |title=Magic Johnson thinks LeBron doesn't have enough endorsements |url=http://ftw.usatoday.com/2013/05/magic-johnson-thinks-lebron-doesnt-have-enough-endorsements/ |access-date=June 9, 2013 |website=For The Win|publisher=USA Today}} [[PepsiCo]],{{#invoke:cite|web|url=https://www.cnbc.com/2021/05/13/lebron-james-on-pepsi-partnership-after-17-years-with-coca-cola-.html|title=Lebron James talks about his new Pepsi deal as he unveils first campaign with Mtn Dew Rise|last=Golden|first=Jessica|date=May 13, 2021|website=[[CNBC]]|access-date=September 24, 2023}} [[State Farm]],{{Cite news |date=February 13, 2008 |title=LeBron James enters partnership with State Farm |url=http://usatoday30.usatoday.com/money/advertising/2008-02-13-lebron-state-farm_N.htm |access-date=October 27, 2009 |work=USA Today|agency=Associated Press}} and [[Taco Bell]].{{cite web |last=Daus Venu |first=Marcus |date=June 28, 2023 |title=Taco Bell names LeBron James as global ambassador as it attempts to drop Taco Tuesday trademark
