== Honours == Carlsen won the [[Chess Oscar]]s from 2009 to 2013. The Chess Oscar, organised by the Russian chess magazine [[64 (magazine)|''64'']], was awarded to the year's best player according to a worldwide poll of leading chess critics, writers, and journalists, but it was no longer awarded after 2013, as ''64'' ceased publication.<ref>{{cite web |title=Chess Oscar winner Carlsen to be tested for doping |url=http://www.chessbase.com/newsdetail.asp?newsid=8613 |publisher=ChessBase News |date=6 November 2011 |access-date=16 December 2012}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Carlsen Gets His 4th Chess Oscar: 'I Think It's Well-Deserved' |url=http://chess-news.ru/en/node/12356 |publisher=chess-news.ru |date=13 June 2013 |access-date=13 June 2013 |archive-date=11 May 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200511100334/http://chess-news.ru/en/node/12356 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
The Norwegian tabloid ''[[Verdens Gang]]'' (VG) has awarded Carlsen the "[[Name of the Year]]" (''Årets navn'') twice, in 2009<ref>{{cite web |author=Strøm, Kristian Ole |title="Sjakkens Mozart" ble årets navn |trans-title="Mozart of Chess" became the name |url=http://www.vg.no/nyheter/innenriks/artikkel.php?artid=581096 |publisher=Verdens Gang |date=19 December 2009 |access-date=15 February 2013}}</ref> and 2013.<ref>Øyvind Askeland and Knut Arne Hansen (20 December 2013) [http://www.vg.no/sport/sjakk/artikkel.php?artid=10134026 Magnus Carlsen kåret til Årets navn] [[Verdens Gang|VG]]. Retrieved 21 December 2013 {{in lang|no}}</ref> VG also named him "Sportsman of the Year" in 2009,<ref>{{cite web |author=Strøm, Kristian Ole |title=Magnus (19) er landets største idrettsstjerne! ...men Carlsen skulle gjerne vært best i verden i fotball |trans-title=Magnus (19) is the country's biggest sports star! ... but Carlsen would like to be best in soccer |url=http://www.vg.no/sport/artikkel.php?artid=597047 |publisher=Verdens Gang |date=24 December 2009 |access-date=15 February 2013}}</ref> and he was additionally honored with the Open Class (Åpen klasse) and "Utøvernes pris" awards in 2013.<ref>{{Cite web |last=admin |date=2014-01-05 |title=Carlsen cleans up at sports gala |url=https://www.newsinenglish.no/2014/01/05/carlsen-cleans-up-at-sports-gala/ |access-date=2025-06-23 |website=Norway's News in English — www.newsinenglish.no |language=en-US}}</ref> Carlsen has also won the ''{{interlanguage link|Folkets idrettspris|no}}'', a people's choice award for "Sportsman of the Year" from the Norwegian newspaper ''[[Dagbladet]]'', in 2009,<ref>{{cite web |author=Skjervum, Eivind |title=Magnus Carlsen (19) suveren vinner av Folkets Idrettspris |trans-title=Magnus Carlsen (19) clear winner of People's Sports Prize |url=http://www.vg.no/sport/artikkel.php?artid=597047 |work=Dagbladet |date=24 December 2009 |access-date=15 February 2013}}</ref> 2012,<ref>{{cite web |author=Kvam, Lars Hojem |title=Magnus Carlsen fikk flere stemmer enn de tre neste til sammen |trans-title=Magnus Carlsen got more votes than the next three in comparison |url=http://www.dagbladet.no/2012/12/23/sport/folkets_idrettspris/karing/magnus_carlsen/sjakk/24957367/ |work=Dagbladet |date=24 December 2012 |access-date=15 February 2013}}</ref> 2013,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Burheim |first=Mads Gudim |date=2013-12-28 |title=- Når jeg taper, om ikke jeg hater motstanderen, så er det ikke langt ifra |url=https://www.dagbladet.no/sport/nar-jeg-taper-om-ikke-jeg-hater-motstanderen-sa-er-det-ikke-langt-ifra/61937751 |access-date=2025-06-23 |website=www.dagbladet.no |language=nb-NO}}</ref> 2014,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Haarstad |first=Thomas |date=2014-12-31 |title=Mente andre fortjente prisen mer, men Carlsen er igjen folkets favoritt |url=https://www.dagbladet.no/sport/mente-andre-fortjente-prisen-mer-men-carlsen-er-igjen-folkets-favoritt/60938085 |access-date=2025-06-23 |website=www.dagbladet.no |language=nb-NO}}</ref> and 2016.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Baardsen |first=Joachim |date=2016-12-24 |title=Carlsen: - VM-tittelen er ikke verdt noe hvis jeg ikke kan bevise at jeg er best i verden |url=https://www.dagbladet.no/sport/carlsen---vm-tittelen-er-ikke-verdt-noe-hvis-jeg-ikke-kan-bevise-at-jeg-er-best-i-verden/66558641 |access-date=2025-06-23 |website=www.dagbladet.no |language=nb-NO}}</ref>
In 2011, he was awarded the [[Peer Gynt Prize]], a Norwegian prize awarded annually to "a person or institution that has achieved distinction in society".<ref>{{cite web |title=Chess star wins prestigious award |url=http://www.newsinenglish.no/2011/03/21/chess-star-wins-prestigious-award/ |publisher=Views and News from Norway |date=21 March 2011 |access-date=16 December 2012}}</ref>
In 2013, [[Time (magazine)|''Time'']] magazine named Carlsen one of the [[Time 100|100 most influential people in the world]].<ref>{{cite magazine |author=Kasparov, Garry |title=The 2013 TIME 100: Magnus Carlsen |magazine=Time |url=https://time100.time.com/2013/04/18/time-100/slide/magnus-carlsen/ |date=18 April 2013 |access-date=18 April 2013}}</ref>
In 2020, ''[[Forbes]]'' named Carlsen the highest-earning [[esports]] player in the world.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Gardner |first=Matt |title=Esports' Biggest Winners In 2020: Prizes Plummet, Chess Leads Pack |url=https://www.forbes.com/sites/mattgardner1/2021/01/12/the-2020-esports-earnings-table-prizes-plummet-chess-wins-big/ |access-date=2025-04-15 |website=Forbes |language=en}}</ref>
Carlsen was named Player of the Year at the inaugural [[Chess.com]] Awards in 2020.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Team (CHESScom) |first=Chess com |date=2020-12-31 |title=2020 Chess.com Award Winners |url=https://www.chess.com/article/view/the-2020-chess-com-awards |access-date=2025-06-23 |website=Chess.com |language=en-US}}</ref> He received the same honor again in 2021 and 2023.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Pinhata (PedroPinhata) |first=Pedro |date=2022-01-07 |title=2021 Chess.com Awards Winners |url=https://www.chess.com/news/view/2021-chesscom-awards-winners |access-date=2025-06-23 |website=Chess.com |language=en-US}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Team (CHESScom) |first=Chess com |date=2024-01-03 |title=Announcing The 2023 Chess.com Awards Winners |url=https://www.chess.com/news/view/2023-chesscom-awards-winners |access-date=2025-06-23 |website=Chess.com |language=en-US}}</ref>
At the [[Centennial|centenary]] celebration of the [[FIDE|International Chess Federation]] in 2024, Carlsen accepted the FIDE award for Greatest of All Time.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Svensen (TarjeiJS) |first=Tarjei J. |date=2024-09-20 |title=Carlsen Accepts G.O.A.T. Award, Calls On FIDE To Uphold Restrictions On Russia And Belarus |url=https://www.chess.com/news/view/carlsen-accepts-goat-award-urges-fide-to-reject-proposal-on-russia-belarus |access-date=2025-06-23 |website=Chess.com |language=en-US}}</ref>
== Rating == [[File:Magnus Carlsen Elo february 2023.svg|thumb|Carlsen's Elo rating evolution from 2002 to 2023|350px]] In the January 2006 FIDE rankings, at the age of 15 years and 32 days, Carlsen attained a 2625 [[Elo rating system|Elo rating]], which made him the youngest person to surpass 2600<ref>{{cite web |title=Carlsen's Conquests – at 22 he has broken all records |author=von Keitz, Michael |url=http://www.chessbase.com/newsdetail.asp?newsid=8845 |publisher=ChessBase News |date=12 February 2013 |access-date=12 February 2013}}</ref> (the record has since been broken by [[Wesley So]], [[Wei Yi]], [[John M. Burke]], and [[Yağız Kaan Erdoğmuş]]<ref>{{cite web |title=Erdogmus Smashes Prestigious Rating Record, Receives Hero's Welcome Back Home |author=Svensen, Tarjei J.|url=https://www.chess.com/news/view/yagiz-kaan-erdogmus-smashes-record-as-youngest-ever-2600-player-receives-heroes-welcome-in-turkey|publisher=Chess.com News |date=1 October 2024 |access-date=5 December 2024}}</ref>). In the July 2007 FIDE rankings, at the age of 16 years and 213 days, Carlsen attained a 2710 Elo rating, which made him the youngest person to surpass 2700 (the record has since been broken by Wei Yi).<ref>{{cite web |title=Anand tops the July 2007 FIDE ratings |url=http://www.chessbase.com/newsdetail.asp?newsid=3969 |publisher=ChessBase News |date=3 July 2007 |access-date=5 January 2013}}</ref>
On 5 September 2008, after winning in round 4 of the [[Bilbao Chess Masters Final#First Grand Slam Masters Final (2008)|Bilbao Masters]], Carlsen, aged 17 years and 280 days old, briefly became {{Numero|1}} on the unofficial [[Live Elo rating|live ratings]] list.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.chessbase.com/newsdetail.asp?newsid=4892 |title=Bilbao R4: Topalov topples Anand, Carlsen No. 1 in the world |publisher=ChessBase News |date=5 September 2008 |access-date=3 January 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://chess.liverating.org/toplist.php?id=2008090501# |title=Live Rating on 5 Sep 2008 |publisher=Chess.liverating.org |date=17 August 2011 |access-date=30 November 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110818103606/http://chess.liverating.org/ |archive-date=18 August 2011 |url-status=dead}}</ref> Carlsen's victory in the [[Pearl Spring chess tournament#2009|2009 Nanjing Pearl tournament]] raised his FIDE rating to 2801, making him, aged 18 years and 336 days, at the time the youngest player ever to surpass 2800<ref name="ChessBase 10 October 2009" /> (a record subsequently broken by Alireza Firouzja).<ref name="Alireza2800">{{cite web |last1=Levin |first1=Anthony |title=Alireza Firouzja Youngest Chess Player Ever To Break 2800 |url=https://www.chess.com/news/view/alireza-firouzja-youngest-chess-player-ever-to-break-2800 |website=chess.com |date=22 November 2021 |access-date=22 November 2021}}</ref> The youngest before Carlsen to achieve this feat was [[Vladimir Kramnik]] at the age of 25,<ref>{{cite web|url=https://translate.google.com/translate?hl=en&sl=no&u=http://www.vg.no/sport/artikkel.php%3Fartid%3D574885&ei=OeP-SsLdNIWXtgeoy-WRDg&sa=X&oi=translate&ct=result&resnum=2&ved=0CAsQ7gEwAQ&prev=/search%3Fq%3Dhttp://www.vg.no/sport/artikkel.php%253Fartid%253D574885%26hl%3Den%26client%3Dfirefox-a%26rls%3Dorg.mozilla:en-US:official%26hs%3DAs1 |title=Dad Carlsen – This has been a dream |access-date=31 January 2012}}</ref> and up until this point only Kasparov, Topalov, Kramnik, and Anand had achieved a 2800+ rating.<ref>{{cite web |title=FIDE ratings: Anand now number one |url=http://www.chessbase.com/newsdetail.asp?newsid=6784 |publisher=ChessBase News |date=1 November 2010 |access-date=6 January 2013}}</ref>
The FIDE rankings in January 2010 recorded Carlsen's rating at 2810, which made him {{Numero|1}} rated player in the world.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://ratings.fide.com/individual_calculations.phtml?idnumber=1503014&rating_period=2010-01-01 |title=Magnus Carlsen: Individual Calculations January 2010 |publisher=FIDE |access-date=31 January 2012 |archive-date=17 March 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150317101551/http://ratings.fide.com/individual_calculations.phtml?idnumber=1503014&rating_period=2010-01-01 |url-status=dead }}</ref> This meant that Carlsen became, at the age of 19 years and 32 days, the youngest ever [[List of FIDE chess world number ones|world {{Numero|1}}]], as well as the first player from a Western nation to reach the top of the FIDE rankings since Bobby Fischer in 1971.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.google.com/hostednews/afp/article/ALeqM5g1QmAdxADzyQCWyLLzs2N63owZEA |title=Norwegian Carlsen is youngest to top chess rankings |agency=Agence France-Presse |date=1 January 2010 |access-date=16 February 2013 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140227105136/http://www.google.com/hostednews/afp/article/ALeqM5g1QmAdxADzyQCWyLLzs2N63owZEA |archive-date=27 February 2014 |url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |author=Moskwa, Wojciech |url=https://www.reuters.com/article/idUSTRE5BT17H20091230 |title=Norwegian teenager to be crowned new chess king |work=Reuters |date=30 December 2009 |access-date=3 January 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite magazine |url=http://www.time.com/time/magazine/article/0,9171,1950683,00.html |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100105160755/http://www.time.com/time/magazine/article/0,9171,1950683,00.html |url-status=dead |archive-date=5 January 2010 |title=A Bold Opening for Chess Player Magnus Carlsen |magazine=Time |date=11 January 2010 |author=Harell, Ebden |access-date=31 January 2012}}</ref>
Carlsen lost the {{Numero|1}} ranking in November 2010, regained it in January 2011, lost it again in March 2011, and regained it in the July 2011 list. As of August 2025, he has [[List of FIDE chess world number ones|held the number one position continously]] since then.
On the January 2013 FIDE rankings, Carlsen reached 2861, thus surpassing Garry Kasparov's 2851 record from July 1999.<ref name=wic-carlsenwinslondon2012>{{cite news |title=Carlsen wins London Chess Classic and takes Kasparov's record |author=Crowther, Mark |url=http://www.theweekinchess.com/chessnews/events/4th-london-chess-classic-20121/carlsen-wins-london-chess-classic-and-takes-kasparovs-record |work=[[The Week in Chess]]|date=10 December 2012 |access-date=3 November 2012}}</ref><ref name="chessvibesDec2012"/> In the May 2014 rankings, Carlsen achieved an [[Comparison of top chess players throughout history#Elo system|all-time high record]] of 2882,<ref>{{cite web|url =http://ratings.fide.com/id.phtml?event=1503014|title =Rating Progress Chart – Carlsen, Magnus (NOR)|author =Staff writer(s)|publisher =FIDE|date =May 2014}}</ref> with a peak of 2889 on the live ratings list achieved on 21 April 2014.<ref>{{cite web|url =https://2700chess.com/|title =Highest Ever Rankings|author =Staff writer(s)|publisher =2700chess.com|date =21 April 2014}}</ref> In August 2019 he equalled his peak FIDE rating of 2882.<ref>{{cite web|url =https://en.chessbase.com/post/fide-ratings-2019-8|title =August ratings: Carlsen at all-time high|first =Macauley|last =Peterson|website =ChessBase|date =1 August 2019}}</ref>
{{As of|2025|07|post=,}} Carlsen is also ranked {{Numero|1}} in the FIDE rapid rating list with a rating of 2819, and {{Numero|1}} in the FIDE blitz rating list with a rating of 2883.<ref>{{cite web |title=FIDE Ratings |url=https://ratings.fide.com/top_lists.phtml?list=open |website=ratings.fide.com |access-date=12 July 2025}}</ref>
== Playing style == Carlsen had an aggressive style of play as a youth,<ref>{{cite web |author=Barden, Leonard |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2006/jan/28/chess.gdnsport3 |title=Chess: Barden on chess | Sport |work=The Guardian |date=28 January 2006 |access-date=3 January 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |author=Pein, Malcolm |url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/culture/chess/malcolmpein/3654195/Dangers-of-over-optimism.html# |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130309182629/http://www.telegraph.co.uk/culture/chess/malcolmpein/3654195/Dangers-of-over-optimism.html |archive-date=9 March 2013 |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |title=Dangers of over-optimism |work=The Daily Telegraph |date=31 July 2006 |access-date=3 January 2013}}</ref> and, according to Simen Agdestein, his play was characterised by "a fearless readiness to offer material for activity".<ref>Agdestein (2004), p. 45.</ref> As he matured, Carlsen found that this risky playing style was not as well suited against the elite of the chess world. When he started playing in top tournaments, he had trouble getting much out of the opening. To progress, Carlsen's style became more universal, capable of handling all sorts of positions well. He opens with both [[Queen's Pawn Game|1.d4]] and [[King's Pawn Game|1.e4]], as well as [[English Opening|1.c4]], and, on occasion, [[Zukertort Opening|1.Nf3]], thus making it harder for opponents to prepare against him and reducing the effect of computer analysis.<ref name=CNRU>{{cite web|title=Magnus Carlsen: "I Love the Middlegame, Because There You Get Pure Chess"|url=http://chess-news.ru/en/node/19178|website=Chess-News|date=9 October 2023}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |url=http://www.dagsavisen.no/kultur/article401031.ece |title=Magnus Carlsens system |author=Grønn, Atle |date=27 February 2009 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110716170211/http://www.dagsavisen.no/kultur/article401031.ece |archive-date=16 July 2011 |publisher=Dagsavisen |language=no |access-date=29 November 2010}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.chessgames.com/perl/explorer?pid=52948&side=white |title=Chess Opening Explorer |publisher=Chessgames.com |access-date=3 January 2013}}</ref> He said in 2015 that the [[chess middlegame|middlegame]] is his favourite part of the game as it comes down to "pure chess".<ref name=CNRU/>
In a 2016 interview, [[Anish Giri]] said: "Magnus and I are very close in terms of style, but in our approach to the game we're total opposites. Magnus tries to put the accent only on play, getting away from preparation, but for me preparation plays an enormous role."<ref>{{cite web|url =https://chess24.com/en/read/news/giri-magnus-and-i-are-very-close-in-terms-of-style|title =Giri: "Magnus and I are very close in terms of style"|website =chess24.com|first =Colin|last =McGourty|date =21 March 2016|access-date=14 December 2017}}</ref>
{{quote box | quote = [Carlsen] has been known to say that he isn't all that interested in opening preparation; his main forte is the middlegame, in which he manages to outplay many of his opponents with positional means. ... Carlsen's repertoire is aimed at avoiding an early crisis in the game. He invariably aims for middlegames that lend themselves to a strategic approach. | source=[[Jan Timman]], 2012<ref>{{cite journal |author=Timman, Jan |title=Is modern-day chess really all about preparation? |journal=New in Chess |year=2012 |issue=5 |pages=98–101}}</ref> | align = right | width = 25em | bgcolor = #c6dbf7 | salign = right }}
Garry Kasparov, who coached Carlsen from 2009 to 2010, said that Carlsen has a [[Positional chess|positional style]] similar to that of past world champions such as [[Anatoly Karpov]], [[José Raúl Capablanca]], and [[Vasily Smyslov]], rather than the {{chessgloss|tactician|tactical style}} of [[Alexander Alekhine]], [[Mikhail Tal]], and Kasparov himself.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.chessvibes.com/reports/kasparov-on-coaching-carlsen/ |title=Kasparov on coaching Carlsen |publisher=Chessvibes |date=11 September 2009 |access-date=3 January 2013 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130128122643/http://www.chessvibes.com/reports/kasparov-on-coaching-carlsen |archive-date=28 January 2013 |url-status=dead}}</ref> In a 2013 interview, [[Peter Heine Nielsen]] said: "The days of big novelties are over, and that fits Magnus' style well."<ref>{{cite web|url =http://www.thechessmind.net/blog/2013/4/27/a-short-interview-with-peter-heine-nielsen.html|title =A Short Interview With Peter Heine Nielsen|website =The Chess Mind Blog|first =Dennis|last =Monokroussos|date =27 April 2013|access-date=14 December 2017}}</ref> According to Carlsen, however, he does not have any preferences in playing style.<ref name=a>{{cite web |url=http://www.chessbase.com/newsdetail.asp?newsid=7778 |title=Magnus Carlsen – 'I don't quite fit into the usual schemes' |publisher=ChessBase News |date=22 December 2011 |access-date=9 September 2013 |archive-date=9 November 2012 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121109162157/http://chessbase.com/newsdetail.asp?newsid=7778 |url-status=live }}</ref> Kasparov said in 2013 that "Carlsen is a combination of Karpov [and] [[Bobby Fischer|Fischer]]. He gets his positions [and] then never lets go of that bulldog bite. Exhausting for opponents."<ref>{{cite web |author=Kasparov, Garry |url=https://twitter.com/Kasparov63/status/402222989321568256 |title=Kasparov63: Carlsen is a combination of ... |publisher=Twitter |date=17 November 2013 |access-date=19 November 2013}}</ref>{{Primary source inline|date=November 2018}} Carlsen has also stated that he follows in the traditions of Karpov and Fischer, but additionally mentions [[Reuben Fine]] as a player who "was doing in chess similar to what I am doing".<ref name="chess.com">{{cite web |author=Doggers, Peter |title=Anand-Carlsen Match Only a Week Away |url=http://www.chess.com/news/anand-carlsen-match-only-a-week-away-9074 |publisher=[[Chess.com]] |date=31 October 2014 |access-date=21 November 2014 |archive-date=2 December 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141202124804/http://www.chess.com/news/anand-carlsen-match-only-a-week-away-9074 |url-status=dead }}</ref>
Anand said of Carlsen in 2012: "Magnus has an incredible innate sense. ... The majority of ideas occur to him absolutely naturally. He's also very flexible, he knows all the structures and he can play almost any position." He also compared Carlsen to [[Boris Spassky]] in his prime, and stated that "Magnus can literally do almost everything."<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.chessintranslation.com/2012/05/anands-whychess-interview/ |title=Anand's WhyChess interview |publisher=Chess in Translation |date=10 May 2012 |access-date=2 January 2013}}</ref> Kasparov expressed similar sentiments: "[Carlsen] has the ability to correctly evaluate any position, which only Karpov could boast of before him."<ref>{{cite web |author=Mishanp |url=http://www.chessintranslation.com/2011/01/kasparovs-verdict-on-carlsens-withdrawal/ |title=Kasparov's verdict on Carlsen's withdrawal |publisher=Chessintranslation.com |date=9 January 2011 |access-date=3 January 2013}}</ref> When asked in a 2016 interview whether Carlsen's style resembles his own, Karpov answered: "It is quite possible. He grew up when I was in power, and perhaps he studied my games. He can convert a minimal advantage into a real one."<ref>{{cite web |url=http://reklamaconnect.com/Post/anatolij-karpov---igrok-s-bolshoj-bukvy |script-title=ru:Анатолий Карпов – Игрок с большой буквы!|website =ReklamaConnect.com |date=15 April 2016 |language=ru |access-date=14 December 2017}}</ref>
In a 2012 interview, Vladimir Kramnik stated that Carlsen's "excellent physical shape" was a contributing factor to his success against other top players as it prevents "psychological lapses", which enables him to maintain a high standard of play over long games and at the end of tournaments, when the energy levels of others have dropped.<ref>{{cite journal |author=Geuzendam, Dirk Jan ten |title=Vladimir Kramnik: 'I like playing chess now probably more than ever' |journal=New in Chess |year=2012 |issue=1 |pages=32–41|author-link=Geuzendam, Dirk Jan ten}}</ref> Levon Aronian said in 2015: "Magnus' main secret is his composure and the absence of any soul-searching after mistakes during a game."<ref>{{cite web |url=https://chess24.com/en/read/news/aronian-magnus-main-secret-is-his-composure |title=Aronian: "Magnus' main secret is his composure" |website=[[Chess24.com]] |first=Colin |last=McGourty |date=2 February 2015 |access-date=14 December 2017}}</ref> [[Tyler Cowen]] gave a point of view on Carlsen's playing style: "Carlsen is demonstrating one of his most feared qualities, namely his 'nettlesomeness,' to use a term coined for this purpose by [[Kenneth W. Regan|Ken Regan]], of the [[University at Buffalo]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://cse.buffalo.edu/~regan/ |title=Kenneth W. Regan |publisher=University at Buffalo, [[The State University of New York]] |work=CSE Department |access-date=March 22, 2019}}</ref> Using computer analysis, you can measure which players do the most to cause their opponents to make mistakes. Carlsen has the highest nettlesomeness score by this metric, because his creative moves pressure the other player and open up a lot of room for mistakes. In contrast, a player such as Kramnik plays a high percentage of very accurate moves, and of course he is very strong, but those moves are in some way calmer and they are less likely to induce mistakes in response."<ref>{{cite web |url=http://marginalrevolution.com/marginalrevolution/2013/11/nettlesomeness-and-the-first-half-of-the-carlsen-anand-match.html |title=Nettlesomeness, and the first half of the Carlsen-Anand match |first =Tyler|last = Cowen | author-link =Tyler Cowen|publisher=MarginalRevolution |date=16 November 2013 |access-date=25 January 2015}}</ref>
Carlsen's endgame prowess has been singled out for praise.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.chessbase.com/newsdetail.asp?newsid=8257 |title=Chess News – CBM training: Carlsen in Capablanca's footsteps |date=17 June 2012 |publisher=ChessBase News |access-date=3 January 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |last1=Naroditsky |first1=Daniel |title=Magnus Carlsen's Best Endgame Wins |url=https://www.chess.com/article/view/magnus-carlsens-best-endgame-wins |website=Chess.com |date=27 February 2015 |access-date=7 August 2023}}</ref><ref>{{cite book |author=Karolyi, Tibor |title=Endgame Virtuoso Magnus Carlsen: His Extraordinary Skills Uncovered and Explained |year=2018 |publisher=New in Chess |isbn=9789056917777 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=AxFqDwAAQBAJ}}</ref> [[Jon Speelman]], analysing several of Carlsen's endgames from the 2012 London Classic (in particular, his wins against McShane, Aronian, and Adams), described what he calls the "Carlsen effect": <blockquote>... through the combined force of his skill and no less important his reputation, he drives his opponents into errors. ... He plays on for ever<!-- written as two words in source-->, calmly, methodically and, perhaps most importantly of all, without fear: calculating superbly, with very few outright mistakes and a good proportion of the "very best" moves. This makes him a monster and makes many opponents wilt.<ref>{{cite journal |author=Speelman, Jon |title=Carlsen's Endgame Magic |journal=New in Chess |year=2013 |issue=1 |pages=33–41}}</ref></blockquote>
An artificial intelligence approach, designed by Jean-Marc Alliot of the ''[[Institut de recherche en informatique de Toulouse]]'' ("Toulouse Computer Science Research Institute"), which compares chess grandmaster moves against that of [[Stockfish (chess)|Stockfish]]—a chess engine that outperforms all human opponents—rated Carlsen as the [[Comparison of top chess players throughout history|best player of all time]] as he had the highest probability among all World Chess Champions to play the moves that Stockfish suggested.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2017/04/170425110954.htm|website=[[Science Daily]]|publisher=[[CNRS]]|date=25 April 2017|title=When artificial intelligence evaluates chess champions}}</ref>
Carlsen also plays speed chess online under many screen names. He is known for meme openings such as the [[Bongcloud Attack]] and exceptionally strong play, despite banter and gags.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Holmes |first1=Joe |title=The World's Best Chess Player Has a Second Life as an Online Speed Chess Prankster |url=https://slate.com/culture/2020/02/magnus-carlsen-speed-chess-drdrunkenstein-pseudonyms-twitch-youtube.html |website=Slate Magazine |language=en |date=21 February 2020}}</ref>
== Notable games == * Carlsen vs. [[Garry Kasparov]], Reykjavík Rapid (2004), [[Queen's Gambit Declined]]: [[Cambridge Springs Defense]] (D52), {{chessAN|½–½}}.{{refn|group=games|{{cite web |url=http://www.chessgames.com/perl/chessgame?gid=1279168 |title=Carlsen vs. Garry Kasparov, Reykjavík Rapid (2004) |website=[[Chessgames.com]]}}}} At the age of just 13 years, Carlsen had serious winning chances in a [[Fast chess|rapid game]] against Kasparov,<ref name=h /> who was ranked {{Numero|1}} in the world at that time.<ref>{{cite web |title=Top 100 Players January 2004 FIDE Top players archive |url=http://ratings.fide.com/toparc.phtml?cod=57 |access-date=27 May 2013 |publisher=FIDE |archive-date=15 January 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160115034230/http://ratings.fide.com/toparc.phtml?cod=57 |url-status=dead }}</ref> * Carlsen vs. [[Veselin Topalov]], [[M-Tel Masters]] (2009), [[Semi-Slav Defense]]: General (D43), {{chessAN|1–0}}.{{refn|group=games|{{cite web |url=http://www.chessgames.com/perl/chessgame?gid=1544430 |title=Carlsen vs. Veselin Topalov, M-Tel Masters (2009) |website=[[Chessgames.com]]}}}} This was Carlsen's first win against a 2800+ player.<ref>{{cite journal |author=Geuzendam, Dirk Jan ten |title=Alexey Shirov back on board |journal=New in Chess |year=2009 |issue=4 |page=17}}</ref> * Carlsen vs. [[Boris Gelfand]], [[Tal Memorial]] (2011), [[Slav Defense]]: Quiet Variation. [[Emil Schallopp|Schallopp]] Defense (D12), 1–0.<ref>{{cite web | title=Tal Memorial Rd2 – Carlsen beats Gelfand | website=ChessBase | url=https://en.chessbase.com/post/tal-memorial-rd2-carlsen-beats-gelfand | date=18 November 2011 | access-date=5 June 2018}}</ref>{{refn|group=games|{{cite web |url=http://www.chessgames.com/perl/chessgame?gid=1647771 |title=Carlsen vs. Boris Gelfand, Tal Memorial (2011) |website=[[Chessgames.com]]}}}} The {{Numero|1}} Israeli player creates a seemingly decisive rook invasion into White's {{chessgloss|back rank}}, but Carlsen vanquishes the threats. Carlsen called it "one of the most interesting games I have played in recent times".<ref>{{cite journal |author=Geuzendam, Dirk Jan ten |title=Magnus Carlsen perseveres and prevails in Pashkov House |journal=New in Chess |year=2011 |issue=8 |page=23}}</ref> * Carlsen vs. [[Hikaru Nakamura]], [[London Chess Classic]] (2011), [[Italian Game]]: Classical Variation. [[Giuoco Pianissimo]] (C53), 1–0.{{refn|group=games|{{cite web |url=http://www.chessgames.com/perl/chessgame?gid=1649097 |title=Carlsen vs. Hikaru Nakamura, London Chess Classic (2011) |website=[[Chessgames.com]]}}}} Facing the {{Numero|1}} American player, Carlsen sacrifices [[The exchange (chess)|the exchange]] to achieve a winning position in an otherwise near-equal game.{{citation needed|date=April 2025}} * Carlsen vs. [[Viswanathan Anand]], [[Bilbao Chess Masters Final|Bilbao Masters]] (2012), [[Sicilian Defense]]: [[Sicilian Defence#Moscow Variation|Canal Attack]]. Main Line (B52), 1–0.{{refn|group=games|{{cite web |url=http://www.chessgames.com/perl/chessgame?gid=1693032 |title=Carlsen vs. Viswanathan Anand, Bilbao Masters (2012) |website=[[Chessgames.com]]}}}} Playing against the then-World Champion, Carlsen sacrifices a pawn to leave Black with a {{chessgloss|cramped}} position, which leads to Anand's {{chessgloss|resignation}} at move 30. Carlsen stated in 2013 that he considers this game to be one of his very best.<ref>{{cite journal |title=NIC's Café |journal=New in Chess |year=2013 |issue=1 |page=7}}</ref> * Carlsen vs. [[Li Chao (chess player)|Li Chao]], [[Qatar Masters Open|Qatar Masters]] (2015), [[Neo-Grünfeld Defence]]: Goglidze Attack (D70), 1–0.{{refn|group=games|{{cite web |url=http://www.chessgames.com/perl/chessgame?gid=1811793 |title=Carlsen vs. Li Chao, Qatar Masters (2015) |website=[[Chessgames.com]]}}}} Playing against one of the leading Chinese [[Grandmaster (chess)|GM]]s, Carlsen finds a winning attack by sacrificing his queen in a {{chessgloss|sharp}} position.{{citation needed|date=April 2025}} * Carlsen vs. [[Ian Nepomniachtchi]], [[Carlsen versus Nepomniachtchi, World Chess Championship 2021, Game 6|2021 World Championship game 6]] (2021), [[Queen's Pawn Game|Queen's Pawn Game, Symmetrical Variation, Pseudo-Catalan]], 1–0. Game 6 was a 136-move win for Carlsen that lasted 7 hours 45 minutes. {{As of|2025}}, it is the longest game in the history of the World Chess Championship, surpassing the previous record, a 124-move draw in game 5 of the [[World Chess Championship 1978]] between [[Anatoly Karpov]] and [[Viktor Korchnoi]].<ref>{{Cite news |date=2021-12-04 |title=After 136 moves, Magnus Carlsen wins the longest game in World Chess Championship history |url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-12-04/magnus-carlsen-wins-longest-game-world-chess-history/100674464 |access-date=2025-06-23 |work=ABC News |language=en-AU}}</ref>
== Business endeavours == [[File:Magnus Carlsen 2013-05-24 001.jpg|thumb|upright|Carlsen in May 2013]] Carlsen modelled for [[G-Star Raw]]'s Fall/Winter 2010 advertising campaign along with American actress [[Liv Tyler]]. The campaign was shot by Dutch film director and photographer [[Anton Corbijn]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.chessbase.com/newsdetail.asp?newsid=6485 |title=Magnus Carlsen's new career: fashion model! |publisher=ChessBase News |date=7 July 2010 |access-date=3 January 2013}}</ref> The campaign was coordinated with the RAW World Chess Challenge in New York, an event in which Carlsen played an online team of global chess players, who voted on moves suggested by [[Maxime Vachier-Lagrave]], [[Hikaru Nakamura]] and [[Judit Polgár]]. Carlsen, playing white, won in 43 moves.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.chessbase.com/newsdetail.asp?newsid=6666 |title=Magnus pounds The World in RAW Chess Challenge |publisher=ChessBase News |date=12 September 2010 |access-date=3 January 2013}}</ref> In February 2014, he appeared in G-Star Raw's Spring/Summer 2014 campaign along with actress and model [[Lily Cole]].<ref>{{cite news |author=Doggers, Peter |date=17 February 2014 |title=Magnus Carlsen's YouTube Channel And New G-Star Campaign Video |url=http://www.chess.com/news/magnus-carlsen-youtube-channel-g-star-video-upcoming-events-8577 |publisher=Chess.com |access-date=17 February 2014 |archive-date=7 October 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151007040024/http://www.chess.com/news/magnus-carlsen-youtube-channel-g-star-video-upcoming-events-8577 |url-status=dead }}</ref>
Film director [[J. J. Abrams]] offered Carlsen a role in the movie ''[[Star Trek Into Darkness]]'' as "a chess player from the future", but Carlsen was unable to get a [[work permit]] in time for shooting.<ref>{{cite web |title=Magnus couldn't make it to Star Trek 2 |url=http://www.chessbase.com/newsdetail.asp?newsid=8070 |publisher=ChessBase News |date=11 April 2012 |access-date=3 January 2012}}</ref> In 2012, Carlsen was featured in a ''[[60 Minutes]]'' segment<ref>{{cite web |title=Mozart of Chess – Magnus Carlsen on CBS 60 Minutes |url=http://www.chessbase.com/newsdetail.asp?newsid=7933 |publisher=ChessBase News |date=20 February 2012 |access-date=8 January 2013}}</ref> and appeared as a guest on ''[[The Colbert Report]]''.<ref>{{cite web |title=Magnus Carlsen defeats Stephen Colbert 2–0 |url=http://www.chessbase.com/newsdetail.asp?newsid=8103 |publisher=ChessBase News |date=25 April 2012 |access-date=8 January 2012}}</ref> He was also interviewed by [[Rainn Wilson]] for [[SoulPancake]] in 2013.<ref>{{cite web |title=SoulPancake interview with Magnus Carlsen |url=http://www.chessbase.com/newsdetail.asp?newsid=8830 |publisher=ChessBase News |date=7 February 2013 |access-date=7 February 2013}}</ref>
{{As of|2012}}, Carlsen is the only active chess professional with a full-time [[talent manager|manager]]; [[Espen Agdestein]], a [[FIDE Master]] and brother of Carlsen's former trainer [[Simen Agdestein]], began working as an [[talent agent|agent]] for Carlsen in late 2008.<ref>{{cite web |title=Agdestein, Espen FIDE Chess Profile |url=http://ratings.fide.com/card.phtml?event=1500201 |publisher=FIDE |access-date=10 September 2013}}</ref> Agdestein's work consisted initially of finding sponsors and negotiating media contacts but, since 2011, he has taken over management tasks formerly performed by Carlsen's father Henrik.<ref>{{cite journal |author=Geuzendam, Dirk Jan ten |title=Interview: Managing Magnus |journal=New in Chess |year=2012 |issue=5 |pages=44–48}}</ref> According to ''The New York Times'', Carlsen earned US$1.2 million in 2012, the bulk of which was from sponsorships.<ref name="Moss 2013">{{cite news |author=Lyall, Sarah |title=Shrewd marketing moves for top-ranked chess player |newspaper=The New York Times |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2013/04/08/sports/shrewd-marketing-moves-for-top-ranked-chess-player-magnus-carlsen.html?pagewanted=1&_r=1&hp& |date=8 April 2013 |access-date=8 April 2013}}</ref>
In August 2013, Carlsen became an ambassador for [[Nordic Semiconductor]],<ref>{{cite news |url=http://www.4-traders.com/NORDIC-SEMICONDUCTOR-ASA-1413213/news/Nordic-Semiconductor-ASA-Magnus-Carlsen-the-best-player-in-chess-history-becomes-an-ambassador-f-17186870/ |title=Nordic Semiconductor ASA : Magnus Carlsen, the best player in chess history, becomes an ambassador for Nordic Semiconductor in three-year sponsorship deal... |publisher=4-traders.com |date=13 August 2013 |access-date=17 August 2013}}</ref> and in November was selected as one of the "sexiest men of 2013" by ''[[Cosmopolitan (magazine)|Cosmopolitan]]''.<ref>{{cite journal |title=The sexiest men of 2013 |url=http://www.cosmopolitan.com/uk/entertainment/centrefolds/g3161/cosmopolitan-sexiest-men-of-2013/?slide=31 |journal=Cosmopolitan |date=25 November 2013 |archive-date=20 May 2017 |access-date=5 June 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170520114735/http://www.cosmopolitan.com/uk/entertainment/centrefolds/g3161/cosmopolitan-sexiest-men-of-2013/?slide=31 |url-status=dead }}</ref> In 2017, Carlsen made a special guest appearance on ''[[The Simpsons]]'' in [[The Cad and the Hat|an episode]] where Homer's chess history is revealed.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.chess.com/news/view/magnus-mentors-homer-simpson-3596|publisher=Chess.com|first=Mike|last=Klein|date =20 February 2017|title =Magnus Carlsen Mentors Homer Simpson}}</ref> In 2020, Carlsen announced that he had signed a two-year sponsorship deal with gambling company [[Unibet]] to act as a "global ambassador".<ref>{{cite web|url =https://en.chessbase.com/post/magnus-carlsen-global-ambassador-unibet|title =Carlsen named "Global Ambassador" for Unibet|first =Johannes|last =Fischer|website =ChessBase|date =31 January 2020}}</ref> Unibet parent company [[Kindred Group]] is also a sponsor of Offerspill Chess Club. Offerspill was founded by Carlsen in 2019 after the Norwegian Chess Federation turned down Kindred's sponsorship offer. It is now Norway's largest chess club; Carlsen is its current chairman.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://english.offerspill.com/|title=Registration | Offerspill Sjakklubb|website=English.offerspill.com|access-date=7 November 2021}}</ref> In April 2022, the Kindred Group (through Unibet) extended its partnership with Carlsen for another two years, and also extended its sponsorship of Offerspill.<ref>{{cite web| url = https://finance.yahoo.com/news/unibet-extends-global-sponsorship-five-071500739.html| title = Unibet extends global sponsorship with five-time chess World Champion Magnus Carlsen| date = 1 April 2022| website = finance.yahoo.com| publisher = [[Yahoo!]]| access-date = 26 July 2022}}</ref>
In December 2024, Carlsen became the official chess ambassador for the [[Esports World Cup]], an esports tournament series that takes place in [[Saudi Arabia]], as part of the introduction of chess in the 2025 edition.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Šimić |first1=Ivan |date=18 December 2024 |title=Chess joins Esports World Cup 2025, Magnus Carlsen appointed as ambassador |url=https://esportsinsider.com/2024/12/chess-esports-world-cup-2025-magnus-carlsen |access-date=10 January 2025 |work=Esports Insider}}</ref>
===Play Magnus=== {{main|Play Magnus Group}}
In October 2013, Carlsen co-founded a company, [[Play Magnus AS]], with Espen Agdestein and Anders Brandt.<ref>{{cite web|url =https://www.forbes.com/sites/davidnikel/2020/10/09/norways-chess-champion-floats-tech-company-raises-30-million/|title =World Chess Champion Magnus Carlsen Floats Tech Company, Raises $30 Million|first =David|last =Nikel|website =Forbes|date =9 October 2020}}</ref> Based in Oslo, Norway, Play Magnus' first product was an [[iOS]] app, called ''[[Play Magnus (mobile app)|Play Magnus]]'', that allows the user to play a [[chess engine]] created using a database of thousands of Carlsen's recorded games from the age of five and up. Carlsen stated he wished for the app to encourage more people to play chess.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/finance/newsbysector/mediatechnologyandtelecoms/digital-media/10661306/World-chess-champion-Magnus-Carlsen-launches-app.html |archive-url=https://ghostarchive.org/archive/20220110/https://www.telegraph.co.uk/finance/newsbysector/mediatechnologyandtelecoms/digital-media/10661306/World-chess-champion-Magnus-Carlsen-launches-app.html |archive-date=10 January 2022 |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |title=World chess champion Magnus Carlsen launches app |work=The Telegraph |date=25 February 2014 |access-date=13 March 2014}}{{cbignore}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://archive.boston.com/bostonglobe/ideas/brainiac/2014/03/you_could_beat.html|title=Playing a young Magnus Carlsen at chess|first=Kevin|last=Hartnett|website=Boston Globe|date=19 March 2014}}</ref>
In March 2019, Play Magnus AS merged with [[chess24]].com, consolidating into the Play Magnus Group.<ref>{{cite web|title=chess24 and Play Magnus join forces|url=https://chess24.com/en/read/news/chess24-and-play-magnus-join-forces|last=McGourty|first=Colin|date=15 March 2019|website=chess24.com|access-date=13 February 2020}}</ref> On 8 October 2020, Play Magnus Group was listed on the [[Oslo Stock Exchange]]. Magnus Chess, an entity controlled by Carlsen and his family, then owned 9.5% of Play Magnus Group.<ref>{{cite web|url =https://www.fide.com/news/764|title =Play Magnus listed on Oslo Stock Exchange|website =FIDE|date =8 October 2020}}</ref>
In August 2022, [[Chess.com]] finalised an offer of acquisition for Play Magnus Group, which officially closed on 16 December 2022. As part of the acquisition, Carlsen signed as a brand ambassador for Chess.com.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Team (CHESScom) |first=Chess com |title=Chess.com Makes Offer To Play Magnus Group |url=https://www.chess.com/news/view/chesscom-playmagnus |access-date=24 August 2022 |website=Chess.com |language=en-US}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Play Magnus Group receives Chess.com offer |url=https://chess24.com/en/read/news/play-magnus-group-receives-chess-com-offer |access-date=24 August 2022 |website=chess24.com |language=en}}</ref><ref name="Acquisition">{{cite web |title=Chess.com Officially Acquires Play Magnus, Carlsen Signs As Ambassador |url=https://www.chess.com/news/view/chesscom-acquires-pmg |website=Chess.com |access-date=23 December 2022 |date=21 December 2022}}</ref>
=== Take Take Take === In October 2024, Magnus Carlsen cofounded Take Take Take. It was the first app in Chess World which offered text-based commentary to fantasy chess, from live games to post-game recaps, and from challenges to some app exclusive content.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-11-14 |title=Take Take Take: The ultimate chess experience is just a download away - ChessBase India |url=https://www.chessbase.in/news/Take-Take-Take |access-date=2025-01-17 |website=www.chessbase.in}}</ref> Take Take Take then partnered with [[Chess.com]] to jointly organise [[Champions Chess Tour 2024|Champions Chess Tour Finals]] in December 2024.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Team (CHESScom) |first=Chess com |date=2024-12-18 |title=Chess.com, Take Take Take Champions Chess Tour |url=https://www.chess.com/news/view/chesscom-carlsen-partner-cct-finals |access-date=2025-01-17 |website=Chess.com |language=en-US}}</ref>
== Personal life == As of 2016, Carlsen identifies as a [[social democrat]] and mostly follows a [[vegetarian]] diet; two of his sisters are vegetarians.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.ft.com/content/cbb22f4c-858e-11e6-8897-2359a58ac7a5 |archive-url=https://ghostarchive.org/archive/20221210/https://www.ft.com/content/cbb22f4c-858e-11e6-8897-2359a58ac7a5 |archive-date=10 December 2022 |url-access=subscription |url-status=live|title=Inside the home (and mind) of world chess champion Magnus Carlsen|newspaper=Financial Times|date=5 October 2016|access-date=8 October 2016|last1=Bose|first1=Mihir}}</ref>
Carlsen is an avid [[association football|football]] fan, with [[Real Madrid CF]] as his favourite club. In recognition of becoming world chess champion, he took the honorary kick-off in a [[La Liga]] game between Real Madrid and [[Real Valladolid]] on 30 November 2013.<ref>{{cite web|url =https://www.realmadrid.com/en/news/2013/11/magnus-carlsen-took-the-honorary-kick-off-for-the-real-madrid-valladolid-match|title =Magnus Carlsen took the honorary kick off for the Real Madrid-Valladolid match|website =Real Madrid|date =30 November 2013}}</ref> Carlsen also follows the [[Premier League]] and plays [[Fantasy football (association)|fantasy football]]. In December 2019, he reached the {{Numero|1}} spot on a [[Fantasy Premier League]] game, ahead of seven million other players, before eventually finishing the season in 10th place.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2019/dec/16/chess-champion-magnus-carlsen-top-of-world-fantasy-football-rankings-premier-league|title=Chess champion Magnus Carlsen moves to top of world fantasy football rankings|last=sport|first=Guardian|date=16 December 2019|work=The Guardian|access-date=16 December 2019|language=en-GB|issn=0261-3077}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/football/premier-league/fantasy-premier-league-rankings-magnus-carlsen-chess-champion-fpl-a9248731.html|title=World chess champion hits No 1 in world for Fantasy Premier League|date=16 December 2019|website=The Independent|language=en|access-date=16 December 2019}}</ref>
Carlsen has been in a relationship with Ella Victoria Malone since at least February 2024. In early January 2025, it was announced that they would be getting married during the first weekend of the month in a private ceremony.<ref>{{cite web | url = https://www.nrk.no/sport/magnus-carlsen-skal-gifte-seg-med-ella-victoria-malone-1.17166702 | title = Magnus Carlsen skal gifte seg med Ella Victoria Malone | trans-title = Magnus Carlsen will marry Ella Victoria Malone | work = [[NRK]] | date = 2025-01-02 | access-date = 2025-01-02 | language = nb | first1 = Simon Zetlitz | last1 = Nessler | first2 = Astri Chatura | last2 = Øverdal }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.chess.com/news/view/magnus-carlsen-set-to-tie-the-knot-this-weekend|title=Magnus Carlsen Set To Marry Ella Victoria Malone After World Blitz Gold Medal|date=2 January 2025 |publisher=chess.com|accessdate=2 January 2025}}</ref> The wedding ceremony took place at [[Holmenkollen Chapel]] in the [[Vestre Aker]] borough of [[Oslo]], Norway, on 4 January 2025.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.onmanorama.com/sports/other-sports/2025/01/04/magnus-carlsen-gets-married-ella-victoria-malone.html|title=Magnus Carlsen, Ella Malone get married in Oslo|publisher=onmanorama.com|accessdate=4 January 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.chess.com/news/view/chess-king-magnus-carlsen-marries-his-queen-ella-victoria|title=Chess King Magnus Carlsen Marries His Queen Ella Victoria|date=4 January 2025 |publisher=chess.com|accessdate=4 January 2025}}</ref>
In May 2025, Carlsen announced that he and Ella are expecting their first child.<ref>{{Cite news |title=Magnus Carlsen and his wife, Ella Victoria, have announced that they are expecting their first child |url=https://www.chess.com/news/view/magnus-carlsen-and-wife-ella-victoria-announces-pregnancy}}</ref> On 27 September, Ella gave birth to a boy.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-09-30 |title=Magnus Carlsen Becomes a Father for the First Time |url=https://www.telecomasia.net/news/other/magnus-carlsen-becomes-a-father-for-the-first-time/ |access-date=2025-09-30 |website=www.telecomasia.net |language=en-GB}}</ref>
== Books and films == * Grandmaster Zenon Franco (2020). ''Magnus Wins With White''. [Limited Liability Company Elk and Ruby Publishing House]. {{ISBN|978-5604-17701-3}}. * Grandmaster Zenon Franco (2020). ''Magnus Wins With Black''. Directed by [[Øyvind Asbjørnsen]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0481603/ |title=The Prince of Chess |publisher=[[IMDb]] }}</ref> * Opedal, Hallgeir (2011). ''Smarte trekk. Magnus Carlsen: Verdens beste sjakkspiller [Smart Moves. Magnus Carlsen: The World's Best Chess Player]''. Kagge. {{ISBN|978-82-489-1050-3}}. * Mikhalchishin, Adrian; Stetsko, Oleg. (2012). ''Fighting Chess with Magnus Carlsen (Progress in Chess)''. Edition Olms. {{ISBN|978-3-283-01020-1}}. * Crouch, Colin (2013). ''Magnus Force: How Carlsen Beat Kasparov's Record''. Everyman Chess. {{ISBN|978-1-78194-133-1}}. * Sivertsen, Aage G. (2015). ''Magnus'' [[Kagge Forlag]]. {{ISBN|978-82-489-1659-8}}. * Kotronias, Vassilios & Logothetis, Sotiris (2013). ''Carlsen's assault on the throne''. Quality Chess. {{ISBN|978-1-906552-22-0}}. * Butler, Brin-Jonathan (2018). ''The Grandmaster: Magnus Carlsen and the Match That Made Chess Great Again''. {{ISBN|978-1-501172601}}. * ''[[Magnus (2016 film)|Magnus]]'' (2016). Directed by [[Benjamin Ree]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.imdb.com/title/tt5471480/ |title=Magnus |publisher=[[IMDb]] }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://en.chessbase.com/post/magnus-the-chess-movie|title=Magnus – the chess movie|date=29 March 2016|website=Chess News|access-date=1 May 2016}}</ref>

revision.text:
{{Short description|2014 aircraft disappearance}}
{{EngvarB|date=July 2020}} {{Use dmy dates|date=January 2025}} <!-- All mentioned dates in the article shall refer to the date registered at the local time of the event. --> {{Infobox aircraft occurrence | name = Malaysia Airlines Flight 370 | image = MAS_plane.jpg<!-- Image has been chosen through discussion on the talkpage, if you wish to change the image please mention it on the talkpage first. --> | alt = 9M-MRO, a Malaysia Airlines B777-200ER livery just after lifting off the runway | caption = 9M-MRO, the aircraft involved in the disappearance, pictured in 2013 | occurrence_type = Disappearance <!--CURRENTLY, THE AIRCRAFT IS MISSING. DO NOT CHANGE OR EDIT THE OCCURRENCE UNLESS A CAUSE IS CONFIRMED BY INVESTIGATORS--> | date = {{start date|2014|03|08|df=y}} | summary = Inconclusive, some debris found<!-- SEE TALK PAGE FOR CURRENT CONSENSUS AND DO NOT EDIT THIS WITHOUT FORMING CONSENSUS ON THE TALK PAGE FIRST --> | site = [[Indian Ocean]], most likely southern<!-- Please do NOT change or add coordinates unless the accident site is confirmed, as it is all speculation at this point. See TALK PAGE.--> | coordinates = <!-- Please do NOT add coordinates unless the accident site is confirmed, as it is all speculation at this point. See TALK PAGE.--> | aircraft_type = [[Boeing 777#777-200ER|Boeing 777-2H6ER]]{{efn|The aircraft was a Boeing 777-200 (Extended Range "ER") model; Boeing [[List of Boeing customer codes|assigns a unique code]] for each company that buys one of its aircraft, which is applied as an [[infix]] to the model number at the time the aircraft is built, hence "777-2H6(ER)" designates a B777-200ER built for Malaysia Airlines (customer code H6).}}<!-- The aircraft model is Boeing 777-200ER. The customer code used at Boeing was "777-2H6ER200ER" as the model number. --> | operator = [[Malaysia Airlines]] | IATA = MH370 | ICAO = MAS370 | callsign = MALAYSIAN 370 | tail_number = 9M-MRO | origin = [[Kuala Lumpur International Airport]], [[Sepang District]], Malaysia | destination = [[Beijing Capital International Airport]], [[Beijing]], China | occupants = 239 | passengers = 227<!-- 227 is the correct number of passengers. Total on board: 239 = 227 passengers + 12 crew --> | crew = 12 | fatalities = 239 (presumed) | survivors = 0 (presumed) }} {{MH370}} '''Malaysia Airlines Flight 370''' ('''MH370/MAS370''') was an [[international passenger flight]] operated by [[Malaysia Airlines]] that disappeared from radar on 8 March 2014, while flying from [[Kuala Lumpur International Airport]] in [[Malaysia]] to its planned destination, [[Beijing Capital International Airport]] in [[China]].<ref name="USAToday_2014-03-08" /> The cause of its disappearance has not been determined. It is widely regarded as the greatest mystery in aviation history,<ref>{{cite web |title=10 aviation Mysteries of Our Time |url=https://www.caaa.com.au/10-aviation-mysteries-of-our-time/ |website=caaa.com.au |date=8 August 2022 |access-date=1 September 2024}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title='The greatest aviation mystery of all time': what really happened to flight MH370? |url=https://www.theguardian.com/tv-and-radio/2023/mar/05/flight-mh370-what-happened-mystery-netflix-documentary |website=The Guardian |date=6 March 2023 |access-date=1 September 2024 |last=Horton |first=Adrian |url-status=live |archive-date=16 March 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240316232043/https://www.theguardian.com/tv-and-radio/2023/mar/05/flight-mh370-what-happened-mystery-netflix-documentary}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |last=Kadri |first=Usama |date=10 June 2024 |title=Flight MH370: can underwater sound signals solve aviation's greatest mystery? |url=https://theconversation.com/flight-mh370-can-underwater-sound-signals-solve-aviations-greatest-mystery-230264 |access-date=10 June 2024 |website=The Conversation}}</ref> and remains the single deadliest case of [[List of missing aircraft|aircraft disappearance]].
The crew of the [[Boeing 777-200ER]], [[Aircraft registration|registered]] as 9M-MRO, last communicated with [[air traffic control]] (ATC) around 38 minutes after takeoff when the flight was over the [[South China Sea]]. The aircraft was lost from ATC's [[secondary surveillance radar]] screens minutes later but was tracked by the Malaysian military's [[primary radar]] system for another hour, deviating westward from its planned flight path, crossing the [[Malay Peninsula]] and [[Andaman Sea]]. It left radar range {{convert|200|nmi|km mi}} northwest of [[Penang Island]] in northwestern [[Peninsular Malaysia]].
With all 227 passengers and 12 crew aboard [[presumed dead]], the disappearance of Flight 370 was the [[List of accidents and incidents involving a Boeing 777|deadliest incident involving a Boeing 777]], the deadliest of 2014, and the deadliest in Malaysia Airlines' history until it was surpassed in all three regards by [[Malaysia Airlines Flight 17|Malaysia Airlines Flight 17]], which was shot down by Russian-backed forces while flying over [[Ukraine]] four months later on 17 July 2014.
The [[Search for Malaysia Airlines Flight 370|search for the missing aircraft]] became the most expensive search in the history of aviation. It focused initially on the South China Sea and Andaman Sea, before a [[Analysis of Malaysia Airlines Flight 370 satellite communications|novel analysis of the aircraft's automated communications]] with an [[Inmarsat]] satellite indicated that the plane had travelled far southward over the southern [[Indian Ocean]]. The lack of official information in the days immediately after the disappearance prompted fierce criticism from the Chinese public, particularly from relatives of the passengers, as most people on board Flight 370 were of Chinese origin. Several pieces of debris washed ashore in the western Indian Ocean during 2015 and 2016; many of these were confirmed to have originated from Flight 370.
After a three-year search across {{convert|120,000|km2|abbr=on}} of ocean failed to locate the aircraft, the [[Joint Agency Coordination Centre]] heading the operation suspended its activities in January 2017. A second search launched in January 2018 by private contractor [[Ocean Infinity]] also ended without success after six months.
Relying mostly on the analysis of data from the Inmarsat satellite with which the aircraft last communicated, the [[Australian Transport Safety Bureau]] (ATSB) initially proposed that a [[Uncontrolled decompression#Gradual decompression|hypoxia event]] was the most likely cause given the available evidence, although no consensus has been reached among investigators concerning this theory. At various stages of the investigation, possible [[Aircraft hijacking|hijacking]] scenarios were considered, including crew involvement, and suspicion of the airplane's cargo manifest; many [[Malaysia Airlines Flight 370 disappearance theories|disappearance theories regarding the flight]] have also been reported by the media.
The Malaysian [[Ministry of Transport (Malaysia)|Ministry of Transport]]'s final report from July 2018 was inconclusive. It highlighted Malaysian ATC's fruitless attempts to communicate with the aircraft shortly after its disappearance. In the absence of a definitive cause of disappearance, air transport industry safety recommendations and regulations citing Flight 370 have been implemented to prevent a repetition of the circumstances associated with the loss. These include increased battery life on [[underwater locator beacon]]s, lengthening of recording times on [[flight recorder|flight data recorders and cockpit voice recorders]], and new standards for aircraft position reporting over open ocean. Malaysia had supported 58% of the total cost of the underwater search, Australia 32%, and China 10%.
== Timeline == {{main|Timeline of Malaysia Airlines Flight 370}} [[File:MH370 scheduled flight map with labels.png|thumb|upright=1.8<!-- large enough to be readable on an average computer screen...per MOS:IMAGESYNTAX "Images containing important detail (for example, a map, diagram, or chart) may need larger sizes than usual to make them readable." -->|Map showing the scheduled destination of the flight and the wider surrounding region]] Flight 370 last made voice contact with ATC at 01:19 [[Time in Malaysia|MYT]], 8 March (17:19 [[Coordinated Universal Time|UTC]], 7 March) when it was over the South China Sea, less than an hour after takeoff. It disappeared from ATC radar screens at 01:22 MYT, but was still tracked on military radar as it turned sharply away from its original northeastern course to head west and cross the Malay Peninsula, continuing that course until leaving the range of the military radar at 02:22 while over the Andaman Sea, {{convert|200|nmi|km mi|abbr=}} northwest of [[Penang Island]], in northwestern Malaysia.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Flimsy 1 MH370 Timeline.pdf |url=https://www.icao.int/APAC/Meetings/2015%20APSARTF3/Flimsy%201%20MH370%20Timeline.pdf |access-date=23 February 2025 |website=www.icao.int}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Malaysia Airlines flight vanishes with more than 200 people aboard: March 8, 2014 |url=https://www.history.com/this-day-in-history/malaysia-airlines-flight-vanishes-with-more-than-200-people-aboard |access-date=2025-02-23 |website=HISTORY |date=4 August 2014 |language=en}}</ref>
[[Search for Malaysia Airlines Flight 370|The multinational search effort]] for the aircraft, which was to become the most expensive aviation search in history,<ref name="BBC new search 26 June 14">{{cite news |title=New missing Malaysian plane MH370 search area announced |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-28031741 |access-date=15 November 2014 |work=[[BBC News]] |date=26 June 2014 |quote=The search for the missing airliner is already among most expensive in aviation history. |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140708021345/https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-28031741 |archive-date=8 July 2014 |url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="Reuters search most expensive">{{cite news|title=Search for MH370 to be most expensive in aviation history|url=https://www.reuters.com/article/us-malaysia-airlines-costs-idUSBREA3709520140408|last=Wardell|first=Jane|location=Sydney|access-date=15 November 2014|publisher=[[Reuters]]|date=8 April 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140414120207/https://www.reuters.com/article/2014/04/08/us-malaysia-airlines-costs-idUSBREA3709520140408|archive-date=14 April 2014|url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="telegraph1" /> began in the [[Gulf of Thailand]] and the South China Sea,<ref name="SciSharp">{{cite web |last=Sharp |first=Tim |title=Facts About Malaysia Flight 370: Passengers, Crew & Aircraft |url=https://www.livescience.com/44248-facts-about-flight-370-passengers-crew-aircraft.html |website=livescience.com |publisher=Future plc |date=17 January 2017 |access-date=20 August 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170508034843/https://www.livescience.com/44248-facts-about-flight-370-passengers-crew-aircraft.html|archive-date=8 May 2017 |url-status=live}}</ref> where the aircraft's signal was last detected on [[secondary surveillance radar]], and was soon extended to the [[Strait of Malacca]] and Andaman Sea. [[Analysis of Malaysia Airlines Flight 370 satellite communications|Analysis of satellite communications]] between the aircraft and Inmarsat's satellite communications network concluded that the flight continued until at least 08:19 (nearly an hour after Malaysia Airlines publicly announced the plane's loss) and flew south into the southern Indian Ocean, although the precise location cannot be determined. Australia assumed charge of the search on 17 March, when the search effort began to emphasise the southern Indian Ocean. On 24 March, the Malaysian government noted that the final location determined by the satellite communication was far from any possible landing sites, and concluded, "Flight MH370 ended in the southern Indian Ocean."<ref name="BBC_2014-03-24"/>
From October 2016 to January 2017, a comprehensive survey of {{convert|120,000|km2|mi2|abbr=on}} of sea floor about {{convert|1,800|km|mi nmi|abbr=on}}<!-- first occurrence of "kilometre" in article, so shouldn't be abbreviated, except everyone knows what km is --> southwest of [[Perth]], Western Australia, yielded no evidence of the aircraft. Several pieces of [[marine debris]] found on the coast of Africa and on Indian Ocean islands off the coast of Africa—the first of which was discovered on 29 July 2015 on [[Réunion]]—have been confirmed as pieces of Flight 370.<ref name=bbc2/><ref name="mh370.gov.my"/><ref name="MSN debris ID"/><ref name="ATSB Mozambique debris id"/> The bulk of the aircraft has not been located, prompting many theories about its disappearance.
In January 2018, a search by private US marine exploration company [[Ocean Infinity]] began in the search zone around {{coord|-35.6|92.8|type:landmark_scale:10000000|name=CSIRO crash area}}, the most likely crash site according to the drift study published in 2017.<ref name="star23">{{cite news |url=https://www.thestar.com.my/news/world/2018/01/23/ship-hired-to-find-mh370-arrives-in-search-zone/ |title=Ship hired to find MH370 arrives in search zone |date=23 January 2018 |newspaper=[[The Star (Malaysia)|The Star]] |access-date=23 January 2018 |agency=Reuters |location=Sydney |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180612141402/https://www.thestar.com.my/news/world/2018/01/23/ship-hired-to-find-mh370-arrives-in-search-zone/ |archive-date=12 June 2018 |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2018/01/326400/norwegian-vessel-arrive-mh370-search-area-weekend |title=Norwegian vessel to arrive at MH370 search area this weekend |date=18 January 2018 |newspaper=[[New Straits Times]] |access-date=19 January 2018 |last=Shah |first=Aliza |location=Kuala Lumpur |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180207010703/https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2018/01/326400/norwegian-vessel-arrive-mh370-search-area-weekend |archive-date=7 February 2018 |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.channelnewsasia.com/news/asiapacific/malaysia-says-search-resumes-for-missing-flight-mh370-9887742|title=Malaysia says search resumes for missing flight MH370|work=[[Channel NewsAsia]]|date=23 January 2018|archive-date=24 January 2018|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180124070815/https://www.channelnewsasia.com/news/asiapacific/malaysia-says-search-resumes-for-missing-flight-mh370-9887742}}</ref> In a previous search attempt, Malaysia had established a [[Joint Investigation Team]] (JIT) to investigate the incident, working with foreign aviation authorities and experts. Malaysia released a final report concerning Flight 370 on 17 October 2017.<ref>{{cite web |last=Brumfiel |first=Geoff |title=Final Report On MH370 Says Failure To Locate Airliner Is 'Almost Inconceivable' |url=https://www.npr.org/sections/thetwo-way/2017/10/03/555330250/final-report-on-mh370-says-failure-to-locate-airliner-is-almost-inconceivable |publisher=NPR |date=3 October 2017 |access-date=31 March 2023 |archive-date=3 April 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230403190342/https://www.npr.org/sections/thetwo-way/2017/10/03/555330250/final-report-on-mh370-says-failure-to-locate-airliner-is-almost-inconceivable |url-status=live }}</ref> Neither the crew nor the aircraft's communication systems relayed a distress signal, indications of bad weather, or technical problems before the aircraft vanished.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-03-06 |title=MH370 disappeared a decade ago. Here's what we know about one of aviation's biggest mysteries |url=https://apnews.com/article/malaysia-mh370-missing-plane-what-to-know-91b69d63a6bc57d6ad2bf3c1eaf6e4ed |access-date=2025-02-23 |website=AP News |language=en}}</ref>
Two Iranian passengers travelling on stolen passports were investigated, but eliminated as suspects. Malaysian police identified the captain as the prime suspect if human intervention was<!-- subjunctive --> the cause of the disappearance, after clearing all others on the flight of suspicion over possible motives. Power was lost to the aircraft's [[satellite data unit]] (SDU) at some point between 01:07 and 02:03; the SDU logged onto Inmarsat's satellite communication network at 02:25, which was three minutes after the aircraft had left the range of radar. Based on analysis of the satellite communications, the aircraft was postulated to have turned south after passing north of [[Sumatra]] and then to have flown for six hours with little deviation in its track, ending when its [[Fuel starvation|fuel was exhausted]].<ref>{{cite news |last1=Pasztor |first1=Andy |last2=Ostrower |first2=Jon |title=U.S. Investigators Suspect Missing Malaysia Airlines Plane Flew On for Hours |url=https://www.wsj.com/articles/SB10001424052702304914904579434653903086282 |work=[[The Wall Street Journal]] |date=13 March 2014 |access-date=8 March 2023 |archive-date=5 January 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150105005526/http://www.wsj.com/articles/SB10001424052702304914904579434653903086282 |url-status=live |url-access=subscription}}</ref>
With the loss of all 239 lives on board, Flight 370 is the second-deadliest incident involving a Boeing 777 and the second-deadliest incident of Malaysia Airlines' history, second to Flight 17 in both categories. Malaysia Airlines was struggling financially, a problem that was exacerbated by a decrease of ticket sales after the disappearance of Flight 370 and the downing of Flight 17; the airline was renationalised by the end of 2014. The Malaysian government received significant criticism, especially from China, for failing to disclose information promptly during the early weeks of the search.<!-- supported by the "Aftermath" section ("Information sharing", "China" subsections), per WP:Lead only controversial statements in the lead need inline citations --> Flight 370's disappearance brought to public attention the limits of aircraft tracking and flight recorders, including the limited battery life of underwater locator beacons (an issue that had been raised about four years earlier<!-- AF447 was lost almost five years earlier, but the recommendations were made during late 2009, not quite 4.5 years earlier, so four years is more appropriate --> following the loss of [[Air France Flight 447]], but had never been resolved). In response to Flight 370's disappearance, the [[International Civil Aviation Organization]] adopted new standards for aircraft position reporting over open ocean, extended recording time for [[cockpit voice recorder]]s, and, starting from 2020, new aircraft designs have been required to have a means of recovering the [[flight recorder]]s, or the information they contain, before they sink into the water.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.chicagotribune.com/nation-world/ct-malaysia-airlines-flight-safety-20160304-story.html|title=How disappearance of Malaysia Airlines flight is changing safety standards|date=4 March 2016|newspaper=Chicago Tribune|access-date=11 March 2022|archive-date=12 March 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230312205603/https://www.chicagotribune.com/nation-world/ct-malaysia-airlines-flight-safety-20160304-story.html|url-status=live}}</ref>
== Background == {{multiple image | header = Interior photographs of 9M-MRO{{efn|from April 2004}} | align = right | direction = vertical | width = 220 | image1 = Boeing 777-2H6-ER, Malaysia Airlines AN0561319.jpg | caption1 = Cockpit | image2 = Boeing 777-2H6-ER, Malaysia Airlines AN0561322.jpg | caption2 = Economy class seats | caption_align = center }}
=== Aircraft === Flight 370 was operated with a Boeing 777-2H6ER,{{efn|The aircraft is a Boeing 777-200ER (for Extended Range) model; Boeing assigns a [[List of Boeing customer codes|unique customer code]] for each company that buys one of its aircraft, which replaces part of the model number at the time the aircraft is built. The code for Malaysia Airlines is "H6", hence "777-2H6ER".<ref name="AutoVQ-29"/>}} [[serial number]] 28420, [[Aircraft registration|registered]] as 9M-MRO. The aircraft was delivered new to Malaysia Airlines on 31 May 2002.<ref name="mh370.mot.gov.my" />{{Rp|39}} The aircraft was powered by two [[Rolls-Royce Trent 800|Rolls-Royce Trent 892]] engines and configured to carry 282 passengers in total capacity.<ref name="AutoVQ-30"/><ref name="mh370.mot.gov.my" />{{Rp|39}} It had accumulated 53,471.6 hours and 7,526 cycles (takeoffs and landings) in service<ref name="Interim report-March 2015" />{{Rp|22}} and had not previously been involved in any major incidents,<ref name=20140308flightglobal/> though a minor incident while taxiing at [[Shanghai Pudong International Airport]] in August 2012 resulted in a broken [[wing tip]].<ref name="20120626news"/><ref name="20120809aviation-safety"/> Its last [[Aircraft maintenance checks|maintenance "A check"]] was carried out on 23 February 2014.<ref name=Flight396801/> The aircraft was in compliance with all applicable Airworthiness Directives for the airframe and engines. A replenishment of the crew member oxygen system was performed on 7 March 2014, a routine maintenance task; an examination of this procedure found nothing unusual.<ref name="Interim report-March 2015" />{{Rp|27}} Ten years after MH370's disappearance, however, leaked documents have shown that MH370 was given supplemental fuel and crew member oxygen supplies just before takeoff.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Dunne |first=John |date=8 March 2024 |title=MH370 was given extra fuel and oxygen before take off in new twist to missing jet riddle |url=https://www.standard.co.uk/news/world/mh370-missing-flight-fuel-oxygen-b1144129.html |access-date=9 April 2024 |website=Evening Standard |url-status=live |archive-date=18 March 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240318180342/https://www.standard.co.uk/news/world/mh370-missing-flight-fuel-oxygen-b1144129.html}}</ref>
The Boeing 777 was introduced in 1994 and has an excellent safety record.<ref name=BI777>{{cite web |url=https://www.businessinsider.com/boeing-777-one-of-safest-planes-in-history-emirates-crash-2016-8?r=US&IR=T |title=A Boeing 777 just crashed, but it's still one of the safest planes ever to fly |first=Benjamin |last=Zhang |date=3 August 2016 |website=[[Business Insider]] |access-date=30 May 2020 |archive-date=3 August 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200803153823/https://www.businessinsider.com/boeing-777-one-of-safest-planes-in-history-emirates-crash-2016-8?r=US&IR=T |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.forbes.com/sites/geoffwhitmore/2019/08/16/what-is-the-safest-airplane-to-fly/#5fdb47c65c72 |title=What Is The Safest Airplane To Fly? |date=16 August 2019 |first=Geoff |last=Whitmore |work=[[Forbes]] |access-date=30 May 2020 |archive-date=1 February 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210201155103/https://www.forbes.com/sites/geoffwhitmore/2019/08/16/what-is-the-safest-airplane-to-fly/#5fdb47c65c72 |url-status=live }}</ref> Since its first commercial flight in June 1995, the type has suffered only [[List of accidents and incidents involving a Boeing 777|seven other hull losses]]:<!--in over 5 million flights CHECK & CITE--> # the crash of [[British Airways Flight 38]] in 2008; # a cockpit fire in a parked [[EgyptAir Flight 667]] at [[Cairo International Airport]] in 2011;<ref name=20121130flightglobal/><ref name="20110729avherald"/> # the crash of [[Asiana Airlines Flight 214]] in 2013, in which three people died; # [[Malaysia Airlines Flight 17]], which was shot down over [[Ukraine]], killing all 298 people aboard in July 2014;<ref name="CNN MH370-MH17"/><ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.cbc.ca/news/world/malaysia-airlines-flight-mh17-reportedly-shot-down-near-ukraine-russia-border-1.2709881|title=Malaysia Airlines Flight MH17 reportedly shot down near Ukraine-Russia border|publisher=Canadian Broadcasting Corporation|location=Canada|work=CBC News|date=17 July 2014|access-date=19 July 2014|archive-date=11 November 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201111221850/https://www.cbc.ca/news/world/malaysia-airlines-flight-mh17-reportedly-shot-down-near-ukraine-russia-border-1.2709881|url-status=live}}</ref> # [[Emirates Flight 521]], which crashed and burned out while landing at [[Dubai International Airport]] in August 2016 killing one person;<ref name="DC201608">{{cite news|title=Emirates flight from Trivandrum crash-lands in Dubai, passengers safe|url=https://www.deccanchronicle.com/world/asia/030816/emirates-flight-from-trivandrum-to-dubai-crash-lands-passengers-safe.html|access-date=3 August 2016|newspaper=Deccan Chronicle|date=3 August 2016|archive-date=17 September 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200917054413/http://www.deccanchronicle.com/world/asia/030816/emirates-flight-from-trivandrum-to-dubai-crash-lands-passengers-safe.html|url-status=live}}</ref> # and in November 2017, the seventh Boeing 777 hull loss occurred when a [[Singapore Airlines]] 777-200ER was written off after catching fire and burning out at [[Singapore Changi Airport]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://aviation-safety.net/database/record.php?id=20171129-0 |title=ASN Aircraft accident Boeing 777-212ER 9V-SQK Singapore-Changi International Airport (SIN) |website=[[Aviation Safety Network|aviation-safety.net]] |access-date=30 May 2020 |archive-date=22 October 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201022155801/https://aviation-safety.net/database/record.php?id=20171129-0 |url-status=live }}</ref>
=== Passengers and crew === {| class="wikitable sortable plainrowheaders floatright" style="font-size:85%; text-align:right; margin:0 0 1.5em 1.5em;" |+ |- ! scope="col" | Nationality<ref name=20140308malaysiaairlines/> ! scope="col" data-sort-type="number"| Count |- ! scope="row" | Australia | 6 |- ! scope="row" | Canada | 2 |- ! scope="row" | China | 153{{efn|Including one Hong Kong resident.<ref name="AutoVQ-31"/>}} |- ! scope="row" | France | 4 |- ! scope="row" | India<ref>{{cite web |last=Singh |first=Ranjit |date=12 March 2014 |title=MH370: Five Indian nationals identified |url=http://english.astroawani.com/news/show/mh370-five-indian-nationals-identified-31687 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141012212539/http://english.astroawani.com/news/show/mh370-five-indian-nationals-identified-31687 |archive-date=12 October 2014 |access-date=29 May 2014 |publisher=astro AWANI}}</ref> | 5 |- ! scope="row" | Indonesia | 7 |- ! scope="row" | Iran | 2{{efn|The manifest initially released by Malaysia Airlines listed an Austrian and an Italian. These were subsequently identified as two Iranian nationals who boarded Flight 370 using stolen passports.<ref name="BBC_2014-03-11_a"/>}} |- ! scope="row" | Malaysia | 50{{efn|38 passengers and 12 crew.}} |- ! scope="row" | Netherlands | 1 |- ! scope="row" | New Zealand | 2 |- ! scope="row" | Russia | 1 |- ! scope="row" | Taiwan<!-- DO NOT MERGE TAIWANESE PASSENGER WITH PEOPLE'S REPUBLIC OF CHINA. REFERENCED SOURCES PROVIDE SEPARATE LISTING FOR PASSENGER FROM TAIWAN ("18, CHUANG/HSIULINGMS, TWN, 45"). SEE TALK PAGE FOR MORE ON THIS SETTLED ISSUE. --> | 1 |- ! scope="row" | Ukraine | 2 |- ! scope="row" | United States | 3 |- class="sortbottom" !'''Total (14 nationalities)'''|| style="text-align:center;" |'''239''' |- class="sortbottom" |} The aircraft was carrying 12 Malaysian crew members and 227 passengers from 14 different nations.<ref name="BBC who was on board">{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-asia-26503469|title=Missing Malaysia plane: The passengers on board MH370|date=17 January 2017|work=BBC News|access-date=24 January 2019|archive-date=24 January 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190124170939/https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-asia-26503469|url-status=live}}</ref> On the day of the disappearance, Malaysia Airlines released the names and nationalities of the passengers and crew, based on the [[Manifest (transportation)|flight manifest]].<ref name=20140308malaysiaairlines/> The passenger list was later modified to include two Iranian passengers travelling on stolen [[Austrian passport|Austrian]] and [[Italian passport]]s.<ref name="BBC_2014-03-11_a"/><!-- Original list had 1 Austrian & 1 Italian (15 nations), but these turned out to be 2 Iranians (reduced to 14 nations) -->
=== Crew === All 12 crew members—two pilots and 10 cabin crew—were Malaysian citizens.<ref name=20140308malaysiaairlines/> * The [[pilot in command]] was 52-year-old Captain Zaharie Ahmad Shah from [[Penang]].<ref>{{cite news |last2=Fernandez |first2=Celine |last1=Wong |first1=Chun Han |title=Pilot Zaharie, of Missing Malaysia Airlines Plane, a Passionate Aviator |url=https://www.wsj.com/articles/SB10001424052702304020104579431214234709946 |access-date=16 August 2024 |work=The Wall Street Journal |date=10 March 2014 |url-status=live |archive-date=16 August 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240816144720/https://www.wsj.com/articles/SB10001424052702304020104579431214234709946 |url-access=subscription |quote=Those Who Know Capt. Zaharie Ahmad Shah Recall an Amiable Geek Who Loves to Fly}}</ref> He joined Malaysia Airlines as a cadet pilot in 1981, and after training and receiving his commercial pilot's license, he became a [[Second officer (aeronautics)|second officer]] with the airline in 1983. He was promoted to captain of the [[Boeing 737-400]] in 1991, captain of the [[Airbus A330-300]] in 1996, and captain of the [[Boeing 777-200]] in 1998. He had been a type-rating instructor and a type-rating examiner since 2007. Zaharie had a total of 18,365 hours of flying experience.<ref name="Interim report-March 2015" />{{Rp|13}}<ref name="20140308straitstimes"/><ref name="20140309in"/> * The [[First officer (aeronautics)|co-pilot]] was 26-year-old First Officer Fariq Abdul Hamid. He joined Malaysia Airlines as a cadet pilot in 2007; after becoming a second officer of the Boeing 737-400, he was promoted to first officer of the Boeing 737-400 in 2010 and then transitioned to the Airbus A330-300 in 2012. In November 2013, he began training as first officer of the Boeing 777-200 aircraft. Flight 370 was his final training flight, and he was scheduled to be examined on his next flight. Fariq had accumulated 2,763 hours of flying experience.<ref name="Interim report-March 2015" />{{Rp|14}}<ref name="20140308nst"/><ref name="cnn-firstoff"/>
=== Passengers === Of the 227 passengers, 153 were Chinese citizens,<ref name="BBC who was on board" /> including a group of 19 artists with six family members and four staff returning from a calligraphy exhibition of their work in [[Kuala Lumpur]]; 38 passengers were Malaysian.
